A JSTree version of my Perl.Wiki

CPAN::MetaCurator V 1.27. 2026-07-23T17:25:03

My home page, for downloading Perl.Wiki.html

Notes:
⊕ Nodes just under the root ('MetaCurator') are called Topics. Paragraphs is the TiddlyWiki equivalent.
⊕ Tokens like [[XS]] indicate a topic (a TiddlyWiki paragraph name), not a Perl module.
⊕ Almost all leaf node are links. Use a middle-button click to open the link in another tab.
⊕ The code does not yet cleanly handle special cases such as the FAQ or pre-formatted paras.
⊕ Also, cases where the para is just a 'See also' are not yet clean.

⊕ My other module, CPAN::MetaPackages, builds & ships an SQLite db containing 02packages.details.txt.
⊕ Most recent version of 02packages.details.txt used: Last-Updated: Sun, 14 Jun 2026 23:17:02 GMT .
⊕ Acronyms: 271. CPAN modules: 265554. Wiki modules: 6803. Topics: 214. Unclassified: 5354.

  • MetaCurator

        • Serialization of data to ram/disk
      • App::CSVUtils - CLI utilities related to CSV
      • Notes for App::CSVUtils!
        • The module contains 64 scripts
      • App::SQLiteUtils - Utilities related to SQLite
      • CPAN::Maker::Bootstrapper - Scaffold a new CPAN distribution in one command
      • Notes for CPAN::Maker::Bootstrapper!
        • Contains CPAN::Maker::ConfigReader etc
      • Cron::Toolkit - Quartz-compatible cron parser with unique extensions and over 400 tests
      • Crypt::PBKDF2 - The PBKDF2 password hashing algorithm.
      • Notes for Crypt::PBKDF2!
        • See also PBKDF2::Tiny
      • CXC::Exporter::Util - Tagged Based Exporting
      • Data::KSUID - KSUID are a globally unique identifier similar to UUIDs but designed to be naturally sorted by generation timestamp and to have representations that are easily postable across systems
      • Data::OptList::Object - Data::OptList, but object-oriented
      • Data::Path::XS - Fast path-based access to nested data structures
      • DBD::SQLite - Self-contained RDBMS in a DBI Driver
      • DBD::SQLite::Constants - Common SQLite constants
      • DBIx::AutoUpgrade::NativeStrings - Automatically upgrade Perl native strings to utf8 before sending them to the database
      • Notes for DBIx::AutoUpgrade::NativeStrings!
        • Primarily for DBD::Oracle, DPD::Pg and DBD::SQLite
      • Destructure::Declare - Lexically scoped structural destructuring let
      • Dev::Util - Base modules for Perl Development
      • Devel::Bug - Transparent inline debugging probe (pure Perl)
      • e - Beast mode unleashed
      • Email::Stuffer - A more casual approach to creating and sending Email:: emails
      • File::Sticker - Read, Write file meta-data
      • Notes for File::Sticker!
        • Includes sticker which sticks tags onto files, populates a database with file meta-data
        • See also Data::TagDB in DatabaseAndSQL1
      • ixchel - Automate various sys admin stuff
      • Notes for ixchel!
        • Contains about 50 modules
      • List::Util::Find - List utilities related to finding items
      • Locale::Codes - A distribution of many modules to handle locale codes
      • Melian - Perl client to the Melian cache
      • Notes for Melian!
        • More details in CachingStuff
      • Net::Gotify - Gotify client for Perl
      • Notes for Net::Gotify!
        • I feel this may become extremely popular
      • Net::IPData - Perl client for the ipdata.co IP geolocation and threat intelligence API
      • Net::Server - Extensible, general Perl server engine
      • Notes for Net::Server!
        • Has a massive amount of flexibility
      • Number::Finance::Human - Human-readable numbers for accounting and finance
      • Object::InsideOut - Comprehensive inside-out object support module
      • Object::InsideOut::Metadata - Introspection for Object::InsideOut classes
      • Path::Tiny - Provides methods like slurp_utf8 and spew_utf8 to read and write files using the correct encoding
      • PAX - Perl Adaptive eXecution compiler and standalone binary packager
      • PDF::Reuse - Reuse and mass produce PDF documents
      • Notes for PDF::Reuse!
        • This module has an important See Also
      • pdftotext - Poppler is a PDF rendering library based on the xpdf-3.0 code base
      • Notes for pdftotext!
        • Appears to be installed as part of Debian
      • Perl::Critic::Community - Community-inspired Perl::Critic policies
      • Perl::Critic::Policy::Community::DiscouragedModules - Various modules discouraged from use
      • Perl::Critic::Policy::Community::PreferredAlternatives - Various modules with preferred alternatives
      • Quote::Code - Quoted strings with arbitrary code interpolation
      • Random::Simple - Simple, usable, real world random numbers
      • Rose::DB - A DBI wrapper and abstraction layer
      • Rose::DB::Object - Extensible, high performance object-relational mapper (ORM)
      • Notes for Rose::DB::Object!
        • Contains Rose::DB::Object::Tutorial - A guided tour of the basics of Rose::DB::Object
        • Contains Rose::DB::Object::Loader, Rose::DB::Object::Manager, etc
        • See also https://dev.to/dragostrif/rosedb-orm-and-perl-3dig
      • Server::Starter - A superdaemon for hot-deploying server programs
      • SQL::Wizard - Composable SQL query builder with expression trees
      • String::Escape - Backslash escapes, quoted phrase, word elision, etc
      • String::ShellQuote - Quote strings for passing through the shell
      • Notes for String::ShellQuote!
        • Does not decode ANSI escape codes
      • Switch::Declare - Compile-time, lexically-scoped switch/case
      • Test::Most::Explain - Enhanced diagnostics for Test::More with structured diffs and helpful hints
      • Text::CSV::Slurp - Convert CSV into an array of hashes, or an array of hashes into CSV
      • Text::Stencil - Fast XS list/table renderer with escaping, formatting, and transform chaining
      • Text::Table::Manifold - Render tables in manifold formats
      • Notes for Text::Table::Manifold!
        • Its See Also lists about similar 40 modules
      • Text::Table::Tiny - Generate simple text tables from 2D arrays
      • Thread::Subs - Execute selected subs concurrently in worker threads
      • Notes for Thread::Subs!
        • The POD is very sophisticated
      • Time::Moment - Represents a date and time of day with an offset from UTC
      • TOML - Tom's Obvious, Minimal Language is a file format for configuration files
      • Notes for TOML!
        • Supports the following data types: string, integer, float, boolean, datetime, array, and table
      • Type::Registry - A glorified hashref for looking up type constraints
      • Type::Tiny - Tiny, yet Moo(se)-compatible, type constraint
      • Notes for Type::Tiny!
        • Contains Types::Standard and many other modules
      • Types::Common - The one stop shop
      • utf8::all - Turn on Unicode - all of it
      • Unicode::CheckUTF8 - Checks if scalar is valid UTF-8
      • Unicode::UTF8 - Encoding and decoding of UTF-8 encoding form

      • Claude::Agent - Perl SDK for the Claude Agent SDK
      • Claude::Agent::Code::Refactor - Automated code refactoring with review-fix loops
      • Claude::Agent::Code::Review - AI-powered code review using Claude
      • FacialHarmonyAI::SiteKit - URL helpers and metadata for FacialHarmonyAI
      • Langertha - The clan of fierce vikings with ðª and ð¡ï¸ to AId your rAId
      • Notes for Langertha!
        • This module is designed to facilitate interactions with various AI models
      • Langertha::Knarr - LLM Proxy with Langfuse Tracing
      • Langertha::Raider - Autonomous agent with conversation history and MCP tools
      • Notes for Langertha::Raider!
        • See App::Raider
      • Langertha::Skeid - Dynamic routing control-plane for multi-node LLM serving with normalized metrics and cost accounting
      • LLM::SimpleClient - Perl module for making single API requests to LLM providers
      • Lugh - Pure C LLM Inference Engine for Perl (built on ggml)
      • MCP - Model Context Protocol Perl SDK
      • MCP::Run - MCP server with a command execution tool
      • MCP::Server - MCP server implementation
      • MCP::Server::Run - MCP server with a command execution tool
      • MCP::Wiki - Markdown wiki MCP server with TOC extraction and git history
      • Net::Async::Crawl4AI - IO::Async Crawl4AI client with an async strategy chain
      • Net::Async::Firecrawl - IO::Async Firecrawl v2 client with flow helpers
      • Notes for Net::Async::Firecrawl!
        • See also WWW::Firecrawl below
      • Net::Gemini - A small gemini client
      • Notes for Net::Gemini!
        • See also TheRFCGuide for rfc2045
      • OpenAPI::Client::OpenAI - A client for the (Microsoft) OpenAI API
      • ProductShotAI::SiteKit - Public URL helpers for ProductShot AI
      • Sim::AgentSoar - SOAR-inspired explicit search with a pluggable LLM worker
      • Sim::Agent - Deterministic, file-backed orchestration of LLM workers and critics via a small S-expression DSL
      • WebService::Chroma - Chromadb client
      • Notes for WebService::Chroma!
        • Chroma is the AI-native open-source vector database. Chroma makes it easy to build LLM apps by making knowledge, facts, and skills pluggable for LLMs
        • https://docs.trychroma.com/docs/overview/getting-started
      • WebService::Ollama - Ollama client
      • Wordsmith::Claude - AI-powered text rewriting with style
      • WWW::Crawl4AI - Perl client and fallback orchestrator for Crawl4AI
      • WWW::Firecrawl - Firecrawl v2 API bindings (self-host first, cloud compatible)
      • Notes for WWW::Firecrawl!
        • https://www.firecrawl.dev/
        • See also Net::Async::Firecrawl above
      • WWW::VastAI - Perl client for the Vast.ai REST APIs
    • AlgorithmicStuff
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
      • Action::Retry - Module to try to perform an action, with various ways of retrying and sleeping between retries
      • Action::Retry::Strategy::Constant - Constant sleep time strategy
      • Action::Retry::Strategy::Fibonacci - Fibonacci incrementation of sleep time strategy
      • Action::Retry::Strategy::Linear - Linear incrementation of sleep time strategy
      • Algorithm::Backoff - Various backoff strategies for retry
      • Algorithm::Backoff::Constant - Backoff using a constant delay
      • Algorithm::Backoff::Exponential - Backoff exponentially
      • Algorithm::Backoff::Fibonacci - Backoff using Fibonacci sequence
      • Algorithm::Backoff::LILD - Linear Increment, Linear Decrement (LILD) backoff
      • Algorithm::Backoff::LIMD - Linear Increment, Multiplicative Decrement (LIMD) backoff
      • Algorithm::Backoff::MILD - Multiplicative Increment, Linear Decrement (MILD) backoff
      • Algorithm::Backoff::MIMD - Multiplicative Increment, Multiplicative Decrement (MIMD) backoff
      • Algorithm::Backoff::RetryTimeouts - A backoff-style retry algorithm with adjustable timeout support
      • Algorithm::Classifier::NaiveBayes - A multinomial naive Bayes text classifier with Laplace smoothing
      • Algorithm::Classifier::IsolationForest - Unsupervised anomaly detection via Isolation Forest or Extended Isolation Forest
      • Notes for Algorithm::Classifier::IsolationForest!
        • https://www.researchgate.net/publication/224384174_Isolation_Forest
      • Algorithm::EventsPerSecond - A sliding-window events-per-second rate counter with a optional XS backend
      • Algorithm::ToNumberMunger - Compile declarative specs into closures that munge raw values into numbers
      • Algorithm::X::DLX - Solve exact cover problems with Algorithm-X and Dancing Links
      • Algorithm::DLX - Solve exact cover problems
      • App::AlgorithmBackoffUtils - Utilities related to Algorithm::Backoff
      • Inline::CLIPS - Perl interface to run CLIPS programs
      • Retry::Backoff - Retry a piece of code, with backoff strategies
      • Retry::Policy - Simple retry wrapper with exponential backoff and jitter
    • AnimationStuff
      • App::FfmpegUtils - Utilities related to ffmpeg
      • Automate::Animate::FFmpeg - Create animation from a sequence of images using FFmpeg
    • AntiVirusStuff
      • Alien::YARA - Find or download and install the YARA malware analysis library
      • File::Scan::ClamAV - Connect to a local Clam Anti-Virus clamd service and send commands
      • YaraFFI - Minimal Perl FFI bindings for the YARA malware scanning engine
      • Notes for YaraFFI!
        • https://yara.readthedocs.io/en/latest/
    • ApacheStuff
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
      • Apache::Log::Parser - Parser for Apache Log (common, combined, and any other custom styles by LogFormat)
      • ApacheLog::Parser - Parse apache 'combined' log format
      • Apache::ParseLog - Object-oriented Perl extension for parsing Apache log files
      • Apache::Session - A persistence framework for session data
      • Apache::Session::Browseable - Add index and search methods to Apache::Session
      • Notes for Apache::Session::Browseable!
        • Written to support Lemonldap::NG. See SystemMonitoring
      • Apache::Session::Generate::Random - Use system randomness for generating session ids
      • Apache2::API - Apache2 API Framework
      • Avro - The official Perl API for the Apache Avro project's serialization and RPC framework
      • Clownfish - Apache Clownfish symbiotic object system
      • Clownfish::CFC - Compiler for Apache Clownfish
      • Common::Log::Parser - Parse the common log format lines used by Apache
      • Dezi::App - Build Dezi search applications
      • ModPerl::RegistryCookerSealed - Monkey-patch :Sealed registry handler() into ModPerl::RegistryCooker
      • Net::API::REST - Framework for RESTful APIs
      • Net::Kafka::Producer::Avro - Apache Kafka message producer based on librdkafka, Avro serialization and Confluent Schema Registry validation
      • Regexp::Log::Common - A regular expression parser for the Common Log Format
      • ThreatDetector - Apache log parser for threat detection & classification
      • Thrift - Apache Thrift is a software framework for scalable cross-language services development.
      • Thrift::XS - Faster Thrift binary protocol encoding and decoding

      • Apple::AppStoreConnect - Apple App Store Connect API client
      • Mac::FSEvents - Monitor a directory structure for changes
      • Mac::OSVersion - Get the Mac OS X or macOS system version
      • Mac::Pasteboard - Manipulate Mac OS X clipboards/pasteboards
      • Mac::PropertyList - Work with Mac plists at a low level
    • ArchiveFiles
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • ftp://ftp.info-zip.org/pub/infozip/doc/ - For zip file details
        • https://metacpan.org/dist/IO-Compress/view/lib/IO/Compress/FAQ.pod
        • https://msgpack.org/
        • For zip file details
        • For info re bzip2
        • For I/O operators
        • For lzf
        • AES Encryption Information
      • Alien::ultraviolet - Alien package for Python uv
      • Archive::7zip - Read/write 7z, zip, ISO9960 and other archives
      • Archive::CAR - Content Addressable Archive (CAR) reader and writer
      • Archive::Extract - A generic archive extracting mechanism
      • Archive::Lha - Read and extract .LZH/.LHA archives
      • Archive::Libarchive::Compress - Recursively archive a directory (using libarchive)
      • Notes for Archive::Libarchive::Compress!
        • Contains many modules
      • Archive::Libarchive::Peek - Peek into archives without extracting them
      • Archive::Tar::Stream - Pure Perl IO-friendly tar file management
      • Archive::SCS - SCS archive controller
      • Archive::Tar - Module for manipulations of tar archives
      • Archive::Tar::Wrapper - API wrapper around the 'tar' utility
      • Notes for Archive::Tar::Wrapper!
        • This module differs from Archive::Tar in 2 ways
      • Archive::Unzip::Burst - Featureless but fast ZIP extraction
      • Archive::Zip - Provide an interface to ZIP archive files
      • Archive::Zip::SimpleZip - Create Zip Archives
      • Notes for Archive::Zip::SimpleZip!
        • Supports deflate, store, bzip2, Zstandard (Zstd), Xz and lzma compression
        • Contains Archive::Zip::SimpleUnzip
      • Archive::Zip::SimpleUnzip - Read Zip Archives
      • Notes for Archive::Zip::SimpleUnzip!
        • Supports deflate, store, bzip2, Zstandard (Zstd), Xz and lzma compression
        • Contained in Archive::Zip::SimpleZip
      • App::zipdetails - Display the internal structure of zip files
      • Notes for App::zipdetails!
        • The zipinfo program that comes with the info-zip distribution (http://www.info-zip.org/) can also display details of the structure of a zip file
      • gb64 - Fast Pure Perl Base64 Encoding and Decoding
      • Compress::Raw::Bzip2 - Low-Level Interface to bzip2 compression library
      • Compress::Raw::Lzma - Low-Level Perl Interface to lzma compression library
      • Compress::Raw::Zlib - Low-Level Interface to zlib or zlib-ng compression library
      • Compress::Stream::Zstd - Perl interface to the Zstd (Zstandard) (de)compressor
      • Notes for Compress::Stream::Zstd!
        • Works with IO::Compress::Zstd
      • Compress::Zlib - Interface to zlib compression library
      • Compress::Zstd - Perl interface to the Zstd (Zstandard) (de)compressor
      • Notes for Compress::Zstd!
        • No dictionary support but support ticket has sample code
        • But see Compress::Stream::Zstd
      • Compression::Util - Implementation of various techniques used in data compression
      • File::Unpack2 - A strong bz2/gz/zip/tar/cpio/rpm/deb/cab/lzma/7z/rar/... archive unpacker, based on mime-types
      • FU::Utils - Miscellaneous Utility Functions
      • Notes for FU::Utils!
        • Supports booleans, brotli, file descriptor passing, gzip, HTTP dates and json
      • Gzip::Faster - Simple and fast gzip and gunzip
      • Gzip::Libdeflate - Libdeflate compression library
      • Notes for Gzip::Libdeflate!
        • This module provides an interface to libdeflate. Libdeflate is a newly-written compression library which offers the same deflate form of compression as zlib, as used in gzip, libpng and many similar utilities. This distribution includes libdeflate within itself, so it is not necessary to have that on your system
      • Gzip::RandomAccess - Extract arbitrary bits of a gzip stream
      • IO::Compress - Is an interface to zlib compression library
      • Notes for IO::Compress!
        • Contains streamzip to create a zip file from stdin
        • Contains zipdetails to display the internal structure of zip files
      • IO::Compress::Base - Base Class for IO::Compress modules
      • IO::Compress::Brotli - Write Brotli buffers/streams
      • IO::Compress::Bzip2 - Write bzip2 files/buffers
      • Notes for IO::Compress::Bzip2!
        • See also IO::Uncompress::Bunzip2
      • IO::Compress::Deflate - Write RFC 1950 files/buffers
      • Notes for IO::Compress::Deflate!
        • See TheRFCGuide for rfc1950
      • IO::Compress::FAQ - Frequently Asked Questions about IO::Compress
      • IO::Compress::Gzip - Write RFC 1952 files/buffers
      • Notes for IO::Compress::Gzip!
        • See also IO::Uncompress::Gunzip
        • See TheRFCGuide for rfc1952
      • IO::Compress::Lzf - Write lzf files/buffers
      • IO::Compress::Lzip - Write lzip files/buffers
      • Notes for IO::Compress::Lzip!
        • Contains IO::Compress::Lzma and IO::Compress::Xz and the 3 uncompressers too
      • IO::Compress::Lzma - Write lzma files/buffers
      • IO::Compress::Lzop - Write lzop files/buffers
      • IO::Compress::RawDeflate - Write RFC 1951 files/buffers
      • Notes for IO::Compress::RawDeflate!
        • See RfGuide for rfc1951
      • IO::Compress::Xz - Write xz files/buffers
      • IO::Compress::Zip - Write zip files/buffers
      • Notes for IO::Compress::Zip!
        • Supports store, deflate, bzip2, lzma, xz, zstd
      • IO::Compress::Zstd - Write zstd files/buffers
      • IO::Uncompress::Bunzip2 - Read bzip2 files/buffers
      • IO::Uncompress::Gunzip - Read RFC 1952 files/buffers
      • Notes for IO::Uncompress::Gunzip!
        • See TheRFCGuide for rfc1952
      • IO::Uncompress::RawInflate - Read RFC 1951 files/buffers
      • Notes for IO::Uncompress::RawInflate!
        • See TheRFCGuide for rfc1951
      • IO::Uncompress::UnLzma - Read lzma files/buffers
      • IO::Uncompress::UnXz - Read xz files/buffers
      • IO::Uncompress::Unzip - Read zip files/buffers
      • IO::Zlib - IO:: style interface to Compress::Zlib
      • MsgPack::Raw - Perl bindings to the msgpack C library
      • PAR - Perl Archive Toolkit
      • Notes for PAR!
        • This module lets you use special zip files, called Perl Archives, as libraries from which Perl modules can be loaded
      • PAR::Packer - PAR Packager
      • Notes for PAR::Packer!
        • This module implements the App::Packer::Backend interface, for generating stand-alone executables, perl scripts and PAR files
      • Sereal - Fast, compact, powerful binary (de-)serialization
      • Sereal::Decoder - Fast, compact, powerful binary deserialization
      • Sereal::Encoder - Fast, compact, powerful binary serialization
      • Stream - Provide input & output encoding & decoding
      • Notes for Stream!
        • Used by Hypersonic
      • streamzip - Create a zip file from stdin
      • Notes for streamzip!
        • Supports store, deflate, bzip2, lzma, xz, zstd
      • XS::libbrotli - Brotli compression library for XS.

      • Array::Autojoin - Arrayrefs that stringify as join(", ", @$it)
      • Array::Circular - Provide an array data structure that can go around in circles
      • Array::Compare - Perl extension for comparing arrays
      • Array::Diff - Find the differences between two arrays
      • Array::Intersection - Exports an array intersection function

        • Other modules discussed in the POD: Class::Iter, Class::Iterator, Data::Iter, Data::Iterator::EasyObj and Tie::Array::Iterable
      • Array::Iterator::Circular - This iterator will loop continuously as long as next or get_next is called
      • Array::Join - Performs SQL-like joins on arrays
      • Array::Merge::Unique - Merge those arrays uniquely
      • Notes for Array::Merge::Unique!
        • See also List::MoreUtils
      • Array::Objectify - Objectify an array
      • Array::PseudoScalar - Arrays that behave like scalars
      • Array::Transpose - Transposes a 2-Dimensional Array
      • Data::LnArray - The great new Data::LnArray
      • Notes for Data::LnArray!
        • Arrays with lots of access methods
      • Data::LnArray::XS - Arrays as just above
      • Data::Table - Data type related to database tables, spreadsheets, CSV/TSV files, HTML table displays, etc
      • HATX - A fluent interface for Hash and Array Transformations
      • List::MoreUtils - Provide the stuff missing in List::Util
      • List::UtilsBy - Higher-order list utility functions
      • Notes for List::UtilsBy!
        • Amazing!
      • Math::MatrixBool - Matrix of Booleans
      • Math::MatrixReal - Matrix of Reals

      • MARC::Validator - Set of plugins for MARC validation

      • App::pherkin - Run Cucumber tests from the command line
      • Chrome::Util::Profile - List available Google Chrome profiles
      • Firefox::Marionette - Automate the Firefox browser with the Marionette protocol
      • Notes for Firefox::Marionette!
        • https://firefox-source-docs.mozilla.org/testing/marionette/Protocol.html
      • Firefox::Util::Profile - Given a Firefox profile name, return its directory
      • Mozilla::CA - Mozilla's CA cert bundle in PEM format
      • MozRepl - Perl interface of MozRepl
      • Pherkin::Extension::Weasel - Pherkin extension for web-testing
      • Selenium::Firefox - Use FirefoxDriver without a Selenium server
      • Selenium::Remote::Driver - Perl Client for Selenium Remote Driver
      • Vivaldi::Util::Profile - List available Vivaldi profiles
      • Weasel - Perl's php/Mink-inspired abstracted web-driver framework
      • Weasel::Driver::Selenium2 - Weasel driver wrapping Selenium::Remote::Driver
      • Weasel::Driver::Selenium4 - Weasel driver wrapping Selenium::Client
      • WWW::Mechanize::Chrome - Automate the Chrome browser
      • WWW::Mechanize::Chrome::DOMops - Operations on the DOM loaded in Chrome
      • WWW::Mechanize::Firefox - Use Firefox as if it were WWW::Mechanize
      • Notes for WWW::Mechanize::Firefox!
        • Recommends MozRepl, above
    • BsdWare
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
      • BSD::Resource - BSD process resource limit and priority functions
      • PerlPowerTools - Power Tools (BSD utilities in pure Perl)
      • Notes for PerlPowerTools!
        • In rare cases these use different options or work differently than standard Linux tools, which can lead to confusion if they and the Linux versions are both on your PATH. I personally deleted them when I hit this problem (or rather, it hit me)
    • BusinessApps
      • See also!

      • API::Eulerian - Simple interface for all the API provided by Eulerian Technologies
      • App::karr - Kanban Assignment & Responsibility Registry
      • BankAccount::Validator::UK -
      • Business::BR::NFSe - Consulta e emissÃ£o de NFS-e (Nota Fiscal de ServiÃ§os EletrÃ´nica) via API REST do Emissor Nacional (Sefin)
      • Business::CAMT - ISO20022 Cash Management (CAMT) messages
      • Business::ES::CodigoPostal - ValidaciÃ³n de cÃ³digos postales espaÃ±oles y obtenciÃ³n de provincia
      • Business::ES::NIF - Validate Spanish NIF, NIE and CIF numbers
      • Business::GoCardless - Top level namespace for the Business::GoCardless set of modules
      • Business::ISSN - Perl extension for International Standard Serial Numbers
      • Business::KontoCheck - Perl extension for checking German and Austrian Bank Account Numbers
      • Business::LCCN - Work with Library of Congress Control Number (LCCN) codes
      • Business::NAB - Business::NAB is the top level namespace for the various modules that are used to parse/create the file formats used for interchange with NAB
      • Business::Tax::US::Form_1040::Worksheets - IRS Form 1040 worksheets calculations
      • Business::Tax::VAT::Validation - Validate EU VAT numbers against VIES/HMRC
      • Business::UPS - A UPS Interface Module
      • Business::US::USPS::IMB - Perl Binding for usps4cb
      • Business::Westpac - Top level namespace for the Business::Westpac set of modules
      • Data::Currency - Provides basic currency formatting and conversion
      • Data::Money - Money/currency with formatting and overloading.
      • Data::MoneyCurrency - Get information for different currencies
      • EAI::Wrap - Framework for easy creation of Enterprise Application Integration tasks
      • Notes for EAI::Wrap!
        • Functionality: DB, DateUtil, FTP and File
      • Finance::Calendar - Represents the trading calendar
      • Finance::Currency::Convert - Convert currencies and fetch their exchange rates (with Finance::Quote)
      • Finance::Currency::Convert::WebserviceX - Lightweight currency conversion using WebserviceX.NET
      • Finance::Exchange - Represents a financial stock exchange object.
      • Finance::GDAX::API::Currency - Currencies
      • Finance::Google::Portfolio - Manipulate Google Finance portfolios a little
      • Finance::MIFIR::CONCAT - Provides CONCAT code generation out of client data according to MIFIR rules
      • Finance::Quote - Get stock and mutual fund quotes from various exchanges
      • Finance::Tax::Aruba - A package that deals with tax calculations for Aruba
      • Finance::Tiller2QIF - Convert Tiller CSV exports to QIF for import into Financial software like GnuCash, KMyMoney, Quicken, HomeBank, Money Manager Ex and many others
      • LedgerSMB::Installer - LedgerSMB is a free web-based double-entry accounting system
      • Notes for LedgerSMB::Installer!
        • https://github.com/ledgersmb/LedgerSMB
      • Lib::Pepper - Perl bindings for the Pepper payment terminal library
      • Locale::Currency - Module for dealing with currency code sets
      • Locale::Currency::Format - Perl functions for formatting monetary values
      • masser::home - Masser is a perl-based fastcgi-driven application framework. Not a Perl module
      • Math::Currency - Exact Currency Math with Formatting and Rounding
      • NBI::Slurm - NBI Slurm module
      • Notes for NBI::Slurm!
        • SLURM stands for Simple Linux Utility for Resource Management
        • Debian via apt has available 7 basic components of SLURM
      • Net::PayPal::Lite - Unofficial Perl extension for PayPal's REST API (Lite version)
      • Net::Stripe - API client for Stripe.com
      • Notes for Net::Stripe!
        • https://stripe.com/au
      • oEdtk - A module for industrial printing processing
      • PDK::Utils - Provides utility functions for managing and parsing PDK configuration contexts
      • WWW::PayPal - Perl client for the PayPal REST API
      • Notes for WWW::PayPal!
        • https://developer.paypal.com/docs/api/orders/v2/
        • https://github.com/paypal/paypal-rest-api-specifications
      • WWW::Salesforce - This class provides a simple SOAP client for Salesforce.com
    • CachingStuff
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • For Melian the sever
        • A great HOWTO
      • BerkeleyDB - Perl extension for Berkeley DB version 2, 3, 4, 5 or 6
      • Notes for BerkeleyDB!
        • (Cache::FastMmap says) Very fast (data ends up mostly in shared memory cache) but acts as a database overall, so data is not automatically expired
      • Cache::Cache - The Cache interface
      • Notes for Cache::Cache!
        • But see CHI, Cache::FastMmap below
      • Cache::FastMmap - Uses an mmap'ed file to act as a shared memory interprocess cache
      • Cache::FileCache - Iimplements the Cache interface
      • Notes for Cache::FileCache!
        • (Cache::FastMmap says) Lots of features, slow
      • Cache::File::Simple - Dead simple file based caching meachanism
      • Cache::Memcached::Fast - Perl client for memcached, in C language
      • Notes for Cache::Memcached::Fast!
        • The API is largely compatible with Cache::Memcached, the original pure Perl client
        • See Debian.Wiki.html's Installation para for installation of memcached
        • See http://www.memcached.org for the memory cache daemon
      • Cache::Memcached::Fast::Safe - Cache::Memcached::Fast with sanitizing keys and fork-safe
      • Cache::Memcached::PDeque - Implements a priority deque using memcached as storage
      • Notes for Cache::Memcached::PDeque!
        • This is an implementation of a double-ended queue, with support for priorities
        • Implements the Cache interface
        • https://metacpan.org/pod/Cache::MemoryCache
        • But see CHI., Cache::FastMmap below
      • Cache::Mmap - Shared data cache using memory mapped files
      • Notes for Cache::Mmap!
        • (Cache::FastMmap says) Similar to Cache::FastMmap, in pure perl. slows down with larger pages
      • Cache::SharedMemoryCache - Extends the MemoryCache class
      • Notes for Cache::SharedMemoryCache!
        • (Cache::FastMmap says) Lots of features, VERY slow. Uses IPC::ShareLite which freeze/thaws ALL data at each read/write
      • CHI - Unified cache handling interface
      • Notes for CHI!
        • The successor to Cache::Cache
      • CHI::Driver::SharedMem - Cache data in shared memory
      • Class::Simple::Cached - Cache messages to an object
      • Class::Simple::Readonly::Cached - Cache messages to an object
      • Data::BitSet::Shared - Shared-memory fixed-size bitset for Linux
      • Data::BloomFilter::Shared - Shared-memory Bloom filter for Linux
      • Data::Buffer - Read/write buffer class
      • Data::Buffer::Shared - Type-specialized shared-memory buffers for multiprocess access
      • Data::CountMinSketch::Shared - Shared-memory Count-Min sketch for Linux
      • Data::CuckooFilter::Shared - Shared-memory Cuckoo filter for Linux
      • Data::Deque::Shared - Shared-memory double-ended queue for Linux
      • Data::DisjointSet::Shared - Shared-memory union-find (disjoint-set) for Linux
      • Data::Graph::Shared - Shared-memory directed weighted graph for Linux
      • Data::HashMap - Fast type-specialized hash maps implemented in C
      • Data::HashMap::Shared - Type-specialized shared-memory hash maps for multiprocess access
      • Data::Heap::Shared - Shared-memory binary min-heap (priority queue) for Linux
      • Data::Histogram::Shared - Shared-memory HdrHistogram for Linux
      • Data::HyperLogLog::Shared - Shared-memory HyperLogLog cardinality estimator for Linux
      • Data::Intern::Shared - Shared-memory string interning table for Linux
      • Data::Log::Shared - Append-only shared-memory log (WAL) for Linux
      • Data::NDArray::Shared - Shared-memory typed N-dimensional numeric array for Linux
      • Data::PubSub::Shared - High-performance shared-memory pub/sub for Linux
      • Data::Pool::Shared - Fixed-size shared-memory object pool for Linux
      • Data::Queue::Shared - High-performance shared-memory MPMC queues for Linux
      • Data::RadixTree::Shared - Shared-memory compressed radix tree (prefix tree) for Linux
      • Data::ReqRep::Shared - High-performance shared-memory request/response IPC for Linux
      • Data::RingBuffer::Shared - Shared-memory fixed-size ring buffer for Linux
      • Data::RoaringBitmap::Shared - Shared-memory Roaring bitmap (compressed uint32 set) for Linux
      • Data::SortedSet::Shared - Shared-memory sorted set (ZSET) for Linux
      • Data::SpatialHash::Shared - Shared-memory spatial hash index for Linux
      • Data::Stack::Shared - Shared-memory LIFO stack for Linux
      • Data::Sync::Shared - Shared-memory synchronization primitives for Linux
      • DBI - Database independent interface for Perl
      • Notes for DBI!
        • (Cache::FastMmap says) Use your favourite RDBMS. can perform well, need a DB server running. very global. socket connection latency
      • File::Meta::Cache - Cache open file descriptors and stat meta data
      • HTTP::Cache::Transparent - Cache the result of http get-requests persistently
      • IPC::MM - Perl interface to Ralf Engelschall's mm library
      • Notes for IPC::MM!
        • (Cache::FastMmap says) Hash implementation is broken, data is not automatically expired, slow
      • LCC - Content Provider Modules for the Local Content Cache system
      • Notes for LCC!
        • See also https://sourceforge.net/projects/lococa/
      • Legba - Global slot storage implemented in XS
      • LRU::Cache - LRU cache with O(1) operation
      • Melian - Perl client to the Melian cache
      • Notes for Melian!
        • A quote from Gemini: Melian (the cache server by Sawyer X and Gonzalo Diethelm) is a fantastic addition to the "High Performance" section of your wiki. It uses a custom binary protocol over UNIX or TCP sockets for microsecond-level lookups - great companion to the modern Perl stack
      • Memoize - Make functions faster by trading space for time
      • Memoize::Expire - Plug-in module for automatic expiration of memoized values
      • MLDBM::Sync - Safe concurrent access to MLDBM databases
      • Notes for MLDBM::Sync!
        • (Cache::FastMmap says) Acts as a database, data is not automatically expired, slow
      • Net::Clacks - Fast client/server interprocess messaging and memcached replacement
      • Resource::Silo - Lazy declarative resource container for Perl
      • WWW::Mechanize::Cached - Cache response to be polite
    • CaptchaStuff
      • See also!
        • https://cloud.google.com/security/products/recaptcha
        • https://developers.google.com/recaptcha/docs/v3
      • Captcha::Stateless::Text - Stateless, text-based CAPTCHAs
      • Captcha::reCAPTCHA - A Perl implementation of the reCAPTCHA API version 0.99
      • Captcha::reCAPTCHA::V2 - A Perl implementation of reCAPTCHA API version 2
      • Captcha::reCAPTCHA::V3 - A Perl implementation of reCAPTCHA API version v3

      • Album - Create and maintain browser based photo albums
      • Alien::CFITSIO - Build and Install the CFITSIO library
      • Notes for Alien::CFITSIO!
        • See also https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/docs/software/fitsio/fitsio.html
      • Alien::GtkNodes - Find or install GtkNodes
      • App::BlurFill - A simple command line tool to create a blurred background image
      • Notes for App::BlurFill!
        • Command line access to a Dancer app
        • curl -OJ -X POST http://localhost:5000/blur -F "image=@input.jpg"
      • App::gimpgitbuild - Build GIMP from git
      • App::ImageMagickUtils - Utilities related to ImageMagick
      • App::Image::Generator - Perl class for image-generator application
      • App::imgsize - Show dimensions of image files
      • Cairo - Perl interface to the cairo 2d vector graphics library
      • Chart::ECharts - Apache ECharts wrapper for Perl
      • Data::Image - Data object for image
      • File::LibMagic - Determine MIME types of data or files using libmagic
      • GD - Interface to Gd Graphics Library
      • GD::Polyline - Polyline object and Polygon utilities (including splines) for use with GD
      • GD::Simple - Simplified interface to GD library
      • Notes for GD::Simple!
        • Tutorial on usage at http://perlmaven.com/drawing-images-using-gd-simple
      • GD::SVG - Seamlessly enable SVG output from scripts written using GD
      • Gimp - Write GIMP extensions/plug-ins/load- and save-handlers in Perl
      • Notes for Gimp!
        • Git repo for the module is at https://gitlab.gnome.org/GNOME/gimp-perl
      • Gnuplot::Builder - Object-oriented gnuplot script builder
      • Graphics::Framebuffer - A Simple Framebuffer Graphics Library
      • Graphics::Penplotter::GcodeXY - Produce gcode files for pen plotters from Perl
      • HTML::D3 - A simple Perl module for generating bar charts using D3.js
      • Image::APNG - Generate Animated PNG (APNG) files from individual PNG images
      • Image::Base - Base class for loading, manipulating and saving images
      • Image::Base::GD - Draw images with GD
      • Image::Bitmap2Paths - Perl extension to convert line-drawing (and font's) bitmaps to paths (of vector formats)
      • Image::Dot - Create 1x1 pixel image files (pure-perl)
      • Image::Epeg - Thumbnail jpegs at lightning speed
      • Image::ExifTool - The command line tool exiftool has many, many options and supports a vast array of types
      • Notes for Image::ExifTool!
        • https://exiftool.org/
        • http://exiftool.sourceforge.net/
      • Image::ExifTool::TagNames - ExifTool tag name documentation
      • Image::Info - Extract meta information from image files
      • Image::JPEG::Size - Find the size of JPEG images
      • ImageMagick - sudo apt-get install imagemagick
      • Notes for ImageMagick!
        • Site has a list of exploits and many examples
      • Image::Magick::Safer - Wrap Image::Magick Read method to check magic bytes
      • Image::Magick::Thumbnail::PDF - Make thumbnail of a page in a pdf document
      • Image::MetaData::JPEG - Perl extension for showing/modifying JPEG (meta)data
      • Notes for Image::MetaData::JPEG!
        • But see Image::ExifTool
      • Image::PNG::Libpng - Perl interface to the C library libpng
      • Image::PNG::QRCode - ABANDONED - Make PNG images containing QR codes from your text
      • Image::Synchronize - Synchronize filesystem modification timestamps of images, movies, and related files
      • Image::ThumbHash - A very compact representation of an image placeholder
      • Imager - Perl extension for Generating 24 bit Images
      • Notes for Imager!
        • To get my demo test.imager.tiles.pl of Imager running, I had to do
        • sudo aptitude install libpng-dev
        • sudo aptitude install libfreetype6-dev
        • sudo aptitude install libfont-freetype-perl
        • Probably the 1st and 3rd only are required
      • Imager::File::GIF - Read and write GIF files
      • Imager::File::HEIF - Read and write HEIF files
      • Imager::File::JPEG - Read and write JPEG files
      • Imager::File::QOI - Read and write QOI image files
      • Imager::File::TIFF - Read and write TIFF files
      • Imager::File::WEBP - Read and write WEBP files
      • Imager::Font::FT2 - Font support using FreeType 2
      • Imager::QRCode - Generate QR Code with Imager using libqrencode
      • Matplotlib::Simple - Simple plotting
      • Notes for Matplotlib::Simple!
        • Note: POD is useless
      • PGPLOT - Allow subroutines in the PGPLOT graphics library to be called from Perl
      • PGPLOTx::Constants - Constants for use with PGPLOT
      • Notes for PGPLOTx::Constants!
        • See also Types::PGPLOT below
      • PGPLOT::Device - Autogenerate PGPLOT device names
      • Prima - A Perl graphic toolkit
      • Prima::Cairo - Prima extension for Cairo drawing
      • Math::Image::CalcResized - Calculate dimensions of image/video resized by ImageMagick-like geometry specification
      • Snig - (Simple|Static|Small|S..) Image Gallery
      • SVG - Perl extension for generating Scalable Vector Graphics (SVG) documents
      • SVG::Barcode - Base class for SVG 1D and 2D codes
      • SVG::Barcode::UPCA - Generator for SVG based UPCA barcodes
      • SVG::Timeline - Create SVG timeline charts
      • SVG::TrafficLight - Perl extension to produce SVG diagrams of traffic lights
      • Types::PGPLOT - Type::Tiny compatible types for the PGPLOT library
      • Notes for Types::PGPLOT!
        • See also PGPLOTx::Constants above
      • WebGPU::Direct - Direct access to the WebGPU native APIs
    • ChemistryStuff
      • See also!
        • http://www.perlmol.org/
        • http://opensmiles.org/opensmiles.html
      • Chemistry::Domain - Class for domains in macromolecules
      • Chemistry::File - Molecule file I/O base class
      • Chemistry::File::InChI - InChI identifier reader
      • Chemistry::File::SMARTS - SMARTS chemical substructure pattern linear notation parser
      • Chemistry::File::SMILES - SMILES linear notation parser/writer
      • Chemistry::MacroMol - Perl module for macromolecules
      • Chemistry::Mol - Molecule object toolkit
      • Notes for Chemistry::Mol!
        • Contains Chemistry::Atom, Chemistry::Bond, Chemistry::Tutorial
      • Chemistry::OpenSMILES - OpenSMILES format reader and writer
      • Chemistry::Pattern - Chemical substructure pattern matching
      • Chemistry::Reaction - Explicit chemical reactions
      • Chemistry::Ring - Represent a ring as a substructure of a molecule
      • Chemistry::Tutorial - PerlMol Quick Tutorial
    • ChildProcesses
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • https://metacpan.org/pod/perlipc#Bidirectional-Communication-with-Another-Process
      • Acme::Ghost - Yet another view to daemon processes
      • AnyEvent::Run - Run a process or coderef asynchronously
      • App::ProcUtils - Command line utilities related to processes
      • Child - Object oriented simple interface to fork()
      • CTK - CTK ToolKit library (CTKlib)
      • CTK::Daemon - Abstract class to implement Daemons
      • Daemon::Control - Create init scripts in Perl
      • Daemon::Daemonize - An easy-to-use daemon(izing) toolkit
      • Daemon::Device - Forking daemon device construct
      • Daemon::Generic - Framework to provide start/stop/reload for a daemon
      • HTTP::Daemon - A simple http server class
      • Forks::Super - Extensions and convenience methods to manage background processes
      • Notes for Forks::Super!
        • Provides new definitions for the Perl functions fork, wait, and waitpid with richer functionality
      • IO::Pipely - Portably create pipe() or pipe-like handles, one way or another
      • IPC::Cmd - Finding and running system commands made easy
      • Notes for IPC::Cmd!
        • system() and background procs w/ piping, redirs, ptys (Unix, Win32)
        • Can use IPC::Run
      • IPC::LockTicket - Use Storable library to IPC token to prevent parallel access to any resources. Including your custom data to transfer asynchronously
      • Notes for IPC::LockTicket!
        • But see Storable::Improved
      • IPC::MicroSocket - Minimal request/response or pub/sub mechanism
      • IPC::Open3 - Open a process for reading, writing, and error handling using open3()
      • IPC::Open3::Simple - A simple alternative to IPC::Open3
      • IPC::Run - system() and background procs w/ piping, redirs, ptys (Unix, Win32)
      • Notes for IPC::Run!
        • IPC::Run is a much more complete solution for running child processes (than the next)
      • IPC::Run3 - Run a subprocess with input/output redirection
      • Notes for IPC::Run3!
        • POD contains important comparison with various similar modules
      • IPC::Shareable - Use shared memory backed variables across processes
      • IPC::ShellCmd - Run a command with a given environment and capture output
      • MooseX::Daemonize - Role for daemonizing your Moose based application
      • Net::Daemon - Perl extension for portable daemons
      • Object::Remote - Call methods on objects in other processes or on other hosts
      • Proc::Application::Daemon - Daemon class based on Proc::Application
      • Proc::Background - Much simpler than the above
      • Notes for Proc::Background!
        • Supports Linux and MS Windows
        • Refers to the previous module
        • Refers to the Win32 module below
      • Proc::Daemon - Run Perl program(s) as a daemon process
      • Proc::Fork - Simple, intuitive interface to the fork() system call
      • Proc::Forking - Provides a set of tool to fork and daemonize
      • Proc::Forkmap - Map with forking
      • Proc::Govern - Run child process and govern its various aspects
      • Proc::PID::File - A module to manage process id files
      • Proc::ProcessTable - Perl extension to access the unix process table
      • Proc::ProcessTable::Colorizer - Like ps, but with colored columns and enhnaced functions for searching
      • Proc::Simple - Launch and control background processes
      • Proc::Spawn - Run external programs
      • Spawn::Safe - Fork and exec a process "safely"
      • Sys::Cmd - Run a system command or spawn a system processes
      • System::Command - Object for running system commands
      • Win32::Console::DotNet - Win32::Console .NET interface
      • Win32::IPC - Base class for Win32 synchronization objects
      • Win32::ShellQuote - Everyone quotes command line arguments the wrong way
      • Workflow::Inotify - Script to daemonize a Linux::Inotify2 handler
      • Zleep - Fork a new process, sleep for the number of ms and then execute the codeblock
    • ClipBoard
      • App::ClipboardUtils - CLI utilities related to clipboard
      • Clipboard - Copy and paste with any OS
      • Notes for Clipboard!
        • It includes 5 command line utilities for clipboard manipulation
      • Clipboard::Any - Common interface to clipboard manager functions
    • CodingStyle
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eshu
      • App::CriticDB - Manage a database of Perl::Critic violations
      • Code::TidyAll - Engine for tidyall, your all-in-one code tidier and validator
      • Eshu - Indentation fixer for C, Perl, XS, XML, HTML, CSS, JavaScript and POD source files
      • Guacamole - Enforce Standard Perl syntax with Guacamole
      • mb - Can easy script in Big5, Big5-HKSCS, GBK, Sjis(also CP932), UHC, UTF-8, ...
      • Perl::Critic - Critique Perl source code for best-practices
      • Perl::Critic::Community - Community-inspired Perl::Critic policies
      • Perl::Critic::Grape - Additional Perl::Critic policies
      • Perl::Critic::PJCJ - Perl::Critic policies for code style consistency
      • Perl::Critic::PolicyBundle::SNEZ - Extra Perl::Critic policies
      • Perl::Critic::Policy::Community::DiscouragedModules - Various modules discouraged from use
      • Perl::Critic::Policy::Community::PreferredAlternatives - Various modules with preferred alternatives
      • Perl::Critic::Policy::ControlStructures::LoopsRequireLabels - Require labels on loops and their break keywords
      • Perl::Critic::Policy::ControlStructures::ProhibitBareBlockLoopControls - Prohibit unlabeled loop controls in non-loop blocks
      • Perl::Critic::Policy::ControlStructures::ProhibitReturnInDoBlock - Do not "return" in "do" block
      • Perl::Critic::Policy::ControlStructures::ProhibitReturnInMappingBlock - Do not "return" in mapping blocks (map, grep, sort)
      • Perl::Critic::Policy::Misc::ProhibitMethodsInStringConcat - Prohibit method calls in string concatenation
      • Perl::Critic::Policy::RegularExpressions::ProhibitEmptyAlternatives - Beware empty alternatives, because they always match
      • Perl::Critic::Policy::Security::RandBytesFromHash - Flag common anti-patterns for generating random bytes
      • Perl::Critic::Policy::ValuesAndExpressions::ProhibitFiletest_rwxRWX - Prohibit file access tests
      • Perl::Critic::Policy::Variables::ProhibitLoopOnHash - Don't write loops on hashes, only on keys and values of hashes
      • Perl::Critic::Policy::Variables::ProhibitNumericNamesWithLeadingZero - Don't use numeric variable names with leading zeros
      • Perl::Critic::Policy::Variables::ProhibitUnusedVariables - Don't ask for storage you don't need
      • Perl::Critic::Policy::Variables::ProhibitUnusedVarsStricter - Don't ask for storage you don't need
      • Perl::Critic::Pulp - Some add-on perlcritic policies
      • Notes for Perl::Critic::Pulp!
        • Contains Types::Standard
        • See DataTypes
      • Perl::Critic::StricterSubs - Perl::Critic plugin for stricter subroutine checks
      • Perl::Tidy - Parses and beautifies perl source

      • Acme::AutoColor - Automatic color names
      • ANSI::Palette - ANSI Color palettes
      • App::ANSIColorUtils - Utilities related to ANSI color
      • Colors - ImageMagick Color Names
      • Color::ANSI::Util - Routines for dealing with ANSI colors
      • Color::Library - An easy-to-use and comprehensive named-color library
      • Color::Rgb - Simple rgb.txt parsing class
      • Color::RGB::Util - Utilities related to RGB colors
      • Color::Spectrum - Just another HTML color generator
      • Colouring::In - Color or colour
      • Colouring::In::XS - Color or colour
      • Data::Displaycolour - Work with display colours
      • Graphics::ColorNames - Defines RGB values for common color names
      • Graphics::ColorNames::CSS - CSS color names and equivalent RGB values
      • Graphics::ColorNames::GrayScale - RGB values of shades of gray and base colors
      • Graphics::ColorNames::HTML - HTML color names and equivalent RGB values
      • Graphics::ColorNames::Mozilla - Mozilla, Netscape 6.0, Galeon (anything using Gecko) color names and equivalent RGB values
      • Graphics::ColorNames::Pantone - RGB values of Pantone colors
      • Graphics::ColorNames::SVG - SVG color names and equivalent RGB values
      • Graphics::ColorNames::WWW - WWW color names and equivalent RGB values
      • Graphics::ColorNamesLite::All - Lots of color names and equivalent RGB values (lite version)
      • Notes for Graphics::ColorNamesLite::All!
        • There is also Graphics::ColorNamesLite::All
        • https://metacpan.org/dist/Graphics-ColorNamesLite-EN
      • Graphics::ColorObject - Convert between color spaces
      • Notes for Graphics::ColorObject!
        • Part of https://metacpan.org/dist/Term-ANSIColor-Concise
      • Graphics::Colourset - Create sets of colours
      • Graphics::ColorUtils - Easy-to-use color space conversions and more
      • Graphics::Toolkit::Color - Color palette creation helper
      • Term::ANSI::Sprintf - Sprintf with ANSI colors
      • Term::ANSIColor - Color screen output using ANSI escape sequences. Built-in
      • Notes for Term::ANSIColor!
        • It only acts on the terminal formatting escape sequences that end in 'm' and leaves all other ones alone
      • Term::ANSIColor::Concise - Produce ANSI terminal sequence by concise notation
      • Notes for Term::ANSIColor::Concise!
        • Contains Graphics::ColorObject
      • Term::ANSIColor::Gradients - Curated ANSI 256-color palette library
      • Term::Chrome - DSL for colors and other terminal chrome
      • Term::ExtendedColor - Color screen output using 256 colors
    • CommandLineStuff
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ANSI_escape_code
        • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/X11_color_names
        • For unibilium, a terminfo parsing library
        • For env var NO_COLOR
        • For Turso DB
      • Aion::Emitter - Event dispatcher
      • Aion::Env - Creates a constant associated with the value from .env
      • Aion::Run - Role for console commands
      • Alien::libtickit - Alien wrapping for libtickit
      • Notes for Alien::libtickit!
        • This module behaves like ExtUtils::PkgConfig
      • Alien::Turso::CLI - Install and find Turso CLI
      • App::ansicolumn - ANSI sequence aware column command
      • App::ansifold - Fold command handling ANSI terminal sequences
      • App::BashHistoryUtils - CLI utilities related to bash history file
      • App::CaseUtils - CLI utilities related to case (uppercase/lowercase)
      • App::ChangeShebang - Change shebang lines for relocatable perl
      • App::Cmd - Write command line apps with less suffering
      • App::cat::v - cat-v command implementation
      • App::chot - Command Heuristic Omni-Tracer
      • Notes for App::chot!
        • But see App::PMUtils
      • App::lms - A utility to locate and display command or library files
      • Notes for App::lms!
        • But see App::PMUtils
      • App::nup - N-up output wrapper for optex -Mup
      • App::oo_modulino_zsh_completion_helper - Provides zsh completion for OO-Modulinos
      • App::optex - General purpose command option wrapper
      • App::optex::up - Optex module for multi-column paged output
      • App::optex::rpn - Optex module for Reverse Polish Notation calculation
      • App::optex::xform - Data transform filter module for optex
      • App::papersway - PaperWM-like scrollable tiling window management for Sway/i3wm
      • Notes for App::papersway!
        • https://github.com/paperwm/paperwm
      • App::PickArgs - Pick one or more items from command-line arguments
      • App::PMUtils - Command-line utilities related to Perl modules
      • App::ptimeout - Module implementing ptimeout
      • App::SpeedTest - Command-line interface to speedtest.net
      • Argv - The special filehandle that iterates over command-line filenames in @ARGV
      • ARGV::OrDATA - Let the diamond operator read from DATA if there's no ARGV
      • B::cstring - Returns a double-quote-surrounded escaped version of STR which can be used as a string in C source code
      • B::perlstring - Returns a double-quote-surrounded escaped version of STR which can be used as a string in Perl source code
      • BATsh - Bilingual Shell: cmd.exe and bash in one script (self-contained)
      • Chandra - Perl bindings to webview-c for creating cross-platform GUIs
      • Check::Term - Check terminal functionality
      • Check::Term::Color - Check terminal color functionality
      • CLI::Cmdline - Minimal command-line parser with short/long options and aliases in pure Perl
      • CLI::Framework - Build standardized, flexible, testable command-line applications
      • CLI::Helpers - Subroutines for making simple command line scripts
      • CLI::Simple - A minimalist object oriented base class for CLI applications
      • Command::Run - Execute external command or code reference
      • Command::Runner - Run external commands and Perl code refs
      • cpanm-shcompgen - Install modules from CPAN (and activate shell completion)
      • Notes for cpanm-shcompgen!
        • See shcompgen below
      • Curses - Terminal screen handling and optimization
      • Curses::UI - A curses based OO user interface framework
      • Curses::UI::POE - A subclass makes Curses::UI POE Friendly
      • Expect - Automate interactions with command line programs that expose a text terminal interface
      • Getopt::App - Write and test your script with ease
      • Notes for Getopt::App!
        • Helps you structure your scripts and integrates Getopt::Long with a very simple API
        • Makes it very easy to test your script, since the script file can be sourced without actually being run
      • Getopt::ArgvFile - Interpolates script options from files into @ARGV or another array
      • Getopt::Class - Extended dictionary version of Getopt::Long
      • Notes for Getopt::Class!
        • Fancy!
      • Getopt::EX - Getopt Extender
      • Getopt::EX::Colormap - ANSI terminal color and option support
      • Getopt::EX::Config - Getopt::EX module configuration interface
      • Getopt::EX::Hashed - Hash object automation for Getopt::Long
      • Getopt::EX::i18n - General i18n module
      • Getopt::EX::LabeledParam - Labeled parameter handling
      • Getopt::EX::termcolor - Getopt::EX termcolor module
      • Getopt::Guided - getopts implementation that follows POSIX utility guidelines
      • Getopt::Lazier - Lazy Getopt-like command-line options and argument parser
      • Getopt::Long - Extended processing of command line options
      • Getopt::Long::Bash - Bash option parsing that does what you mean
      • Getopt::Long::More - Like Getopt::Long, but with more stuffs
      • Getopt::Std - Process single-character switches with switch clustering. Built-in
      • Getopt::Type::Tiny - Clean Getopt::Long wrapper with Type::Tiny support
      • Getopt::Yath - Option processing yath style
      • IO::Termios - Supply termios(3) methods to IO::Handle objects
      • MooX::Cmd - Giving an easy Moo style way to make command organized CLI apps
      • MooX::Options - Explicit Options eXtension for Object Class
      • OptArgs2 - Command-line argument and option processor
      • PerlVision - Text-mode User Interface Widgets
      • Progress::Any - Record progress to any output
      • Progress::Any::Output::TermProgressBarColor - Output progress to terminal as color bar
      • Progress::Any::SIGHandler - Output progress to terminal as simple message
      • Ragnetto::Console - ANSI terminal control utility
      • Reply - Read, eval, print, loop, yay!
      • Rope::Cmd - Command Line Applications via Rope
      • shcompgen - Generate shell completion scripts
      • Notes for shcompgen!
        • See cpanm-shcompgen above
      • Shell::Cmd - Run shell commands with enhanced support
      • Shell::Perl - A read-eval-print loop in Perl
      • Notes for Shell::Perl!
        • See also Devel::REPL
      • String::ShellQuote - Quote strings for passing through the shell
      • Term::Choose - Choose items from a list interactively.
      • Term::Choose::LineFold - Control the number of occupied columns of a character string on a terminal with a monospaced font
      • Term::Choose::LineFold::XS - XS acceleration for Term-Choose-LineFold
      • Term::Choose::Util - TUI-related functions for selecting directories, files, numbers and subsets of lists.
      • Term::Encoding - Detect encoding of the current terminal
      • Term::Form - Read lines from STDIN
      • Term::CLI - CLI interpreter based on Term::ReadLine
      • Term::Terminfo - Access the terminfo database
      • Term::ProgressSpinner - Terminal Progress bars!
      • Term::ReadKey - A perl module for simple terminal control
      • Notes for Term::ReadKey!
        • Contains GetTerminalSize
      • Term::ReadLine - Perl interface to various readline packages. If no real package is found, substitutes stubs instead of basic functions
      • Term::ReadLine::Gnu - Perl extension for the GNU Readline/History Library
      • Term::ReadLine::Repl - A batteries included interactive Term::ReadLine REPL module
      • Term::UI - Term::ReadLine UI made easy
      • Tickit - Terminal Interface Construction KIT
      • TUI::Vision - Perl TUI Framework (Turbo Vision 2.0 Port)
      • Notes for TUI::Vision!
        • TUI::Vision is the successor namespace to the TV:: (TVision?) module family
      • TVision - Perl glue to the TurboVision library
      • Notes for TVision!
        • A modern port of Turbo Vision 2.0, the classical framework for text-based user interfaces. Now cross-platform and with Unicode support
      • Util::CommandLine - Command-line interface helper utility
    • ConfigFiles
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • https://editorconfig.org/
        • For .Desktop files
      • Advanced::Config - Perl module reads configuation files from various sources
      • Notes for Advanced::Config!
        • Advanced::Config is an enhanced implementation of a config file manager that allows you to manage almost any config file as a true object with a common interface
      • App::Cme - Configuration data checker or editor based on Config::Model
      • Notes for App::Cme!
        • Contains the cms command
      • App::Env - Manage application specific environments
      • App::Options - Combine command line options, environment vars, and option file values (for program configuration). Also discusses a range of other config-type modules
      • AppConfig - Perl5 module for reading configuration files and parsing command line arguments
      • Clearcase - Object oriented interface to Clearcase
      • Notes for Clearcase!
        • IBM DevOps Code ClearCase (also known as IBM Rational ClearCase) supports software configuration management (SCM)
      • Colon::Config - XS helper to read a configuration file using ':' as separator
      • Conf::Libconfig - Perl extension for libconfig
      • Config::Abstraction - Configuration Abstraction Layer
      • Config::Any - Load configuration from different file formats, transparently
      • Config::App - Cascading merged application configuration
      • Config::Auto - Magical config file parser
      • Config::Dot - Module for simple configure file parsing
      • Config::General - Generic Config Module
      • Notes for Config::General!
        • Has many features
      • Config::IniFiles - A module for reading .ini-style configuration files
      • Config::INI::Reader - A subclassable .ini-file parser
      • Config::INI::Reader::Multiline - Parser for .ini files with line continuations
      • Config::INI::RefVars - INI file reader, allows the referencing of INI and environment variables within the INI file
      • Config::INI::Tiny - Parse INI configuration in extremely little code
      • Config::IOD - Read and write IOD/INI configuration files
      • Notes for Config::IOD!
        • This module is a round-trip parser for IOD configuration format
        • If you only need to read IOD configuration files, you might want to use Config::IOD::Reader instead
        • See IOD below
      • Config::IOD::Reader - Read IOD/INI configuration files
      • Notes for Config::IOD::Reader!
        • It is a minimalist alternative to the more fully-featured Config::IOD
        • See IOD below
      • Config::Model - A framework to validate, migrate and edit configuration files
      • Notes for Config::Model!
        • I hope you never need code this complex
        • See also Config::Model::Tester
      • Config::Model::CursesUI - Curses interface to edit config data
      • Config::Model::Itself - Model (or schema) editor for Config::Model
      • Config::Model::LcdProc - Edit and validate LcdProc configuration file
      • Notes for Config::Model::LcdProc!
        • http://lcdproc.omnipotent.net/
      • Config::Model::OpenSsh - A graphical config editor for /etc/ssh/sshd_config, /etc/ssh/ssh_config and ~/.ssh/config
      • Config::Model::Systemd - Editor and validator for systemd configuration files
      • Config::Model::TkUI - Tk GUI to edit config data through Config::Model
      • Config::MorePerl - Convenient and flexible config loader in perl format
      • Config::Neat - Parse/render human-readable configuration files with inheritance and schema validation
      • Config::NINI - Parse NINI files
      • Config::Param - All you want to do with parameters for your program (or someone else's)
      • Notes for Config::Param!
        • Also discusses a range of other config-type modules
      • Config::Parser - Base class for configuration file parsers
      • Config::Resolver - Recursively resolve placeholders in a data structure
      • Config::ROFL - Yet another config module. Uses Config::ZOMG
      • Config::Simple - Simple configuration file class
      • Config::Simple::Conf - A fast and lightweight configuration file handler
      • Config::Structured - Provides generalized and structured configuration value access
      • Config::Tiny - Read/Write .ini style files with as little code as possible
      • Config::Tiny::Ordered - Read/Write ordered .ini style files with as little code as possible
      • Config::Writer - A module to write configuration files in an easy and safe way
      • Config::ZOMG - Yet another layer over Config::Any
      • ConfigReader::Simple - A simple line-oriented configuration file parser
      • Env::Assert - Ensure that the environment variables match what you need, or abort
      • Env::AsYaml - Is intended to be a tool for examination of the environment
      • Env::Dot - Read environment variables from a .env file
      • ENV::Util - Parse prefixed environment variables and dotenv (.env) files into Perl
      • File::DesktopEntry - Object to handle .desktop files
      • IOD - IOD (INI On Drugs) file format specification
      • Notes for IOD!
        • See Config::IOD and Config::IOD::Reader above
      • Parse::PlainConfig - Provides hierarchical config file structures
      • Perinci::CmdLine::Util::Config - Utility routines related to config files
      • TOML - Tom's Obvious, Minimal Language is a file format for configuration files
      • Notes for TOML!
        • Supports the following data types: string, integer, float, boolean, datetime, array, and table
      • TOML::Tiny - A minimal, pure perl TOML parser and serializer
    • ConstantStuff
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
      • Common::CodingTools - Common constants and functions for programmers
      • Const::Exporter - Declare constants for export
      • Const::Fast - Facility for creating read-only scalars, arrays, and hashes
      • Notes for Const::Fast!
        • But see Const::XS for speed tests
      • Const::Fast::Exporter -
      • Const::XS - Facility for creating read-only scalars, arrays, hashes
      • Constant::Export::Lazy - This module only allows you to declare function symbol constants, akin to the constant module by defining functions that are only called as needed. The interface is rather complex
      • Constant::Exporter - This module only allows you to declare function symbol constants, akin to the constant module, although you can specify tags
      • constant::string - Perl pragma to declare constants whose values are their own names
      • Exporter::Constants - This module only allows you to declare function symbol constants, akin to the constant module, without tags
      • MooseX::SetOnce - Write-once, read-many attributes for Moose
      • MooseX::Types::Ro - Moose type constraints for read-only containers
      • MooX::Const - Syntactic sugar for constant and write-once Moo(se) attributes
      • Readonly - Facility for creating read-only scalars, arrays, hashes
      • Notes for Readonly!
        • But see Const::XS for speed tests
      • Readonly::Tiny - Simple, correct readonly values
      • Readonly::Values::Boolean - Boolean Constants
      • Readonly::Values::Days - Days Constants
      • Readonly::Values::Months - Months Constants
      • Sub::Trigger::Lock - A coderef for use in Moose triggers that will lock hashrefs and arrayrefs
      • Types::Const - Types that coerce references to read-only
      • Types::ReadOnly - Type constraints and coercions for read-only data structures and locked hashes
    • CPAN
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • For the CPAN Testers wiki
        • For Docker
        • For Docker Compose
        • https://fastapi.metacpan.org
        • https://github.com/metacpan/metacpan-api
        • https://github.com/metacpan/metacpan-docker
        • https://github.com/metacpan/metacpan-web
        • https://keepachangelog.com/
        • https://metacpan.org
        • https://perlmaven.com/pinto-tutorial
        • https://pinguinorodriguez.cl/blog/bundling-vendored-modules/
      • App::CPAN::Dependents - Recursively find all reverse dependencies for a distribution or module
      • App::CPAN::Get - Tool to fetch module from CPAN archive
      • App::CPAN::Mini::Visit - Explore each distribution in a minicpan repository
      • App::CPAN::Search - Base class for cpan-search script
      • App::CpanDak - Cpanm, with some sort of distroprefs
      • App::cpangitify - Convert cpan distribution from BackPAN to a git repository
      • Notes for App::cpangitify!
        • See also Git::CPAN::Patch
      • App::CPANIDX - Queryable web-based CPAN Index
      • Acme::CPANAuthors - We are CPAN authors
      • Acme::CPANAuthors::InMemoriam - Remembering our fallen heroes
      • Acme::CPANAuthors::Not - We are not CPAN authors
      • App::cpm - A fast CPAN module installer
      • App::cpx - Install and execute CPAN package binaries
      • App::lcpan - Manage your local CPAN mirror
      • App::makefilepl2cpanfile - Convert Makefile.PL to a cpanfile automatically
      • App::MechaCPAN - Mechanize the installation of CPAN things
      • App::pause - A CLI for PAUSE
      • App::PAUSE::CheckPerms - Check if PAUSE permissions are consistent for all modules in a dist
      • App::PAUSE::Comaint - Make someone co-maint of your module on PAUSE/CPAN
      • App::sourcepan - Modulino implementation of soucepan
      • Notes for App::sourcepan!
        • Contains sourcepan, to fetch source archives from CPAN
      • Changes - Changes file management
      • Notes for Changes!
        • Subclasses: Changes::Change, Changes::Group, Changes::NewLine, Changes::Release, Changes::Version
      • CPAN - Query, download and build perl modules from CPAN sites
      • CPAN::Audit - Audit CPAN distributions for known vulnerabilities
      • CPAN::Audit::DB - The CPAN Security Advisory data as a Perl data structure, mostly for CPAN::Audit
      • CPAN::Changes - Parser for CPAN style change logs
      • Notes for CPAN::Changes!
        • Contains CPAN::Changes::Spec
      • CPAN::Changes::Parser - Parse a CPAN Changelog file
      • CPAN::Changes::Parser::KeepAChangeLog - Parser for Keep a Changelog formatted files
      • CPAN::Common::Index - Common library for searching CPAN modules, authors and distributions
      • CPAN::DistnameInfo - Extract distribution name and version from a distribution filename
      • CPAN::Distroprefs - Read and match distroprefs
      • CPAN::Faker - Build a bogus CPAN instance for testing
      • CPAN::FindDependencies - Find dependencies for modules on the CPAN
      • CPAN::Index::File::Whois - Interface to 00whois.xml
      • CPAN::IndexPod - Index the POD from an unpacked CPAN
      • CPAN::Info::FromURL - Extract/guess information from a URL
      • Notes for CPAN::Info::FromURL!
        • Contains CPAN::Author::FromURL, CPAN::Dist::FromURL, CPAN::Module::FromURL, CPAN::Release::FromURL
      • CPAN::InGit - Manage custom CPAN trees to pin versions for your projects
      • CPAN::Maker - Create a CPAN distribution
      • CPAN::Maker::Bootstrapper - Scaffold a new CPAN distribution in one command
      • Notes for CPAN::Maker::Bootstrapper!
        • Contains CPAN::Maker::ConfigReader etc
      • CPAN::Meta::X - Catalog of custom (x_*) keys in CPAN distribution metadata being used in the wild
      • CPAN::MetaCurator - Manage the cpan.metacurator.sqlite database
      • CPAN::MetaPackager - Manage the cpan.metapackager.sqlite database
      • CPAN::Mini - Create a minimal mirror of CPAN
      • CPAN::Mini::FromList - Create a minimal CPAN mirror from a list of modules you specify
      • CPAN::Mini::Inject - Inject modules into a CPAN::Mini mirror.
      • CPAN::Mirror::Tiny - Create partial CPAN mirror (a.k.a. DarkPAN
      • CPAN::Nearest - Find the nearest module to a given name
      • CPAN::PackageDetails - Create or read 02packages.details.txt.gz
      • CPAN::ParseDistribution - Index a file from the BackPAN
      • CPAN::Perl::Releases - Mapping Perl releases on CPAN to the location of the tarballs
      • CPAN::Perl::Releases::MetaCPAN - Mapping Perl releases on CPAN to the location of the tarballs via MetaCPAN API
      • CPAN::Plugin::Sysdeps - A CPAN.pm plugin for installing system dependencies
      • CPAN::Reporter - Adds CPAN Testers reporting to CPAN.pm
      • CPAN::Repository - API to access a directory which can be served as CPAN repository
      • CPAN::Repository::Packages - Parse 02packages
      • CPAN::Repository::Perms - Parse 06perms
      • Notes for CPAN::Repository::Perms!
        • See Parse::CPAN::Perms below
      • CPAN::Requirements::Dynamic - Dynamic prerequisites in meta files
      • CPAN::RPM - A Perl module packager
      • CPAN::Search::Lite - Setup and maintain a searchable CPAN database
      • CPAN::Search::Tester - Interface to search CPAN module tester
      • CPAN::Source - CPAN source list data aggregator
      • CPAN::SQLite - Maintain and search a minimal CPAN database
      • CPAN::Tarball::Patch - Patch a CPAN tarball, via CPAN::Distroprefs mechanism
      • CPAN::Testers::Schema - Schema for CPANTesters database processed from test reports
      • CPAN::Testers::ParseReport - Parse reports to www.cpantesters.org from various sources
      • CPAN::UnsupportedFinder - Identify unsupported or poorly maintained CPAN modules
      • CPAN::Uploader - Upload things to the CPAN
      • CPAN::02Packages::Search - Search packages in 02packages.details.txt
      • CPANPLUS - API & CLI access to the CPAN mirrors
      • Notes for CPANPLUS!
        • Has many sub-modules
      • CPANPLUS::Backend - Programmer's interface to CPANPLUS
      • CPANPLUS::Config - Configuration defaults and heuristics for CPANPLUS
      • CPANPLUS::Configure - Configuration for CPANPLUS
      • CPANPLUS::Dist::Debora - Create Debian or RPM packages from Perl modules
      • CPANPLUS::Module - CPAN module objects for CPANPLUS
      • CPANSA::DB - The CPAN Security Advisory data as a Perl data structure, mostly for CPAN::Audit
      • DarkPAN::Utils - A set of utilities for working with a DarkPAN
      • Git::CPAN::Patch - Patch CPAN modules using Git
      • Notes for Git::CPAN::Patch!
        • See also App::cpangitify
      • Menlo - A CPAN client
      • MetaCPAN - Running the MetaCPAN stack with Docker
      • MetaCPAN::API - A comprehensive, DWIM-featured API to MetaCPAN - Deprecated
      • Notes for MetaCPAN::API!
        • See MetaCPAN::Client
      • MetaCPAN::Client - A comprehensive, DWIM-featured client to the MetaCPAN API
      • MetaCPAN::Pod::HTML - Format Pod as HTML for MetaCPAN
      • MetaCPAN::Role - A collection of roles useful to MetaCPAN project
      • Module::Metadata - Gather package and POD information from perl module file
      • Module::Metadata::Changes - Manage machine-readable Changes/CHANGES/Changelog.ini files
      • Notes for Module::Metadata::Changes!
        • Contains Ron's simple home-grown change file manager called ini.report.pl
      • Net::API::CPAN - A client to issue queries to the MetaCPAN REST API
      • OrePAN2 - Yet another DarkPAN manager
      • OrePAN2::S3 - This script is used to add distributions to your own DarkPAN repository housed on Amazon's S3 storage
      • OrePAN2::Server - DarkPAN Server
      • Package::DeprecationManager - Manage deprecation warnings for your distribution
      • Parse::CPAN::Authors - Parse 01mailrc.txt.gz
      • Parse::CPAN::Meta - Parse META.yml and META.json CPAN metadata files
      • Parse::CPAN::Packages - Parse 02packages.details.txt.gz
      • Notes for Parse::CPAN::Packages!
        • But see https://metacpan.org/pod/Parse::CPAN::Packages
      • Parse::CPAN::Perms - Parse 06perms.txt.gz
      • Notes for Parse::CPAN::Perms!
        • See CPAN::Repository::Perms above
      • Parse::CPAN::Packages::Fast - Parse CPAN's package index
      • Parse::CPAN::Whois - Parse CPAN's authors/00whois.xml file
      • Parse::Distname - Parse a distribution name
      • PAUSE::Packages - Interface to PAUSE's packages file (02packages.details.txt)
      • PAUSE::Permissions - Interface to PAUSE's module permissions file (06perms.txt)
      • PAUSE::Users - Interface to PAUSE's users file (00whois.xml)
      • Release::Checklist - A QA checklist for CPAN releases
      • WorePAN - Creates a partial CPAN mirror for tests
    • CpanFile
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
      • CPAN::Audit - Audit CPAN distributions for known vulnerabilities
      • Notes for CPAN::Audit!
        • This module contains cpan-audit, which uses a cpanfile or a cpanfile.snapshot file
    • cron
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
      • BPM::Engine - Business Process Execution Engine
      • BPM::XPDL - XPDL access
      • Chronic - A constraints-based, opportunistic, application-level scheduler
      • Cron::Toolkit - Quartz-compatible cron parser with unique extensions and over 400 tests
      • Schedule::Activity - Generate random activity schedules
      • Schedule::Cron - cron-like scheduler for Perl subroutines. Use it to create a daemon
      • Schedule::Easing - Scheduled ramp-up and linear activation of events
      • Schedule::LongSteps - Manage long term processes over arbitrary large spans of time
