        • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PESEL
      • App::SahUtils - Collection of CLI utilities for Sah and Data::Sah
      • Attribute::Signature - Allows you to define a call signature for subroutines
      • Attribute::Validate - Validate your subs with attributes
      • Alt::Params::Util::PLICEASE - Simple, compact and correct param-checking functions
      • Notes for Alt::Params::Util::PLICEASE!
        • Contains Params::Util
      • Brick - Complex business rule data validation
      • Notes for Brick!
        • See also Brick::Tutorial, Brick::UserGuide
      • CGI::ArgChecker - An extensible CGI parameter validation module (allowing commonly used checks on parameters to be called more concisely and consistently)
      • CGI::FormMagick::Validator - Validate data from FormMagick forms
      • CGI::Validate - Advanced CGI form parser and type validation
      • Class::ParmList - Methods for processing named parameter lists
      • Class::Utils - Class utilities
      • Data::Censor - Censor sensitive stuff in a data structure
      • Data::Checks - Value constraint checking
      • Data::Constraint - Prototypical value checking
      • Data::Domain - Data description and validation
      • Notes for Data::Domain!
        • See also CGI::Expand and Test::InDomain
      • Data::FormValidator - Validates user input (usually from an HTML form) based on input profile
      • Notes for Data::FormValidator!
        • http://mark.stosberg.com/Tech/perl/form-validation/
      • Data::FormValidator::Tutorial - A tutorial!
      • Data::FormValidator::Util::HTML - HTML utility functions for DFV
      • Data::Message::Simple - Data object for simple message
      • Data::Processor - A tool for transforming, verifying, and producing Perl data structures from / against a schema, defined as a Perl data structure
      • Data::Sah - Fast and featureful data structure validation
      • Notes for Data::Sah!
        • It's See Also contains a long list of validators
      • Data::Sah::Coerce - Coercion rules for Data::Sah
      • Data::Sah::CoerceCommon - Common stuffs for Data::Sah::Coerce and Data::Sah::CoerceJS
      • Data::Sah::CoerceJS - Generate coercer code
      • Data::Sah::Tiny - Validate Sah schemas with as little code as possibl
      • Data::Transmute - Transmute (transform) data structure using rules data
      • Data::Validate - Common data validation methods
      • Data::Validate::Domain - Domain and host name validation
      • Data::Validate::IP - IPv4 and IPv6 validation methods
      • Data::Validate::Sanctions - Validate a name against sanctions lists
      • Data::Verifier - Profile based data verification with Moose type constraints
      • Devel::CallChecker - Custom op checking attached to subroutines
      • Notes for Devel::CallChecker!
        • But see Devel::CallParser
      • Devel::CallParser - Custom parsing attached to subroutines
      • File::LocalizeNewlines - Localize the newlines for one or more files
      • Notes for File::LocalizeNewlines!
        • See also PerlIO::eol
      • Flat::Profile - Streaming-first profiling for CSV/TSV flat files
      • Flat::Schema - Deterministic schema contracts for flat files
      • Func::Util - Functional programming utilities with XS/OP acceleration
      • Getargs::Long - Named subroutine arguments, with optional type checking
      • Getargs::Mixed - Perl extension allowing subs to handle mixed parameter lists
      • Getopt::GetArgs - Perl module to allow enhanced argument passing, including passing of case-insensitive named arguments as well as positioned arguments
      • HTML::Template::Associate::FormValidator - HTML::Template::Associate Data::FormValidator plugin
      • Identifier::PL::PESEL - Validator for polish PESEL number
      • kura - Store constraints for Data::Checks, Type::Tiny, Moose and more
      • Kwalify - Kwalify schema for data structures
      • Method::Signatures - Method and function declarations with signatures and no source filter
      • Method::Signatures::Simple - Basic method declarations with signatures, without source filters
      • Mo::utils - Mo utilities
      • Notes for Mo::utils!
        • Contains 19 methods for checking data properties
      • Mo::utils::Array - Mo array utilities
      • Mo::utils::CEFACT - Mo UN/CEFACT Common Code utilities
      • Mo::utils::CSS - Mo CSS utilities
      • Mo::utils::Hash - Mo hash utilities
      • Mo::utils::Language - Mo language utilities
      • Mo::utils::Number - Mo number utilities
      • Mo::utils::URI - Mo utilities for URIs
      • MooseX::Method::Signatures - Method declarations with type constraints and no source filter - deprecated
      • Mouse::Util::TypeConstraints - Type constraint system for Mouse
      • Number::ZipCode::JP - Validate Japanese zip-codes
      • OMOP::CSV::Validator - Validates OMOP CDM CSV files against their expected data types
      • Params::Check - A generic input parsing/checking mechanism
      • Params::Filter - Secure field filtering for parameter construction
      • Notes for Params::Filter!
        • Checks for presence or absence, but does not validate
      • Params::Get - Get the parameters to a subroutine in any way you want
      • Params::Sah - Validate method/function parameters using Sah schemas
      • Params::Signature - Support for parameter validation using subroutine signature, type declaration, default values, optional parameters
      • Params::Smart - Use both positional and named arguments in a subroutine - Deprecated
      • Params::SomeUtil - Simple, compact and correct param-checking functions
      • Params::Validate - Validate method/function parameters
      • Params::Validate::Strict - Validates a set of parameters against a schema
      • Notes for Params::Validate::Strict!
        • Note: Silently skips type checks for undef values
      • Params::ValidationCompiler - Build an optimized subroutine parameter validator once, use it forever
      • Notes for Params::ValidationCompiler!
        • Recommended by Dave Rolsky
      • Parse::Method::Signatures - Perl6 like method signature parser
      • PerlIO::eol - PerlIO layer for normalizing line endings
      • Notes for PerlIO::eol!
        • See also File::LocalizeNewlines
      • Poz - A simple, composable, and extensible data validation library for Perl
      • Return::Set - Return a value optionally validated against a strict schema
      • Sah - Schema for data structures (specification)
      • Sah::FAQ - Frequently asked questions
      • Sah::Schemas::DefHash - Sah schemas for DefHash
      • Sah::Schema::identifier - A string that starts with [A-Za-z_] followed by zero of more [A-Za-z0-9_]
      • Notes for Sah::Schema::identifier!
        • Contains many sub-modules
      • Sah::SchemaBundle::CrockfordBase32 - Sah schemas related to Crockford's Base 32 encoding
      • Sah::SchemaBundle::KDEActivity - Sah schemas related to KDE activities
      • Sah::Type - Standard types
      • Scalar::Util - A selection of general-utility scalar subroutines
      • Notes for Scalar::Util!
        • This module contains a dozen subroutines
      • Signature::Attribute::Checked - Apply value constraint checks to subroutine parameters
      • Smart::Args - Argument validation for you
      • String::Ident - Clean up strings for use as identifiers and in URLs
      • Sub::NamedParams - Perl extension for using named arguments with any sub
      • Valiant - Object validation inspired by Ruby on Rails and more
      • Validate::Simple - (Relatively) Simple way to validate input parameters
      • Validate::Tiny - Minimalistic data validation
    • DatesAndTimes
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • http://datetime.perl.org/
        • (2021-11-11) deprecates Date::Manip
        • For Global dates and times
        • For Date and Time Format
        • https://www.w3.org/TR/xmlschema-2/#dateTime
        • https://www.kcaran.com/posts/utc-local-date-conversion-in-perl-with-time-piece.html
        • https://www.tondering.dk/claus/calendar.html
      • App::Greple::ical - Module to support Apple macOS Calendar data
      • Algorithm::Time::ToNumber - Convert time to a number
      • App::SpreadRevolutionaryDate - Spread date and time from Revolutionary (Republican) Calendar on Bluesky, Twitter, Mastodon, Freenode and Liberachat.
      • App::week - Colorful calendar command for ANSI terminal
      • Calendar::Simple - Perl extension to create simple calendars
      • Notes for Calendar::Simple!
        • Uses DateTime if installed
      • Date - Extremely fast Date framework with timezones, microseconds, relative dates and intervals support
      • Date::Age - Return an age or age range from date(s)
      • Date::Calc - Gregorian calendar date calculations
      • Date::Calendar - Calendar objects for different holiday schemes
      • Date::Cmp - Compare two dates with approximate parsing support
      • Date::Extract - Extract probable dates from strings
      • Date::Find - Find year, month, day from (filename) strings
      • Date::Holidays - Date::Holidays::* adapter and aggregator for all your holiday needs
      • Date::Holidays::Abstract - Abstract class for Date::Holidays::* packages
      • Notes for Date::Holidays::Abstract!
        • See also Date::Holidays::Super
      • Date::Holidays::Adapter - An adapter class for Date::Holidays::* modules
      • Date::Holidays::AU - Determine Australian Public Holidays
      • Date::Holidays::BY - Determine public holidays and business days in Belarus
      • Date::Holidays::DE - Determine German holidays
      • Date::Holidays::DK - Determine Danish public holidays
      • Date::Holidays::FR - Determine French holidays
      • Date::Holidays::GB - Determine British holidays - Current UK public and bank holiday dates up to 2028
      • Date::Holidays::PT - Determine Portuguese public holidays
      • Date::Holidays::Super - A SUPER class for the Date::Holidays::* modules
      • Notes for Date::Holidays::Super!
        • See also Date::Holidays::Abstract
      • Date::Holidays::TW - Determine whether it is Taiwan Holidays or not
      • Date::Holidays::UK - Determine UK Public Holidays
      • Date::Holidays::US - Date::Holidays Adapter for US Federal holidays
      • Date::Holidays::USExtended - Provides an extended set of United States holidays
      • Date::Interval - Handling of temporal intervals based on Date::Manip
      • Date::Japanese::Era - Conversion between Japanese Era / Gregorian calendar
      • Date::Japanese::Holiday - Calculate Japanese Holiday
      • Date::Manip - Date manipulation routines
      • Date::ManipX::Almanac - Add almanac date/time functionality (sunrise, etc.) to Date::Manip
      • Date::Parse - Parse date strings into time values
      • Date::Parse::Modern - Provide string to unixtime conversions
      • Date::RangeParser::EN - Parser for plain English date/time range strings
      • Notes for Date::RangeParser::EN!
        • Extremely flexible
      • Date::Tolkien::Shire - Convert dates into the Shire Calendar
      • Date::Tolkien::Shire::Data - Data functionality for Shire calendars
      • Date::Utility - A class that represents a datetime in various formats
      • Notes for Date::Utility!
        • Has a huge number of methods
      • DateTime - A date and time object for Perl
      • DateTime::Calendar::Christian - Dates in the Christian calendar
      • DateTime::Calendar::Japanese::Era - DateTime Extension for Japanese Eras
      • DateTime::Calendar::Julian - Dates in the Julian calendar
      • DateTime::Event::Jewish::Sunrise - Calculate halachically interesting times
      • DateTime::Event::Sunrise - Perl DateTime extension for computing the sunrise/sunset on a given day
      • DateTime::Fiction::JRRTolkien::Shire - DateTime implementation of the Shire calendar
      • DateTime::Format::Builder - Create DateTime parser classes and objects
      • DateTime::Format::DateManip - Perl DateTime extension to convert Date::Manip dates and durations to DateTimes and vice versa
      • DateTime::Format::DB2 - Parse and format DB2 dates and times
      • DateTime::Format::Duration - Format and parse DateTime::Durations
      • DateTime::Format::Flexible - Flexibly parse strings and turn them into DateTime objects
      • DateTime::Format::ICal - Parse and format iCal datetime and duration strings
      • DateTime::Format::Intl - A Web Intl.DateTimeFormat Class Implementation
      • Notes for DateTime::Format::Intl!
        • This is a big module with a huge range of features
      • DateTime::Format::ISO8601 - Parses ISO8601 formats
      • DateTime::Format::JP - Japanese DateTime Parser and Formatter
      • DateTime::Format::Lite - Parse and format datetimes with strptime patterns, returning DateTime::Lite objects
      • DateTime::Format::Mail - Convert between DateTime and RFC2822/822 formats
      • Notes for DateTime::Format::Mail!
        • See TheRFCGuide for rfc2822
      • DateTime::Format::MySQL - Parse and format MySQL dates and times
      • DateTime::Format::Natural - Parse informal natural language date/time strings
      • Notes for DateTime::Format::Natural!
        • Note: This package contains various modules, including DateTime::Format::Natural::Lang::EN, which provides the English specific grammar and variables
      • DateTime::Format::DateParse - Parses Date::Parse compatible formats
      • DateTime::Format::RelativeTime - A Web Intl.RelativeTimeFormat Class Implementation
      • DateTime::Format::Strptime - Parse and format strp and strf time patterns
      • Notes for DateTime::Format::Strptime!
        • But see DateTime::Format::Lite
      • DateTime::Format::Text - Find a Date in Text
      • DateTime::Format::Unicode - Unicode CLDR Formatter for DateTime
      • DateTime::Format::W3CDTF - Parse and format W3CDTF datetime strings
      • DateTime::HiRes - Create DateTime objects with sub-second current time resolution
      • DateTime::Lite - Lightweight, low-dependency drop-in replacement for DateTime
      • DateTime::Locale - Localization support for DateTime.pm
      • DateTime::Locale::FromCLDR - DateTime Localised Data from Unicode CLDR
      • DateTime::Ordinal - The great new DateTime::Ordinal!
      • DateTime::Schedule - Determine scheduled days in range based on exclusions
      • DateTime::Set - Datetime sets and set math
      • DateTime::SpanSet - Set of DateTime spans
      • DateTime::TimeZone - Time zone object base class and factory
      • Notes for DateTime::TimeZone!
        • Normally use via DateTime above
      • DateTime::Tiny - A date object, with as little code as possible
      • DateTimeX::Easy - Parse a date/time string using the best method available
      • DateTimeX::Format::Ago - I should have written this module "3 years ago"
      • Email::Date - Find and Format Date Headers
      • Email::Date::Format - Produce RFC 2822 date strings
      • Notes for Email::Date::Format!
        • See TheRFCGuide for rfc2282
      • HTML::CalendarMonth - Generate and manipulate HTML calendar months
      • HTML::CalendarMonthSimple - Perl Module for Generating HTML Calendars
      • Notes for HTML::CalendarMonthSimple!
        • But use the previous module
      • HTTP::Date - Date conversion routines
      • Month::Simple - Simple month-based date arithmetic
      • Net::CalDAVTalk - Module to talk CalDAV and give a JSON interface to the data
      • Net::CardDAVTalk - A library for talking to CardDAV servers
      • Oxford::Calendar - University of Oxford calendar conversion routines
      • POSIX::RT::Signal - POSIX Real-time signal handling functions
      • POSIX::RT::Timer - POSIX real-time timers
      • Notes for POSIX::RT::Timer!
        • Contains POSIX::RT::Clock
      • SIRTX::Datecode - Module for interacting with SIRTX Datecodes
      • Text::JSCalendar - Convert between iCalendar * JSCalendar
      • Text::JSContact - Bidirectional vCard <=> JSContact conversion
      • Time::AutoRes - Use Time::HiRes or fall back to core code
      • TimeDate - Date formating subroutines
      • Time::Duration - Rounded or exact English expression of durations. Built-in
      • Time::Duration::LocaleObject - Time duration string in language chosen by an object
      • Time::Duration::Object - Time::Duration, but an object
      • Time::Duration::Parse - Parse string that represents time duration
      • Time::Duration::Parse::More - Parse natural language time duration expressions
      • Time::Elapsed - This module transforms the elapsed seconds into a human readable string
      • Time::HiRes - High resolution alarm, sleep, gettimeofday, interval timers
      • Time::HiRes::Sleep::Until - Provides common ways to sleep until...
      • Time::HiRes::Value - Represent a time value or interval in exact microseconds
      • Time::JulianDay - Julian calendar manipulations
      • Time::Left - Object model for time limits
      • Time::Local - Efficiently compute time from local and GMT time
      • Time::Moment - Represents a date and time of day with an offset from UTC
      • Time::Nanos - Nanosecond time resolution via clock_gettime()
      • Time::OlsonTZ::Data - Olson timezone data
      • Time::ParseDate - Date parsing both relative and absolute
      • Time::Piece - Object Oriented time objects. Built-in
      • Notes for Time::Piece!
        • Includes Time::Seconds
      • Time::Precise - Extending standard time related functions to always include nanoseconds and always use full year
      • Time::Spec - A wrapper around struct timespec
      • Time::Str - Parse and format date/time strings in multiple standard formats
      • Time::TAI64 - Perl extension for converting TAI64 strings into standard unix timestamps
      • TimeZone::TimeZoneDB - Interface to https://timezonedb.com for looking up Timezone data
      • Vigil::Calendar - Provides a way to describe a calendar _month so that you can easily build HTML calendars or populate your flavour of web calendar
    • DebianOS
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • My home page http://savage.net.au/index.html for the Debian.Wiki
      • Container::Builder - Build Container archives
      • Dpkg - Module with core variables
      • DPKG::Packages::Parser - Parses the Debian Packages file
    • DebuggingStuff
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
      • Carp::Always - Warns and dies noisily with stack backtraces
      • Carp::Object - https://metacpan.org/pod/Carp::Object
      • Data::Dump - Can be used by Struct::Dumb. See DataTypes
      • Notes for Data::Dump!
        • See also Data::Pretty
      • Data::Dump::Color - Like Data::Dump, but with color
      • Data::Dump::HTML::Collapsible - Dump Perl data structures as HTML document with collapsible sections
      • Data::Dump::Partial - Dump data structure compactly and potentially partially
      • Data::Dumper - Stringified perl data structures, suitable for both printing and eval
      • Notes for Data::Dumper!
        • See Data::TreeDumper, Tree::Simple::View & the latter's See Also
      • Data::Dumper::Compact - Vertically compact width-limited data formatter
      • Data::Dumper::Concise - Less indentation and newlines plus sub deparsing
      • Data::Dumper::Interp - Interpolate Data::Dumper output into strings for human consumption
      • Data::Dumper::Simple - Easily dump variables with names
      • Data::Printer - Colored & full-featured pretty print of Perl data structures and objects
      • Debug::CodeBlock - Add DEBUG codeblocks to your code
      • Debug::Comments - Source filter which turns comments into log messages
      • Debug::Easy - A Handy Debugging Module With Colorized Output and Formatting
      • Debug::Filter::PrintExpr - Convert comment lines to debug print statements
      • Devel::Assert - Assertions for Perl >= 5.14
      • Notes for Devel::Assert!
        • See also assertions
      • Devel::Bug - Transparent inline debugging probe (pure Perl)
      • Devel::EndStats - Display run time and dependencies after running code
      • Devel::MAT::Dumper - Write a heap dump file for later analysis
      • Devel::OverloadInfo - Introspect overloaded operators
      • Devel::ptkdb - Perl debugger using a Tk GUI
      • Devel::REPL - A modern perl interactive shell
      • Notes for Devel::REPL!
        • Unfortunately, I (author of Runtime::Debugger) did not find a way to get the lexical variables in a scope
        • See also Shell::Perl
      • Devel::StackTrace - An object representing a stack trace
      • Devel::StrictMode - Determine whether strict (but slow) tests should be enabled
      • Devel::Trace::Subs - Generate, track, store and print code flow and stack traces
      • Dump::Krumo - Fancy, colorful, human readable dumps of your data
      • Loo - Pure XS data introspector and code deparser with customisable colour output
      • perl - perl5db.pl - The standard perl debugger
      • Reply - Read, eval, print, loop, yay!
      • Notes for Reply!
        • Same problem as Devel::REPL
      • Runtime::Debugger - Easy to use REPL with existing lexical support and DWIM tab completion
      • Smart::Comments - Comments that do more than just sit there
    • DFA
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Deterministic_finite_automaton
      • App::GUI::Cellgraph - Draw pattern by cellular automata
      • App::GUI::GoLP - A GUI for viewing Life-like cellular automata, in Perl/Prima
      • Data::FSM - Data object for Finite State Machine
      • DFA::Kleene - Kleene's Algorithm for Deterministic Finite Automata
      • DFA::Statemap - This is the SMC (State Machine Compiler) runtime for target language Perl
      • Dict::FSA - This module is a perl wrapper around fsa
      • Notes for Dict::FSA!
        • See http://www.eti.pg.gda.pl/~jandac/fsa.html
      • FLAT - Formal Language & Automata Toolkit
      • FSA::Engine - A Moose Role to convert an object into a Finite State Machine
      • FSM::Basic - Finite state machine using HASH as state definitions
      • FSA::Rules - Build simple rules-based state machines in Perl
      • Parse::FSM - Deterministic top-down parser based on a Finite State Machine
      • Set::FA - A Set of Discrete Finite Automata
      • Notes for Set::FA!
        • Contains Set::FA::Element, a single Discrete Finite Automaton
    • DistZilla
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
      • Dist::Zilla - Distribution builder; installer not included!
      • Dist::Zilla::Plugin::GatherDir::Template - Gather all the files in a directory and use them as templates
      • Dist::Zilla::Plugin::GenerateFile - Build a custom file from only the plugin configuration
      • Dist::Zilla::Plugin::GenerateFile::FromShareDir - Create files in the repository or build, using a sharedir template
      • Dist::Zilla::Plugin::GitHub::CreateRelease - Create a GitHub Release
      • Dist::Zilla::Plugin::ShareDir - Install a directory's contents as "ShareDir" content
    • DnsStuff
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • https://c-ares.org/
        • For DNS Robot: 53 free online DNS and network tools
        • https://publicsuffix.org/list/public_suffix_list.dat
      • Alien::cares - Find or build the c-ares async DNS resolver library
      • AnyEvent::DNS::EtcHosts - Use /etc/hosts before DNS
      • App::sdseasydyn - EasyDNS DDNS updater (CLI-first App distribution)
      • App::sslmaker - Be your own SSL certificate authority
      • Data::DNS - An interface to the DNS root zone database
      • DNS::Robot - Perl client for the DNS Robot API (dnsrobot.net)
      • EV::cares - High-performance async DNS resolver using c-ares and EV
      • Net::DNS - Perl Interface to the Domain Name System
      • Net::DNS::Native - Non-blocking system DNS resolver
      • Net::DNS::Paranoid - Paranoid dns resolver
      • Notes for Net::DNS::Paranoid!
        • Can use with Furl::HTTP
        • If using LWP, should wrap Net::DNS::Paranoid module with LWPx::ParanoidHandler
      • Net::DNS::Resolver::Unbound - Net::DNS resolver based on libunbound
      • Net::DNS::SEC - DNSSEC extensions to Net::DNS
      • Net::Domain - Attempt to evaluate the current host's internet name and domain
      • Net::Domain::Parts - Extract sub-domain, domain and TLD parts of a domain name.
      • Notes for Net::Domain::Parts!
        • Meaning 'www.perlmonks.org' => Domain: perlmonks.org, Subdomain: www, TLD: org
      • Net::Domain::TLD - Work with TLD names
      • Net::IDN::Encode - Internationalizing Domain Names in Applications (IDNA)
      • Notes for Net::IDN::Encode!
        • See TheRFCGuide for rfc3492, rfc5890
        • See also URI in [[HTTPHandling]]
      • Net::IDN::PP - A pure-Perl implementation of the Punycode algorithm for encoding internationalized domain names (IDNs)
      • Net::PublicSuffixList - The Mozilla Public Suffix List
      • Net::SSLeay - Perl bindings for OpenSSL and LibreSSL
      • OpenDNS::MyIP - Get your public IP address
      • WWW::DNSMadeEasy - Accessing DNSMadeEasy API
      • Zonemaster::Backend - A system for running Zonemaster tests asynchronously through an RPC-API
      • Zonemaster::CLI - Run Zonemaster tests from the command line
      • Zonemaster::Engine - A tool to check the quality of a DNS zone
      • Zonemaster::LDNS - Perl wrapper for the ldns DNS library
    • EBookHandling
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
      • App::EbookUtils - Command-line utilities related to ebooks

      • EBook::Gutenberg - Fetch ebooks from Project Gutenberg
      • EBook::Ishmael - Convert ebook documents to plain text

        • Formats supported
      • FB3::Convert - Scripts and API for converting FB3 from and to different formats
    • EmailStuff
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • JMAP rather than IMAP: https://jmap.io/
        • EAV::XS (Address validation)
        • For Email::* & Mail::*
      • App::mailmake - App harness for the mailmake CLI
      • Notes for App::mailmake!
        • See Mail::Make below
      • App::Milter::Limit - Sendmail Milter that limits message rate by sender
      • App::Milter::Limit::Plugin::BerkeleyDB - BerkeleyDB driver for App::Milter::Limit
      • App::Milter::Limit::Plugin::SQLite - SQLite driver for App::Milter::Limit
      • App::wsgetmail - Fetch mail from the cloud using webservices
      • Data::JSEmail - Convert between Email::MIME and the JSON format from RFC 8621 (JMAP Mail)
      • Email::Abstract - Unified interface to mail representations
      • Email::Abuse::Investigator - Analyse spam email to identify originating hosts, hosted URLs, and suspicious domains
      • Email::Address - RFC 2822 Address Parsing and Creation
      • Email::Address::List - RFC close address list parsing
      • Email::Mailer - Multi-purpose emailer for HTML, auto-text, attachments, and templates
      • Notes for Email::Mailer!
        • Modelled on Email::Simple
        • Looks good
      • Email::MIME - Easy MIME message handling
      • Encode::MIME::Header - MIME encoding for an unstructured email header
      • Email::Send::YYClouds - Send simple mail using smtp relay server
      • Email::Sender - A library for sending email
      • Notes for Email::Sender!
        • This module has a huge list of contributors
      • Email::Sender::Transport::Mailgun - Mailgun transport for Email::Sender
      • Email::Sender::Transport::SMTP - Send email over SMTP
      • Notes for Email::Sender::Transport::SMTP!
        • Replaces Email::Sender::Transport::SMTPS
      • Email::Sender::Transport::SMTP::Persistent - An SMTP client that stays online
      • Notes for Email::Sender::Transport::SMTP::Persistent!
        • The stock Email::Sender::Transport::SMTP reconnects each time it sends a message. This transport only reconnects when the existing connection fails
      • Email::Simple - Simple parsing of RFC2822 message format and headers
      • Notes for Email::Simple!
        • See TheRFCGuide for rfc2822
      • Email::Stuffer - A more casual approach to creating and sending Email:: emails
      • Email::Valid - Check validity of Internet email addresses
      • Encode::IMAPUTF7 - Modification of UTF-7 encoding for IMAP
      • Exim - Supercedes sendmail, under Linux at least
      • MailTools - Bundle of ancient email modules
      • Notes for MailTools!
        • Includes Mail::Internet
        • If you start writing a new application, you should use the Mail::Box distribution, which has more features and handles messages much better according to the RFCs
      • Mail::AuthenticationResults - Object Oriented Authentication-Results Headers
      • Mail::BIMI - Brand Indicators for Message Identification (BIMI) retrieval, validation, and processing
      • Mail::Box - Manage a mailbox, a folder with messages
      • Mail::Box::IMAP4 - Handle IMAP4 folders as client
      • Mail::Box::Parser::C - Parsing folders for MailBox with C routines
      • Mail::Box::Parser::Perl - Reading messages from file using Perl
      • Mail::Box::POP3 - Handle POP3 folders as client
      • Mail::DKIM::Iterator - Iterative DKIM validation or signing
      • Mail::DMARC - Perl implementation of DMARC
      • Mail::DMARC::PurePerl - Pure Perl implementation of DMARC
      • Mail::DWIM - Do-What-I-Mean Mailer
      • Mail::Exim::ACL::Geolocation - Map IP addresses to location information
      • Mail::IMAPClient - An IMAP Client API
      • Mail::IMAPTalk - IMAP client interface with lots of features
      • Mail::Make - Strict, Fluent MIME Email Builder
      • Mail::Message - General message object
      • Mail::Milter::Authentication - A Perl Mail Authentication Milter
      • Mail::POP3Client - Perl 5 module to talk to a POP3 (RFC1939) server
      • Notes for Mail::POP3Client!
        • See TheRFCGuide for rfc1939
      • Mail::Reporter - Base-class and error reporter for Mail::Box
      • Mail::Sendmail - Simple platform independent mailer
      • Mail::SpamAssassin::Plugin::DBSCORE - Spamassassin scores from database
      • Mail::SPF - An object-oriented implementation of Sender Policy Framework
      • Mail::Transport - Use Mail Transfer Agents (MTAs)
      • MailboxValidator::SingleValidation - Email verification module for Perl using MailboxValidator API
      • MIME::Base32 - Base32 encoder and decoder
      • MIME::Base32::XS - Encoding and decoding Base32
      • MIME::Base64 - Encoding and decoding of base64 strings
      • Notes for MIME::Base64!
        • http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Base64#URL_applications
      • MIME::Charset - Charset Information for MIME
      • MIME::EncWords - Deal with RFC 2047 encoded words (improved)
      • Notes for MIME::EncWords!
        • See TheRFCGuide for rfc2047
      • MIME::Entity - Class for parsed-and-decoded MIME message
      • Notes for MIME::Entity!
        • Before reading further, you should see MIME::Tools to make sure that you understand where this module fits into the grand scheme of things
      • MIME::Lite - Low-calorie MIME generator
      • Notes for MIME::Lite!
        • There are a number of alternatives, like Email::MIME or MIME::Entity and Email::Sender, which you should probably use instead
        • Actually, just start with Mail::Box
      • MIME::Mini - Minimal code to parse/create mbox files and mail messages
      • MIME::QuotedPrint - Encoding and decoding of quoted-printable strings
      • MIME-tools - Modules for parsing (and creating!) MIME entities
      • Notes for MIME-tools!
        • POD contains 2 important diagrams
      • MIME::Types - Definition of MIME types "MIME Type" is the old name for "Media Type"
      • Net::IMAP::Client - Not so simple IMAP client library
      • Net::SimpleMail - A simple module to send emails via simplemail.co.in
      • Net::MitDK - Perl API for http://mit.dk/
      • Notes for Net::MitDK!
        • https://metacpan.org/release/KARASIK/Net-MitDK-0.07/source/README.md - For English docs
      • Net::validMX - Use DNS and/or regular expressions to verify if an email address could be valid
      • Qmail::Deliverable - Determine deliverability of local addresses
      • Sendmail::PMilter - Perl binding of Sendmail Milter protocol
      • Sieve::Generator - Generate Sieve email filter scripts
      • Sisimai - Analyze bounce mails
      • WWW::MailboxOrg - Perl client for Mailbox.org API

        • For Promises/A+
      • Alien::liburing - Alien wrapper for liburing
      • Algorithm::Cron - Abstract implementation of the cron(8) scheduling algorithm
      • Algorithm::RateLimiter::TokenBucket - Loop-agnostic Token Bucket Rate Limiter
      • AnyEvent - The DBI of event loop programming
      • Notes for AnyEvent!
        • Contains AE, a simpler/faster/newer/cooler AnyEvent API
      • AnyEvent::FTP - Simple asynchronous FTP client and server
      • AnyEvent::Gearman - Asynchronous Gearman client/worker module for AnyEvent applications
      • AnyEvent::Impl::Prima - Prima event loop adapter for AnyEvent
      • AnyEvent::ITM - Debug ITM/SWD stream deserializer for AnyEvent
      • AnyEvent::Net::Curl::Queued - Moo wrapper for queued downloads via Net::Curl & AnyEvent
      • AnyEvent::Promises - Simple implementation of Promises/A+ spec
      • AnyEvent::Util - Various utility functions
      • AnyEvent::XSPromises - Another Promises library, this time implemented in XS for performance
      • AnyEvent::YACurl - Yet Another curl binding for AnyEvent
      • Async::Event::Interval - Scheduled and one-off restartable asynchronous events
      • Conduit - Serve HTTP with Future::IO
      • Devel::Events - Extensible instrumentation framework
      • EV - Perl interface to libev, a high performance full-featured event loop
      • EV::ClickHouse - Async ClickHouse client using EV
      • EV::Etcd - Async etcd v3 client using native gRPC and EV/libev
      • EV::Future - Minimalist and high-performance async control flow for EV
      • EV::Gearman - Asynchronous Gearman client and worker on libev
      • EV::Hiredis - Asynchronous redis client using hiredis and EV
      • Notes for EV::Hiredis!
        • See also EV::Redis below
      • EV::Kafka - High-performance asynchronous Kafka/Redpanda client using EV
      • EV::MariaDB - Async MariaDB/MySQL client using libmariadb and EV
      • EV::Memcached - High-performance asynchronous memcached client using EV
      • EV::Nats - High-performance asynchronous NATS client using EV
      • EV::Pg - Asynchronous PostgreSQL client using libpq and EV
      • EV::Redis - Asynchronous redis client using hiredis and EV
      • Notes for EV::Redis!
        • Fork of EV::Hiredis
      • EV::Websockets - WebSocket client/server using libwebsockets and EV
      • Event::RPC - Event based transparent Client/Server RPC framework
      • Feersum - A PSGI engine for Perl based on EV/libev
      • Future - Represent an operation awaiting completion
      • Future::AsyncAwait - Deferred subroutine syntax for futures
      • Future::Batch - Process multiple Future-returning operations with concurrency control
      • Future::Batch::XS - XS implementation of batch processing for Future operations
      • Future::IO - Future-returning IO methods
      • Future::IO::Impl::Glib - Implement Future::IO using Glib
      • Future::IO::Impl::KQueue - Implement Future::IO using kqueue(2)
      • Future::IO::Impl::Ppoll - Implement Future::IO using ppoll(2)
      • Future::IO::Impl::Tickit - Implement Future::IO with Tickit
      • Future::IO::Impl::Uring - A Future::IO implementation for IO::Uring
      • Future::IO::Impl::UV - Implement Future::IO using UV
      • Future::IO::Resolver - Name resolver methods for Future::IO
      • Future::IO::TLS - A TLS interface for Future::IO
      • Future::Queue - A FIFO queue of values that uses Futures
      • Future::Selector - Manage a collection of pending futures
      • Future::Uring - Future-returning io_uring functions
      • Future::Utils - Utility functions for working with Future objects
      • Future::XS - Experimental XS implementation of Future
      • IO - Object-style access to files, pipes, etc
      • IO::All - IO::All to Larry Wall!
      • Notes for IO::All!
        • The Swiss Army knife of file ops
      • IO::AIO - Asynchronous/Advanced Input/Output
      • IO::AIO::LoadLimited - A tiny IO::AIO extension that allows to load multiple files
      • IO::AIO::Util - Useful functions missing from IO::AIO
      • IO::Any - Open anything
      • IO::Async - Asynchronous event-driven programming
      • Notes for IO::Async!
        • This module has an interesting list of modules in See Also
      • IO::Async::Handle - Event callbacks for a non-blocking file descriptor
      • IO::Async::Loop - Core loop of the IO::Async framework
      • IO::Async::Loop::Epoll - Use IO::Async with epoll on Linux
      • IO::Async::Loop::Epoll::FD - Use IO::Async with Epoll and special filehandles
      • IO::Async::Loop::EV - Use IO::Async with EV
      • IO::Async::Loop::FutureIO - Use IO::Async with Future::IO
      • IO::Async::Loop::Poll - Use IO::Async with poll(2)
      • IO::Async::Loop::Ppoll - Use IO::Async with ppoll(2)
      • IO::Async::Loop::Uring - Use IO::Async with IO::Uring
      • IO::Async::Notifier - Base class for IO::Async event objects
      • IO::Async::Process - Start and manage a child process
      • IO::Async::SSL - Use SSL/TLS with IO::Async
      • IO::Async::Stream - Event callbacks and write buffering for a stream filehandle
      • IO::Async::Timer - Base class for Notifiers that use timed delays
      • IO::Async::Timer::Periodic - Event callback at regular intervals
      • IO::AsyncX::Sendfile - Adds support for Sys::Sendfile to IO::Async::Stream
      • IO::Barf - Barfing content to output file
      • IO::Dir - Supply object methods for directory handles
      • IO::FD - Faster accept, socket, listen with file descriptors, not handles
      • IO::File - Supply object methods for filehandles
      • IO::Handle - Supply object methods for I/O handles
      • IO::Interactive - Utilities for interactive I/O
      • IO::KQueue - Perl interface to the BSD kqueue system call
      • IO::Pipe - Supply object methods for pipes
      • IO::Poll - Object interface to system poll call
      • IO::Ppoll - Object interface to Linux's ppoll() call
      • IO::Seekable - Supply seek based methods for I/O objects
      • IO::Select - OO interface to the select system call
      • IO::Select::SSL - IO::Socket::SSL compatible IO::Select
      • IO::Socket - Object interface to socket communications
      • IO::Socket::INET - Object interface for AF_INET domain sockets
      • IO::Socket::INET6 - Object interface for AF_INET/AF_INET6 domain sockets - Deprecated
      • Notes for IO::Socket::INET6!
        • Use IO::Socket::IP instead
      • IO::Socket::IP - Family-neutral IP socket supporting both IPv4 and IPv6
      • IO::Socket::Multicast - Send and receive multicast messages
      • IO::Socket::SSL - SSL sockets with IO::Socket interface
      • IO::Socket::Socks - Provides a way to create socks client or server both 4 and 5 version
      • IO::Socket::Socks::Wrapped - Object wrapped by IO::Socket::Socks::Wrapper
      • IO::Socket::Socks::Wrapper - Add SOCKS support for any perl object / package / program
      • IO::Socket::SSL - SSL sockets with IO::Socket interface
      • Notes for IO::Socket::SSL!
        • Has lots of docs and sample code
      • IO::Trace - Log I/O of an arbitrary process
      • IO::Uring - io_uring for Perl
      • libev - http://software.schmorp.de/pkg/libev.html
      • Math::Ryu - Perl interface to Ryu (sane NV-to-string conversion)
      • Net::LibAsyncNS - A Perl wrapper around libasyncns
      • Promise::AsyncAwait - Async/await with promises
      • Promise::ES6 - ES6-style promises in Perl
      • Promise::Me - Fork Based Promise with Asynchronous Execution, Async, Await and Shared Data
      • Promise::XS - Fast promises in Perl
      • Promises - An implementation of Promises in Perl
      • Riemann::Client - A Perl client for the Riemann event system
      • Notes for Riemann::Client!
        • http://riemann.io/
      • Ryu - Asynchronous stream building blocks
      • Ryu::Async - Use Ryu with IO::Async
      • Signal::Mask - Signal masks made easy
      • Socket - Networking constants and support functions
      • Socket6 - IPv6 related part of the C socket.h defines and structure manipulators
      • Socket::More - List network interfaces and scope passive sockets to them
      • Sys::SigAction - Perl extension for Consistent Signal Handling
      • Syntax::Keyword::Defer - Execute code when leaving a block
      • Time::DoAfter - Wait before doing by label controller singleton
      • Timer::Wheel - Lightweight timer/event scheduler
      • UV - Perl interface to libuv
    • ExceptionHandling
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • https://phoenixtrap.com/2021/12/07/sweeter-perl-exception-classes/
        • https://gist.github.com/Ovid/5205534f7dcc52e4d931aaff301b39aa
      • Aion::Carp - Adds stack trace to exceptions
      • Aspect::Guard - General purpose guard object for destroy-time actions
      • autodie::Scope::Guard - Wrapper class for calling subs at end of scope
      • Capture::Tiny - Capture STDOUT and STDERR from Perl, XS or external programs
      • Notes for Capture::Tiny!
        • In core, so 1st choice
        • This module has a huge See Also list
      • Class::Throwable - A minimal lightweight exception class
      • Clean::Eval - Run code under eval without leaking $@ and get a rich error object back on failure
      • Errno::AnyString - Put arbitrary strings in $!
      • Error - Error/exception handling in an OO-ish way
      • Error::Pure - Perl module for structured errors
      • Notes for Error::Pure!
        • See Task::Error::Pure below
      • Error::Pure::Output::Bio - Output bioperl subroutines for Error::Pure
      • Error::Show - Locate and Diagnose Errors/Exceptions in Perl programs
      • Exception::Base - Lightweight exceptions
      • Notes for Exception::Base!
        • Has a great See Also
      • Exception::Class - A module that allows you to declare real exception classes in Perl
      • Notes for Exception::Class!
        • But see Throwable & HTTP::Throwable
      • Exception::Died - Convert simple die into real exception object
      • Exception::Reporter - A generic exception-reporting object
      • Exception::Reporter::Summarizer::PlackRequest - A summarizer for Plack::Request objects
      • Exception::Sink - General purpose compact exception handling
      • Exception::System - The exception class for system or library calls
      • Exception::ThrowUnless - It provides functions much like the standard perl functions, except that if they fail, they will die instead of returning undef
      • Exception::Warning - Convert simple warn into real exception object
      • ExclusiveLock::Guard - Lexically-scoped lock management
      • Glitch - Exception Handling
      • Guard - Safe cleanup blocks
      • Guard::Stats - Create guard objects and gather averall usage statistics from them
      • Guard::Timer - A scope guard that keeps time
      • Hook::LexWrap - Lexically scoped subroutine wrappers
      • Hook::Output::File - Redirect STDOUT/STDERR to a file
      • Hook::Output::Tiny - Easily enable/disable trapping of STDOUT/STDERR
      • Hook::Scope - Perl extension for adding hooks for exiting a scope
      • HTTP::Exception - Throw HTTP-Errors as (Exception::Class-) Exceptions
      • HTTP::Throwable - A set of strongly-typed, PSGI-friendly HTTP 1.1 exception libraries
      • Notes for HTTP::Throwable!
        • Build on top of Throwable
      • Ouch - Exceptions that don't hurt
      • Perl::Syntax - Syntax Check Perl files and strings
      • Notes for Perl::Syntax!
        • However it doesn't show any errors by design (only interested in process return code)
      • Proc::Guard - Process runner with RAII (Resource acquisition is initialization) pattern
      • Scope::Guard - Lexically-scoped resource management
      • Sub::Prepend - Prepend code to named subroutines
      • Sub::ScopeFinalizer - Execute code on exiting scope
      • Syntax::Check - Wraps 'perl -c' so it works even if modules are unavailable
      • Task::Error::Pure - Install the Error::Pure modules
      • Throwable - A role for classes that can be thrown
      • Throwable::SugarFactory - Build a library of syntax-sugared Throwable-based exceptions
      • Unexpected - Localised exception classes composed from roles
      • Value::Canary - Callbacks for value destruction
      • X::Tiny - Base class for a bare-bones exception factory
    • ExtensibleProvisioningProtocol
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
      • App::pepper - A command-line EPP client
      • Notes for App::pepper!
        • Uses Net::EPP
        • See TheRFCGuide for rfc5731, rfc9154
      • Net::EPP - A Perl library for the Extensible Provisioning Protocol (EPP).
      • Net::EPP::Registry::Nominet - A simple client interface to the Nominet EPP service
      • Net::EPP::Server - Provides a high-level framework for developing Extensible Provisioning Protocol (EPP) servers
    • FAQ
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • https://www.defectivebydesign.org
      • What guides are included in this wiki? - TheEUNormGuide
      • Notes for What guides are included in this wiki?!
        • TheLanguageGuide
        • TheOSGuide
        • TheRFCGuide
      • What do I do about AI in relation to my modules? - The module Safe::Hole includes a file called AI_POLICY.md. Examine that for ideas
      • Notes for What do I do about AI in relation to my modules?!
        • See also https://www.xda-developers.com/linux-kernel-now-allows-ai-written-code/
        • See also https://wiki.gentoo.org/wiki/Project:Council/AI_policy
      • Why are AI agents so dangerous - The fundamental problem is that AI must compress reality into model-legible forms. In this setting, adversaries can exploit the compression. They donât have to attack the territory; they can attack the map. Models lack local contextual knowledge. They process symbols, not meaning. A human sees a suspicious URL; an AI sees valid syntax. And that semantic gap becomes a security gap
      • Which is the best XML processing module? - There isn't one! But there are different ones for different problems
      • Where can I learn about Object Oriented Programming (OOP)? - https://metacpan.org/dist/Marlin/view/lib/Marlin/Manual/Beginning.pod
      • Why don't you include Catalyst, Moose, etc? - My time is limited.
      • What is prompt injection - It is semantic mimicry: adversarial instructions that resemble legitimate prompts, which trigger self-compromise
      • Why is prompt injection dangerous - LLMs process token sequences, but no mechanism exists to mark token privileges. The LLM can't tell the difference between legitimate input and corrupt input, so can be tricked into giving the latter priority. This might make it unsolvable in todayâs LLMs
      • What specifically is the problem with prompt injection - In training, we face poisoned datasets and backdoored models. In inference, we face adversarial inputs and prompt injection. During operation, we face a contaminated context and persistent compromise. We need semantic integrity: verifying not just data but interpretation, not just content but context, not just information but understanding. We can add checksums, signatures, and audit logs. But how do you checksum a thought? How do you sign semantics? How do you audit attention?
    • FeaturesAndPragmas
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • PerlDocumentation
      • base - Establish an ISA relationship with base classes at compile time
      • bigfloat - Transparent big floating point number support for Perl
      • bigint - Transparent big integer support for Perl
      • bignum - Transparent big number support for Perl
      • bigrat - Transparent big rational number support for Perl
      • builtins - Install all the new builtins from the builtin namespace
      • criticism - Perl pragma to enforce coding standards and best-practices
      • diagnostics - Perl pragma to produce verbose warning diagnostics
      • Notes for diagnostics!
        • Likewise, the splain program
      • exact - Perl pseudo pragma to enable strict, warnings, features, mro, filehandle methods
      • exact::class - Simple class interface extension for exact
      • exact::cli - Command-line interface helper utilities extension for exact
      • exact::conf - Cascading merged application configuration extension for exact
      • exact::fun - Functions and methods with parameter lists for exact
      • exact::lib - Compile-time @INC manipulation extension for exact
      • exact::me - Original program path locations extension for exact
      • experimental - Experimental features made easy
      • feature - Perl pragma to enable new features
      • Notes for feature!
        • See https://metacpan.org/pod/Exception::Base for sample code
      • Full::Pragmata - Common pragmata for Perl scripts and modules
      • Notes for Full::Pragmata!
        • A must-read document
      • lazy - Lazily install missing Perl modules
      • lexically - Lexically import functions from non-lexical exporters
      • parent - Establish an ISA relationship with base classes at compile time
      • perlhist - The Perl history records
      • perlpolicy - Various and sundry policies and commitments related to the Perl core
      • signatures - Subroutine signatures with no source filter
      • strict - Perl pragma to restrict unsafe constructs
      • strictures - Turn on strict and make most warnings fatal
      • switch - Perl pragma to activate if/given/when (via use feature 'switch';)
      • syntax - Activate syntax extensions
      • Syntax::Feature::Function - Provides a function keyword
      • warnings - Perl pragma to control optional warnings
    • FileHandling
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]

        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]

        • [Topic]

        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • for jQuery File Upload plugin
        • https://perl.plover.com/TieFile/why-not-DB_File
        • https://metacpan.org/pod/perlop#I%2FO-Operators
      • AnyEvent::Filesys::Notify - An AnyEvent compatible module to monitor files/directories for changes
      • App::BackupPlan - Perl extension for automated, regular backups
      • App::CdUtils - CLI utilities related to changing directories
      • App::CheckPerlReleaseFilename - Check whether filenames look like perl module release archive
      • App::PerlGzipScript - Gzip perl scripts to reduce their file size
      • App::SortCopies - The copy sorter! (What did you expect?)
      • App::DownloadsDirUtils - Utilities related to downloads directories
      • App::DuplicateFilesUtils - CLI utilities related to duplicate files
      • App::FileCleanerByDiskUage - Removes files based on disk space usage till it drops below the specified amount
      • App::FindUtils - Utilities related to finding files
      • App::FilenameKeyValueUtils - CLIs for Filename::KeyValue
      • App::FilenameTimestampUtils - CLIs for Filename::Timestamp
      • App::FilenameTypeUtils - CLIs for Filename::Type::*
      • App::FilenameUtils - CLIs for Filename::*
      • App::perlmv::scriptlet::prefix_with_number - Prefix filenames with number (usually to make them easily sortable)
      • App::renwd - REName current (Working) Directory
      • App::sdif - Contains utility programs cdif, sdif and watchdiff to post-process output of diff
      • App::Sky - Wrapper to rsync/etc. to upload files to a remote server and give download links
      • App::UniqFiles - Report unique/duplicate file {names,contents}
      • AWS::Lambda - Perl support for AWS Lambda Custom Runtime
      • AWS::S3 - Lightweight interface to Amazon S3 (Simple Storage Service)
      • AWS::XRay - AWS X-Ray tracing library
      • Backup::Snapback - Routines for support of rsync-based snapshot backup
      • Cwd::Guard - Temporary changing working directory (chdir)
      • Cwd::utf8 - Fully UTF-8 aware Cwd
      • Env::Path - Advanced operations on path variables
      • Fcntl - Various flag constants and helper functions from C's fcntl.h
      • File::Access::Driver - Convenient File Access with "Batteries included"
      • File::BackupCopy - Create a backup copy of the file
      • File::BaseDir - Use the Freedesktop.org base directory specification
      • File::Basename - Parse file paths into directory, filename and suffix
      • File::Basename::Extra - Extension to File::Basename, adds named access to file parts and handling of filename suffixes
      • File::BOM::Utils - Check, Add and Remove BOMs
      • File::ChangeNotify - Watch for changes to files, cross-platform style
      • File::chdir - A more sensible way to change directories. $CWD and @CWD, which are not global
      • File::Copy - Copy files or filehandles
      • File::Copy::clonefile - Call clonefile system call
      • Notes for File::Copy::clonefile!
        • MacOS only
      • File::Copy::NoClobber - Rename copied files safely if destination exists
      • Notes for File::Copy::NoClobber!
        • See also File::Util::Rename below
      • File::DirList - Provide a sorted list of directory content
      • File::Fetch - A generic file fetching mechanism
      • File::Find - Traverse a directory tree. Built-in
      • Notes for File::Find!
        • But see File::Find::Rule
      • File::FindRoot - Find the directory that's the root for a project
      • File::FindUniq - Find unique or duplicate file {contents,names}
      • File::Find::Closures - Functions you can use with File::Find
      • File::Find::Mason - Find files that contain Mason components
      • File::Find::Rule - Alternative interface to File::Find
      • Notes for File::Find::Rule!
        • But see Path::Iterator::Rule below
      • File::Find::utf8 - Fully UTF-8 aware File::Find
      • File::Finder - Nice wrapper for File::Find ala find(1)
      • File::FStore - Module for interacting with file stores
      • File::FStore::Base - Module for interacting with file stores
      • File::FStore::File - Module for interacting with file stores
      • File::Glob - Perl extension for BSD glob routine
      • File::GlobMapper - Extend File Glob to Allow Input and Output Files
      • File::HomeDir - Find your home and other directories on any platform
      • File::Information - Generic module for extracting information from filesystems
      • File::Information::Base - Generic module for extracting information from filesystems
      • File::Information::Filesystem - Generic module for extracting information from filesystems
      • File::Information::Inode - Generic module for extracting information from filesystems
      • File::Listing - Parse a directory listing
      • File::Lockfile::Emacs - Create/check/delete Emacs-style lockfiles
      • File::MimeInfo - Determine file type from the file name
      • File::MimeInfo::Applications - Find programs to open a file by mimetype
      • File::MimeInfo::Simple - Simple implementation to determine file type
      • File::MMagic - Guess file type
      • File::MoreUtil - File-related utilities, about 27 of them actually
      • File::Open::NoCache::ReadOnly - Open a file and flush from memory on closing
      • File::Path - Create or remove directory trees
      • File::Path::Redirect - Poor man's symbolic link path redirection
      • File::Print::Many - Print to more than one file descriptor at once
      • File::Process - Process text files with customer handlers
      • File::pushd - Change directory temporarily for a limited scope
      • File::Raw - Fast IO operations using direct system calls
      • File::Raw::Archive - Archive container reader/writer
      • File::Raw::Base64 - Base64 plugin for File::Raw
      • File::Raw::Gzip - Gzip / zlib / raw deflate plugin for File::Raw
      • File::Raw::Hash - Cryptographic and integrity digests as a File::Raw plugin
      • File::Raw::JSON - JSON / JSONL plugin for File::Raw
      • File::Raw::Separated - CSV/TSV plugin for File::Raw
      • File::ReadBackwards - Read a file backwards by lines
      • File::Rotate::Backup - Make backups of multiple directories and rotate them on unix
      • File::RsyBak - Backup files/directories with histories, using rsync
      • File::Save::Home - Place file safely under user home directory
      • File::ShareDir - Locate per-dist and per-module shared files
      • Notes for File::ShareDir!
        • But see the next module
      • File::ShareDir::Tarball - Deal transparently with shared files distributed as tarballs
      • File::ShareDir::Tiny - Locate per-dist and per-module shared files
      • Notes for File::ShareDir::Tiny!
        • For the installation half of the solution, there are several options available that depend on your installation tool. For ExtUtils::MakeMaker there is File::ShareDir::Install. Other tools like Module::Build, Module::Build::Tiny and Dist::Build have built in support for sharedirs
      • File::Slurper - A simple, sane and efficient module to slurp a file
      • Notes for File::Slurper!
        • And File::Slurp is obsolete
      • File::Slurper::Temp - File::Slurper + File::Temp
      • Notes for File::Slurper::Temp!
        • And be warned, File::Temp uses global variables. See TestingHelp
      • File::Spec - Portably perform operations on file names
      • File::Spec::Functions - Portably perform operations on file names
      • File::stat - By-name interface to Perl's built-in stat() functions
      • File::stat::Extra - An extension of the File::stat module, provides additional methods.
      • File::StatX - StatX for Perl
      • File::Sticker - Read, Write file meta-data
      • Notes for File::Sticker!
        • Includes sticker which sticks tags onto files, populates a database with file meta-data
        • See also Data::TagDB in DatabaseAndSQL1
      • File::Store - Module for interacting with file stores
      • File::Stubb - Stub file creator
      • File::Symlink::Relative - Create relative symbolic links
      • File::Temp - Return name and handle of a temporary file safely
      • Notes for File::Temp!
        • And be warned, File::Temp uses global variables. See TestingHelp
      • File::URIList - Module for reading and writing RFC 2483 URI lists
      • Notes for File::URIList!
        • See RfcGuide for rfc2483
      • File::Util::Copy - Utilities related to copying files
      • File::Util::Rename - Utilities related to renaming files
      • Notes for File::Util::Rename!
        • See also File::Copy::NoClobber above
      • File::ValueFile - Module for reading and writing ValueFile files
      • File::Which - Perl implementation of the which utility as an API
      • Filename::Compressed - Check whether filename indicates being compressed
      • Filename::Media::Info - Extract various information from media filenames
      • Filename::Timestamp - Extract date/timestamp from filename, if any
      • Filename::Type::Archive - Check whether filename indicates being an archive file
      • Filename::Type::Audio - Check whether filename indicates being an audio file
      • Filename::Type::Backup - Check whether filename indicates being a backup file
      • Filename::Type::Compressed - Check whether filename indicates being compressed
      • Filename::Type::Ebook - Check whether filename indicates being an e-book
      • Filename::Type::Executable - Check whether filename indicates being an executable program/script
      • Filename::Type::Image - Check whether filename indicates being an image
      • Filename::Type::Perl::Release - Check whether filename looks like a perl module release archive, e.g. a CPAN release tarball
      • Filename::Type::Video - Check whether filename indicates being a video file
      • Filesys::Fuse3 - Write filesystems in Perl using Fuse3
      • Notes for Filesys::Fuse3!
        • https://libfuse.github.io/doxygen/index.html - For docs for Fuse
      • Filesys::Notify::KQueue - Wrap IO::KQueue for watching file system
      • Filesys::Notify::Simple - Simple and dumb file system watcher
      • Filesys::Notify::Win32::ReadDirectoryChanges - Read/watch directory changes
      • FindApp - Find your application home and config your process for it
      • Notes for FindApp!
        • Consider this module if you have ever needed
        • use FindBin; use lib "$FindBin::Bin/../lib";
      • Flat::Profile - Streaming-first profiling for CSV/TSV flat files
      • Notes for Flat::Profile!
        • See [[Acronyms]] for ETL
      • IO::Dir - Supply object methods for directory handles
      • IO::ExplicitHandle - Force I/O handles to be explicitly specified
      • IO::FDPass - Pass a file descriptor over a socket
      • IO::File - Supply object methods for filehandles
      • IO::Handle - Supply object methods for I/O handles
      • IO::Seekable - Supply seek based methods for I/O objects
      • IO::SendFile - Perl extension that implements the sendfile() interface
      • IO::String - Emulate file interface for in-core strings
      • IO::Wrap - Wrap raw filehandles in the IO::Handle interface
      • jQuery::File::Upload - Server-side solution for the jQuery File Upload plugin
      • Linux::FD - Linux specific special filehandles
      • Modern::Open - Autovivification, Autodie, and 3-args open support
      • Module::Case - Case sensitive module loading
      • Module::Filename - Provides an object oriented, cross platform interface for getting a module's filename
      • Open::This - Try to Do the Right Thing when opening files
      • Overload::FileCheck - Override/mock perl file check -X: -e, -f, -d, ...
      • Path::Class - Cross-platform path specification manipulation
      • Path::Class::Tiny - A Path::Tiny wrapper for Path::Class compatibility
      • Path::Iterator::Rule - Iterative, recursive file finder
      • Path::Tiny - Provides methods like slurp_utf8 and spew_utf8 to read and write files using the correct encoding
      • Regexp::Pattern::Filename::Type::Archive - Archive filename
      • Regexp::Pattern::Filename::Type::Audio - Audio filename
      • Regexp::Pattern::Filename::Type::Ebook - Ebook filename
      • Regexp::Pattern::Filename::Type::Image - Image filename
      • Regexp::Pattern::Filename::Type::Image::WhatsApp - Image filename saved by WhatsApp
      • Regexp::Pattern::Filename::Type::Media - Media (image/audio/video) filename
      • Regexp::Pattern::Filename::Type::Media::WhatsApp - Media (video, image) filename saved by WhatsApp
      • Regexp::Pattern::Filename::Type::Video - Video filename
      • rename - Renames multiple files using perl expressions
      • Storage::Abstract - Abstraction for file storage
      • Stream::Buffered - Temporary buffer to save bytes
      • Sys::Sendfile - Zero-copy data transfer
      • Sys::Sendfile::FreeBSD - Wrapper for the FreeBSD sendfile(2) function
      • Text::CSV - Comma-separated values manipulator (using XS or PurePerl)
      • Text::CSV::Encoded - Encoding aware Text::CSV
      • Text::CSV_PP - Text::CSV_XS compatible pure-Perl module
      • Text::CSV::Separator - Determine the field separator of a CSV file
      • Text::CSV::Slurp - Convert CSV into an array of hashes, or an array of hashes into CSV
      • Text::CSV_XS - Comma-separated values manipulation routines
      • Tie::File - Access the lines of a disk file via a Perl array
      • Win32::ChangeNotify - Monitor events related to files and directories
    • FindDependencies
      • App::findeps - the Module to find dependencies for files you've selected
      • Module::ScanDeps - Recursively scan Perl code for dependencies
      • Module::ScanDeps::Static - A cleanup of rpmbuild's perl.req
    • FontMatters
