        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
      • Font::TTF - Perl module for TrueType Font hacking
      • Font::FreeType - Read font files and render glyphs from Perl using FreeType2
      • Font::Metrics - Advance widths, kerning and metrics for Standard 14 and TrueType fonts
      • Font::TTF::Font - Memory representation of a font
      • HarfBuzz::Shaper - Use HarfBuzz for text shaping
      • Notes for HarfBuzz::Shaper!
        • This module is not limited to TTFs
    • Games
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • https://cowboyprogramming.com/2007/01/05/evolve-your-heirachy/
        • https://github.com/thrig/ministry-of-silly-vaults/
        • For Blossom word game
      • Acme::Hospital::Bed - The great new Acme::Hospital::Bed!
      • Acme::Matrix - Heavenly digital rain
      • Acme::MetaSyntactic::Themes - The original Acme::MetaSyntactic themes
      • Alien::SDL3 - Build and install SDL3
      • App::GUI::GoLP - A GUI for viewing and editing Life-like cellular automata, in Perl/Prima
      • App::wordlist::blossom - A wordlist wrapper to help list words for Blossom word game
      • App::puzzl - A CLI for writing & running Advent of Code solutions written in Perl
      • Chandra::Game::Tetris - Tetris built on Chandra
      • Chess::Plisco - A comprehensive chess library for Perl
      • Chess4p - A library for chess related functionality
      • Data::SCS::DefParser - A Perl module to parse units contained in SII definition files (plain text variant)
      • Game::Cribbage - Cribbage game engine
      • Game::DijkstraMap - A numeric grid of weights plus some related functions
      • Game::Entities - A simple entity registry for ECS designs
      • Game::FaceGenerator - A web app to combine random images into faces
      • Game::HexDescribe - A web app to add random table driven data to map data
      • Game::LevelMap - Level map representation
      • Game::Life - Plays Conway's Game of Life
      • Game::Life::Faster - Plays John Horton Conway's Game of Life
      • Game::PlatformsOfPeril - The platforms of peril
      • Game::TextMapper - A web app to generate maps based on text files
      • Game::TextPatterns - Generate patterns of text
      • Game::TileMap - Representation of tile-based two-dimensional rectangular maps
      • Games::Board - A parent class for board games
      • Games::Mahjongg::Solver::deBondt::FFI - Perl/FFI bindings to Mahjongg Solitaire Solver (after peeking) by Michiel de Bondt
      • Games::ScottAdams - Perl extension for representing Scott Adams games
      • Games::Solitaire::BlackHole::Solver - A solver for Black Hole Solitaire
      • Games::Sudoku::General - Solve sudoku-like puzzles
      • Games::Sudoku::Html - Visualize and play collections of standard 9x9 Sudoku in your browser
      • Games::Sudoku::PatternSolver - Solve and generate Sudoku 9x9 puzzles
      • Games::Sudoku::PatternSolver::Generator - Produces 9x9 Sudoku solution grids and 9x9 Sudoku puzzles
      • Games::Sudoku::Pdf - Produce pdf files from your digital Sudoku sources or collections
      • LibOrigin - Perl extension for making Origin games
      • Net::RCON::Minecraft - Minecraft RCON remote console
      • Notes for Net::RCON::Minecraft!
        • https://bugs.mojang.com/browse/MC/issues/MC-72390
      • Poker::Eval - Deal, score, and calculate expected win rates of poker hands
      • Run::WeeklyChallenge - Facilitates running a solution to https://theweeklychallenge.org using one or more sets of inputs provided as JSON command line arguments
      • SDL3 - Simple DirectMedia Layer 3.0
      • Task::MetaSyntactic - All Acme::MetaSyntactic themes at once
      • Term::Maze - Mazes in the terminal
      • WWW::ARDB - Perl client for the ARC Raiders Database API (ardb.app)
      • WWW::MetaForge - Perl client for MetaForge gaming APIs
    • GenealogyStuff
      • DateTime::Format::Genealogy - Create a DateTime object from a Genealogy Date
      • Notes for DateTime::Format::Genealogy!
        • https://metacpan.org/dist/DateTime-Format-Genealogy
      • Gedcom::Individual - A module to manipulate GEDCOM individuals
      • Genealogy::ChroniclingAmerica - Find URLs for a given person on the Library of Congress Newspaper Records
      • Genealogy::FindaGrave - Find URLs on FindaGrave for a person
      • Genealogy::Gedcom::Date - Parse GEDCOM dates in French r/German/Gregorian/Hebrew/Julian
      • Genealogy::Military::Branch - Extract military branch from free-text genealogy notes
      • Genealogy::Obituary::Lookup - Lookup an obituary
      • Genealogy::Obituary::Parser - Extract structured family relationships from obituary text
      • Genealogy::Occupation - Normalise and translate genealogical occupation strings
      • Genealogy::Relationship - Calculate the relationship between two people
      • Genealogy::Relationship::Name - Return a genealogical relationship name from step counts
      • Genealogy::Wills - Lookup in a database of wills
      • HTML::Genealogy::Map - Extract and map genealogical events from a GEDCOM file
    • GeographicStuff
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • https://ipapi.com/
        • https://opencagedata.com/api
        • For API v3
        • https://what3words.com/
      • Alien::gdal - Compile GDAL, the Geographic Data Abstraction Library
      • Alien::libmaxminddb - Find or install libmaxminddb
      • App::geoip - A tool to show geographical data based on hostname or IP address(es)
      • Alien::geos::af - Compile GEOS, the Geometry Engine, Open Source
      • Alien::proj - Compile the Proj library
      • Data::OFN::Address - Immutable data object for OFN (OtevÅenÃ© formÃ¡lnÃ­ normy) representation of address in the Czech Republic
      • Data::OFN::Common - OFN common data objects
      • Geo::Address::Formatter - Take structured address data and format it according to the various global/country rules
      • Geo::Address::Parser - Lightweight country-aware address parser from flat text
      • Geo::Address::Parser::Country - Resolve a place string component to a canonical country name
      • Geo::AnomalyDetector - Detect anomalies in geospatial coordinate datasets
      • Geo::BUFR - Perl extension for handling of WMO BUFR files
      • Notes for Geo::BUFR!
        • https://wiki.met.no/bufr.pm/start
      • Geo::CheapRuler - A collection of very fast approximations to common geodesic measurements
      • Geo::Coder::Abbreviations - Quick and Dirty Interface to https://github.com/mapbox/geocoder-abbreviations
      • Geo::Coder::All - This is wrapper for other geocoder cpan modules
      • Geo::Coder::Bing - Geocode addresses with the Bing Maps API
      • Geo::Coder::CA - Provides a Geo-Coding functionality using https://geocoder.ca for both Canada and the US
      • Geo::Coder::Free - Provides a Geo-Coding functionality using free databases
      • Geo::Coder::GeoApify - Provides a Geo-Coding functionality using https://www.geoapify.com/maps-api/
      • Geo::Coder::GeocodeFarm - Geocode addresses with the GeocodeFarm API
      • Geo::Coder::Google::V3 - Google Maps Geocoding API V3
      • Geo::Coder::GooglePlaces::V3 - Google Places Geocoding API V3
      • Geo::Coder::List - Call many Geo-Coders
      • Geo::Coder::Many - Module to tie together multiple Geo::Coder::* modules
      • Geo::Coder::OpenCage - Geocode coordinates and addresses with the OpenCage Geocoding API
      • Geo::Coder::OSM - Geocode addresses with the OpenStreetMap Nominatim API
      • Geo::Coder::Ovi - Geocode addresses with the Ovi Maps API
      • Geo::Coder::Mapbox - Provides a Geo-Coding functionality using https://mapbox.com
      • Notes for Geo::Coder::Mapbox!
        • https://docs.mapbox.com/api/search/geocoding/
        • Based on Geo::Coder::GooglePlaces::V3 - Google Places Geocoding API V3
        • https://metacpan.org/pod/Geo::Coder::GooglePlaces::V3
      • Geo::Coder::TomTom - Geocode addresses with the TomTom Map Toolkit API
      • Geo::Coder::US::Census - Provides a Geo-Coding functionality for the US using https://geocoding.geo.census.gov
      • Geo::Coder::XYZ - Provides a Geo-Coding functionality using https://geocode.xyz
      • Geo::Converter::dms2dd - Parse and convert values in Degrees/Minutes/Seconds formats to decimal degrees
      • Geo::Coordinates::DecimalDegrees - Convert between degrees/minutes/seconds and decimal degrees
      • Geo::GDAL::FFI - A foreign function interface to GDAL
      • Notes for Geo::GDAL::FFI!
        • See also https://gdal.org/en/stable/
      • Geo::GeoNames - Perform geographical queries using GeoNames Web Services
      • Geo::Geos - Perl interface for GEOS C++ library
      • Geo::Geos::Algorithm - Fundamental computational geometry algorithms
      • Geo::Geos::Coordinate - The lightweight class used to store coordinates
      • Geo::Geos::Envelope - Defines a rectangular region of the 2D coordinate plane
      • Geo::Geos::Index::STRtree - A spatial index structure which uses the Sort-Tile-Recursive (STR) algorithm
      • Notes for Geo::Geos::Index::STRtree!
        • See also Tree::R, Tree::STR
      • Geo::GoogleEarth::AutoTour - Generate Google Earth Camera Tours from Tracks and Paths
      • Geo::H3 - H3 Geospatial Hexagon Indexing System
      • Notes for Geo::H3!
        • https://github.com/uber/h3
      • Geo::H3::FFI - Perl FFI binding to H3 v3.x library functions
      • Geo::Inverse - Calculate geographic distance from a latitude and longitude pair
      • Geo::IPinfo - The official Perl library for IPinfo
      • Geo::IP2Location - Look up of country etc (24 fields) using IP address. It supports both IPv4 and IPv6 addressing
      • Geo::IP2Proxy - Reverse search of IP addresses to detect VPN servers, etc
      • Geo::IPfree - Geo::IPfree - Look up the country of an IPv4 address
      • Geo::Leaflet - Generates a Leaflet JavaScript map web page
      • Geo::LibProj::cs2cs - IPC interface to PROJ cs2cs
      • Geo::Local::Server - Returns the configured coordinates of the local server
      • Geo::Location::IP - Map IP addresses to location information
      • Geo::Location::Point - Location information
      • Geo::Location::TimeZoneFinder - Map geographic coordinates to time zone names
      • Geo::IPinfo - The official Perl library for IPinfo
      • Geo::Point - A point on the globe
      • Geo::Proj4 - PROJ cartographic projections library
      • Geo::Shape - Base class for 2-dimensional points on the earth surface
      • Geo::Spline - Calculate geographic locations between GPS fixes
      • Geo::StreetAddress::US - Perl extension for parsing US street addresses
      • Geo::WeatherNWS - A simple way to get current weather data from the NWS
      • Geo::WebService::Elevation::USGS - Elevation queries against USGS web services
      • Geo::WebService::OpenCellID - Perl API for the opencellid.org database
      • Geo::What3Words - Turn WGS84 coordinates into three word addresses and vice-versa using what3words.com HTTPS API
      • Geography::States::Borders - Return the borders of states and provinces
      • GIS::Distance - Calculate geographic distances
      • Notes for GIS::Distance!
        • Contains GIS::Distance::Haversine etc
      • GIS::Distance::Fast - C implementation of GIS::Distance formulas
      • Google::GeoCoder::Smart - Simple Google Geocoding API client
      • HTML::GoogleMaps::V3 - A simple wrapper around the Google Maps API
      • Notes for HTML::GoogleMaps::V3!
        • See also Geo::Coder::GooglePlaces - Google Maps Geocoding API
      • HTML::OSM - A module to generate an interactive OpenStreetMap with customizable coordinates and zoom level
      • IBGE::Municipios - Interface para cÃ³digos de municÃ­pios do IBGE
      • IP::Geolocation::MMDB - Read MaxMind DB files
      • Net::API::Nominatim - Perl client for local or public OpenStreetMap Nominatim Geocoding service. Open source, unrestricted and free
      • Starlink::AST - Interface to the Starlink AST library
      • Notes for Starlink::AST!
        • The Starlink AST library provides facilities for transforming coordinates from one system to another in an object oriented manner
        • See also http://starlink.eao.hawaii.edu/starlink/AST
    • GitStuff
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • https://libgit2.org/
        • https://www.addictivetips.com/ubuntu-linux-tips/best-github-clients-for-linux/
        • Compare 2 versions of a module
      • Alien::Build - Build external dependencies for use in CPAN
      • Alien::Build::Plugin::Download::GitHub - Alien::Build plugin to download from GitHub
      • Alien::Libgit2 - Find or build libgit2, the linkable Git library
      • App::GHGen - Comprehensive GitHub Actions workflow generator, analyzer, and optimizer
      • App::ghmulti - Helps when using multiple Github accounts with SSH keys
      • App::git_log_ch_usrdata - Change username and email address in git log
      • App::GitGot - A tool to make it easier to manage multiple git repositories
      • App::Git::Autofixup - Create fixup commits for topic branches
      • App::Git::Perl - Adds custom git command "git perl" to make it easier to monitor latest changes in perl modules, and make you collaborate faster
      • App::GitHubUtils - Utilities related to GitHub
      • App::GitKtti - Git flow helper scripts for safe branch management
      • App::GitUtils - Day-to-day command-line utilities for git
      • App::Workflow::Lint - Linter for GitHub Actions workflow files
      • Git::Bunch - Manage gitbunch directory (directory which contain git repos)
      • Notes for Git::Bunch!
        • But see http://joeyh.name/code/mr/
      • Git::Database - Provide access to the Git object database
      • Git::Grouper - Categorize git repositories into one/more groups and perform actions on them
      • Git::Hooks - Framework for implementing Git (and Gerrit) hooks
      • Git::Libgit2 - Low-level FFI bindings to libgit2
      • Git::Native - Native Git for Perl via libgit2 (FFI, no fork/exec)
      • Git::RemoteURL::Parse - Parse and classify Git remote URLs (GitHub, GitLab)
      • Git::Repository - Perl interface to Git repositories
      • Notes for Git::Repository!
        • But see Git::Native
      • Git::Server - Secure Git Server with more granular hooks capabilities than default git
      • Notes for Git::Server!
        • Similar purpose and functionality to Git::Hooks but more powerful with less dependencies
      • Git::Wrapper - Wrap git(7) command-line interface
      • Notes for Git::Wrapper!
        • But see Git::Native
      • GitHub::Actions - Work in GitHub Actions using native Perl
      • GitHub::Crud - Create, Read, Update, Delete files, commits, issues, and web hooks on GitHub
      • GitHub::RSS - Collect data from Github.com for feeding into RSS
      • git::workflow - Explains our company's git-workflow code
      • Notes for git::workflow!
        • https://ovid.github.io/articles/easy-git-workflow.html
      • Group::Git - Base module for group of git repository operations
      • Parse::Gitignore - Parse .gitignore files
      • PitHub - A Perl interface to github
      • Notes for PitHub!
        • Sample usage: https://perladvent.org/2022/2022-12-03.html
      • WWW::Gitea - Perl client for the Gitea REST API
    • GnuPG
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • https://gnupg.org/index.html
        • [Topic]
      • GnuPG::Interface - Perl interface to GnuPG
    • GoogleStuff
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • For a Google API key
        • For Google Books API
        • For Google Developer help
      • Google::Ads::GoogleAds::Client - This is the main interface to the Google Ads API
      • Google::ISBNNumbers - Retrieve book info by ISBN number
      • Google::RestApi - Connection to Google REST APIs (currently Drive and Sheets)
      • Net::Google::Analytics::MeasurementProtocol - Send Google Analytics (GA4) user interaction data from Perl
      • Net::Google::Drive::Simple - Includes instructions on how to get a Client ID and token
    • Graphs
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • https://gephi.org
        • For GraphML
        • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Petri_net
      • Algorithm::Graphs::Reachable::Tiny - Compute reachable nodes in a graph
      • Data::Hopen - A dataflow library with first-class edges
      • Notes for Data::Hopen!
        • It is the underlying engine of the App::hopen cross-platform software build generator, but can be used for any dataflow task that can be represented as a directed acyclic graph (DAG)
      • Graph - Graph data structures and algorithms
      • Graph::Directed - Directed graphs
      • Graph::Easy - Convert or render graphs (as ASCII, HTML, SVG or via Graphviz)
      • Graph::Easy::As_svg - Output a Graph::Easy as Scalable Vector Graphics (SVG)
      • Graph::Easy::Introspect - Introspection and AST for Graph::Easy layouts
      • Graph::Grammar - A Perl implementation of a graph rewriting method (a.k.a. graph grammar)
      • Notes for Graph::Grammar!
        • Every rule is evaluated for each vertex in a graph and, if a match is found, an action associated with the rule is executed
      • Graph::Kruskal - Kruskal's Algorithm for Minimal Spanning Trees in Graphs
      • Graph::Layout::Aesthetic - A module for laying out graphs
      • Graph::Layout::Aesthetic::Topology - Graph topology for use by Graph::Layout::Aesthetic
      • Graph::MaxFlow - Compute maximum flow between 2 vertices in a graph
      • Graph::MoreUtils - Utilities for graphs
      • Graph::Nauty - Perl bindings for Nauty
      • Notes for Graph::Nauty!
        • https://pallini.di.uniroma1.it/
      • Graph::PetriNet - Perl extension for Petri Nets
      • Graph::Undirected - Undirected graphs
      • Graph::VF2 - VF2 subgraph isomorphism detection method for Perl Graph
      • GraphViz2 - A wrapper for AT&T's Graphviz
      • Notes for GraphViz2!
        • https://github.com/graphviz-perl
      • libplot - Part of GNU plotutils
      • Notes for libplot!
        • Docs: https://www.gnu.org/software/plotutils/
        • sudo apt-get install plotutils
        • Perl interface Graphics::Libplot - Perl extension for libplot plotting library
      • SVG - Perl extension for generating Scalable Vector Graphics (SVG) documents
      • Svg::Simple - Write Scalar Vector Graphics using Perl syntax
      • SVG::TT::Graph - Base module for generating SVG graphics
    • HaikuOS
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • For the KDL specification
        • For the upstream C library
      • Alien::ckdl - Build and install ckdl, a C library for KDL documents
      • Text::KDL::XS - Fast KDL parser and emitter via libckdl
    • HardwareStuff
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • https://mikrotik.com/support/
        • The Standard for IoT Messaging
        • https://sourceforge.net/projects/linux-gpib
      • Alien::SIMDe - Find or build the SIMDe library
      • Asm::X86 - List of instructions and registers of x86-compatible processors, validating and converting instructions and memory references
      • BACnet::Device - High-level interface for BACnet device communication and COV subscriptions
      • Bread::Board - A solderless way to wire up your application components
      • Data::Power::Inspection - Power Equipment Fault Inspection and Troubleshooting Library
      • Device::AVR::UPDI - Interact with an AVR microcontroller over UPDI
      • Device::Chip::MAX7219Panel - Chip driver for a panel of MAX7219 modules
      • Device::Chip::SSD1306 - Chip driver for monochrome OLED modules
      • Device::Firmata - Module for controlling Firmata devices
      • Device::GPIB - Access a variety of generic and specific GPIB devices
      • Device::Serial::SLuRM - Communicate the SLÂµRM protocol over a serial port
      • Disk::SmartTools - Provide tools to work with disks via S.M.A.R.T.
      • MikroTik::Client - Non-blocking interface to MikroTik API
      • Net::Async::ArtNet - Use Art-Net with IO::Async
      • Notes for Net::Async::ArtNet!
        • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Art-Net
      • Net::MQTT - Perl module for MQTT Protocol (http://mqtt.org/)
      • Net::MQTT::Simple - Minimal MQTT version 3 interface
      • OPCUA::Open62541 - Perl XS wrapper for open62541 OPC UA library
      • Physics::Ellipsometry::VASE - Variable Angle Spectroscopic Ellipsometry data fitting
      • Power::Outlet - Control and query network attached power outlets
      • Speak - Convert text to speech using Google's engine and play it on speakers
    • HashHandling
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
      • CGI::Expand - Convert flat hash to nested data using TT2's dot convention
      • Notes for CGI::Expand!
        • Module's See Also is useful
      • Data::Hash::Diff::Smart - Smart structural diff for Perl data structures
      • Data::Hash::Patch::Smart - Apply structural, wildcard, and array-aware patches to Perl data structures
      • Data::MultiValuedHash - Hash whose keys have multiple ordered values
      • Hash::Case - Base class for hashes with key-casing requirements
      • Hash::Flatten - Flatten/unflatten complex data hashes
      • Hash::Match - Match contents of a hash against rules
      • Hash::Merge - Merges arbitrarily deep hashes into a single hash
      • Hash::Merge::Simple - Recursively merge two or more hashes, simply
      • Hash::MultiValue - Store multiple values per key
      • Hash::Ordered - A fast, pure-Perl ordered hash class
      • Hash::Util - A selection of general-utility hash subroutines - Built-in
      • Hash::Util::Exists::Tiny - Some hash helper functions related to perl's "exists" function.
      • Hash::Util::FieldHash - Support for Inside-Out Classes. Built-in
      • Hash::Util::FieldHash::Compat - Use Hash::Util::FieldHash or ties, depending on availability
      • Hash::Util::Merge - Utility functions for merging hashes
      • Hash::Util::Pick - The non-destructive utilities for picking hash
      • Hash::Util::Set - Set operations on hash keys
      • Hash::Util::Set::XS - XS implementation of Hash::Util::Set
      • Hash::Wrap - Create on-the-fly objects from hashes
      • Notes for Hash::Wrap!
        • The pod's SEE-ALSO has a great list of alternatives
      • HTTP::Rollup - Translate an HTTP query string to a hierarchical structure
      • Pivot::ArrayOfHashes - Pivot arrays of hashes, such as those returned by DBI
      • Tie::Array::AsHash - Tie arrays as hashes by splitting lines on separator
      • Tie::Array::DBD - Tie a plain array to a database table
      • Notes for Tie::Array::DBD!
        • Contains Tie::Hash::DBD
      • Tie::DBI - Tie hashes to DBI relational databases
      • Tie::Hash - Base class definitions for tied hashes. Built-in
      • Notes for Tie::Hash!
        • Contains Tie::StdHash, Tie::ExtraHash
      • Tie::Hash::Abbrev - A hash which can be accessed using abbreviated keys
      • Tie::Hash::Abbrev::Smart - A hash which can be accessed using abbreviated keys
      • Tie::Hash::Array - A hash which is internally implemented as a sorted array
      • Tie::Hash::Create - Extend Tie::ExtraHash, enables the tie-object to reflect the tied array
      • Tie::Hash::Indexed - Ordered hashes for Perl
      • Notes for Tie::Hash::Indexed!
        • It generally fails tests on Perl 5.18 and above due to the hash randomization change
        • See https://metacpan.org/pod/Hash::Ordered#SEE-ALSO
      • Tie::Hash::KeysMask - Control key aliasing by mask function, e.g. omit case of character distinction
      • Tie::Hash::MultiValue - Store multiple values per key
      • Tie::Hash::MultiValueOpts - Store multiple values per option
      • Tie::InsertOrderHash - Insert-order-preserving tied hash
      • Tie::IxHash - Ordered associative arrays for Perl
      • Notes for Tie::IxHash!
        • Slow. See https://metacpan.org/pod/Hash::Ordered#SEE-ALSO
      • Tie::LLHash - Ordered hashes (quite slow)
      • Tie::OrderedHash - Ordered Hashes with a public C ABI
      • Tie::RefHash - Use references as hash keys
      • Tie::RefHash::Weak - A Tie::RefHash subclass with weakened references in the keys
      • Tie::StoredOrderHash - Ordered associative arrays for Perl
      • Util::H2O - Convert a hash into an object with methods
      • Notes for Util::H2O!
        • https://perladvent.org/2023/2023-12-22.html (reads config files, generates accessors)
        • https://perladvent.org/2022/2022-12-06.html
      • Util::H2O::More - Convenience utilities built on Util::H2O (baptise, d2o, INI/YAML/HTTP helpers, Getopt helpers)
    • HealthMatters
      • ACME::2026 - Checklists for glorious 2026 goals
      • App::BPOMUtils::RPO::Ingredients - Group ingredients suitable for food label
      • App::MineralUtils - Utilities related to mineral supplements
      • Sah::SchemaBundle::Nutrient - Sah schemas related to nutrients
      • TableData::Health::Nutrient - Nutrients database
      • WWW::Search::PharmGKB - Search and retrieve information from the PharmGKB database
    • HTMLHandling
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • https://datatables.net
        • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Litavis
        • http://savage.net.au/misc/Mojo.Wiki.html
        • https://www.w3.org/TR/REC-html40/sgml/entities.html
      • Alien::LibGumbo - Gumbo parser library
      • Notes for Alien::LibGumbo!
        • See also HTML::Gumbo
      • App::htidx - Generate static HTML directory listings
      • CGI::FormMagick - Easily create CGI form-based applications
      • Crayon - Dedupe, minify and extend CSS
      • Notes for Crayon!
        • But see Litavis below
      • Data::TableReader::Decoder::HTML - Access the tables of an HTML document
      • Form::Diva - Generate HTML5 form label and input fields
      • Form::Factory - A general-purpose form handling API
      • Form::Sensible - A sensible way to handle form based user interface
      • Form::Tiny - Input validator centered around Type::Tiny
      • Notes for Form::Tiny!
        • https://brtastic.xyz/blog/article/perl-form-tiny
      • Form::Toolkit - A toolkit to build Data centric Forms
      • HTML::AutoTag - Just another HTML tag generator
      • Notes for HTML::AutoTag!
        • Generate nested HTML (HTML4, XHTML and HTML5) tags with custom indentation, custom encoding and automatic attribute value rotation
      • HTML::Composer - Compose validated HTML from Perl data structures
      • HTML::Defang - Cleans HTML as well as CSS of scripting and other executable contents, and neutralises XSS attacks
      • HTML::Detoxifier - Practical module to strip harmful HTML
      • HTML::Display - Display HTML locally in a browser
      • Notes for HTML::Display!
        • Can be used by Spreadsheet::HTML. See SpreadsheetStuff
      • HTML::DOMbo - Convert between XML::DOM and {XML/HTML}::Element trees
      • HTML::ElementTable - Perl extension for manipulating a table composed of HTML::Element style components
      • HTML::Entities - Encode or decode strings with HTML entities
      • HTML::Entities::Numbered - Conversion of numbered HTML entities
      • HTML::Escape - Faster than HTML::Entities because it does less
      • HTML::Form - Class that represents an HTML form element
      • HTML::FormFu - HTML Form Creation, Rendering and Validation Framework
      • HTML::FromMail - Base-class for the HTML producers
      • HTML::GenerateUtil - Routines useful when generating HTML output
      • HTML::Gumbo - HTML5 parser based on gumbo C library
      • Notes for HTML::Gumbo!
        • See also Alien::LibGumbo
      • HTML::HTMLdoc - Perl interface to the htmldoc program for producing PDF Files from HTML content
      • Notes for HTML::HTMLdoc!
        • For htmldoc see https://www.msweet.org/htmldoc/
        • HTMLDOC converts HTML and Markdown source files or web pages to EPUB, PostScript, or PDF files with an optional table of contents
      • HTML::Object - HTML Parser, Modifier and Query Interface
      • Notes for HTML::Object!
        • This module is yet another HTML parser, manipulation and query interface, but probably the most comprehensive one
      • HTML::Parser - HTML parser class
      • Notes for HTML::Parser!
        • Contains HTML::Entities
      • HTML::ParseBrowser - Simple interface for User-Agent string parsing
      • HTML::PopupTreeSelect - HTML popup tree widget
      • HTML::RewriteAttributes - Concise attribute rewriting
      • HTML::Scrubber - Perl extension for scrubbing/sanitizing HTML
      • HTML::Selector::XPath - CSS Selector to XPath compiler
      • HTML::Similarity - Calculate the structural similarity between two HTML documents
      • HTML::Strip - Perl extension for stripping HTML markup from text
      • HTML::StripScripts - Strip scripting constructs out of HTML
      • HTML::TableContentParser - Do interesting things with the contents of tables
      • HTML::Tabulate - HTML table rendering class
      • HTML::Tagset - Data tables useful in parsing HTML
      • Notes for HTML::Tagset!
        • These hashes hold info per HTML tag about tags
      • HTML::Tidy - (X)HTML validation in a Perl object
      • HTML::Tiny - Lightweight, dependency free HTML/XML generation
      • HTML::Tree - Build and scan parse-trees of HTML
      • Notes for HTML::Tree!
        • Contains various articles
      • HTML::TreeBuilder - HTML::TreeBuilder - Parser that builds a HTML syntax tree
      • Notes for HTML::TreeBuilder!
        • Can be slow for large docs, hence the next module
      • HTML::TreeBuilder::LibXML - HTML::TreeBuilder and XPath compatible interface with libxml
      • Notes for HTML::TreeBuilder::LibXML!
        • HTML::TreeBuilder::LibXML is drop-in-replacement for HTML::TreeBuilder::XPath
      • HTML::TreeStructured - Perl extension for generating tree structured HTML- Has minimal documentation
      • Notes for HTML::TreeStructured!
        • But see HTML::PopupTreeSelect
      • HTML::T5 - HTML validation in a Perl object
      • Notes for HTML::T5!
        • This is a replacement for HTML::Lint
        • Requires Alien::TidyHTML5, which provides the C html-tidy library or ascertains its presence
      • HTML::Valid - Tidy/validate HTML
      • HTML::Zoom - Selector based streaming template engine
      • HTML5::DOM - Super fast html5 DOM library with css selectors (based on Modest/MyHTML)
      • Litavis - CSS preprocessor and compiler implemented in C via XS
      • MathML::Entities - Convert XHTML+MathML Named Entities to Numeric Character References
      • Mojo::DOM58 - Minimalistic HTML/XML DOM parser with CSS selectors
      • Rose::HTML::Objects - Object-oriented interfaces for HTML.
      • Tags::HTML::Container - There are many modules in this series. See e.g. UsernamePassword
      • Notes for Tags::HTML::Container!
        • And Tags::HTML::Page, Tags::HTML::Element etc
      • Tags::HTML::Footer - Tags helper for HTML footer
      • Tags::HTML::Messages - Tags helper for HTML messages
      • Text::CleanFragment - Clean up text to use as URL fragment or filename
      • Text::Table::HTML - Generate HTML table
      • Text::Table::HTML::DataTables - Generate HTML table with jQuery and DataTables plugin
      • Web::Query - Yet another scraping library like jQuery
      • WWW::ClickSource - Determine the source of a visit on your website : organic, adwords, facebook, referer site
      • XAO::Base - Set of base classes for the XAO family of products
      • XAO::FS - XAO framework database layer
      • XAO::Web - XAO Web Developer, dynamic content building suite
      • Notes for XAO::Web!
        • https://ejelta.com/xao/
    • HTTPHandling
      • See also!

        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • http://blogs.perl.org/users/neilb/2011/10/cpan-modules-for-parsing-user-agent-strings.html
        • https://daniel.haxx.se/http2/
        • https://data-star.dev/
        • https://dev.to/fgasper/perling-and-curling-2i10
        • https://http2.github.io/
        • https://http3-explained.haxx.se/
        • https://nghttp2.org/
        • https://www.iana.org/assignments/http-status-codes/http-status-codes.xhtml
        • http://savage.net.au/misc/Mojo.Wiki.html
      • Alien::nghttp2 - Find or build the nghttp2 HTTP/2 C library
      • Authen::WebAuthn - A library to add Web Authentication support to server applications
      • Notes for Authen::WebAuthn!
        • This module lets you validate Web Authentication registration and authentication responses
        • https://www.w3.org/TR/webauthn/
      • Business::Stripe::Webhook - A Perl module for handling webhooks sent by Stripe
      • CGI::Ajax - A perl-specific system for writing Asynchronous web applications
      • CGI::Expand - Convert flat hash to nested data using TT2's dot convention
      • CGI::Info - Information about the CGI environment
      • CGI::Session - Persistent session data in CGI applications
      • CGI::Session::Driver::mariadb - CGI::Session driver for MariaDB database
      • CGI::Simple - A Simple totally OO CGI interface that is CGI.pm compliant
      • Cookie - Cookie Object with Encryption or Signature
      • Cookie::Jar - Cookie Jar Class for Server & Client
      • Cookies::Roundtrip - Convert between different HTTP Cookie formats, well, at least we tried!
      • Data::URIID - Extractor for identifiers from URIs
      • Notes for Data::URIID!
        • See also File::FStore in FileHandling
      • Data::URIID::Base - Extractor for identifiers from URIs
      • Datastar::SSE - Module for creating Datastar Server Events
      • Duadua - Detect User-Agent, do up again! Parses the User-Agent string
      • FU - Framework Ultimatum: A Lean and Efficient Zero-Dependency Web Framework.
      • Header help - HTTP::UserAgentClientHints
      • Notes for Header help!
        • This repository hosts the User Agent Client Hints (UA-CH) specification.
      • HTTP::Body - HTTP Body Parser
      • HTTP::BrowserDetect - Determine Web browser, version, and platform from an HTTP user agent string
      • HTTP::Cookies - HTTP cookie jars
      • HTTP::Cookies::Chrome - Cookie storage and management for Google Chrome
      • Notes for HTTP::Cookies::Chrome!
        • Uses HTTP::Cookies
      • HTTP::CookieJar::LWP - LWP adapter for HTTP::CookieJar
      • HTTP::Cookies::Microsoft - Access to Microsoft cookies files
      • HTTP::Cookies::Mozilla - Cookie storage and management for Mozilla
      • HTTP::Cookies::Netscape - Access to Netscape cookies files
      • HTTP::DetectUserAgent - Yet another HTTP useragent string parser
      • HTTP::DAV - A WebDAV client library for Perl5
      • HTTP::Headers - Class encapsulating HTTP Message headers
      • Notes for HTTP::Headers!
        • But see HTTP::XSHeaders
      • HTTP::Message - HTTP style message (base class)
      • HTTP::Parser::XS - A fast, primitive HTTP request parser
      • HTTP::Parser2::XS - Yet another http parser
      • HTTP::Promise - Asynchronous HTTP Request and Promise
      • HTTP::Promise::Stream::Brotli - Stream Encoder for Brotli Encoding
      • HTTP::Request::Common - Construct common HTTP::Request objects
      • HTTP::Request::FromCurl - Create a HTTP::Request from a curl command line
      • HTTP::Request::Webpush - HTTP Request for web push notifications
      • HTTP::Response - HTTP style response message
      • HTTP::SecureHeaders - Manage security headers with many safe defaults
      • Notes for HTTP::SecureHeaders!
        • https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/HTTP_Headers_Cheat_Sheet.html
      • HTTP::Session2 - HTTP session management - Deprecated
      • HTTP::State - Fast RFC6265bis-draft + CHIPS Cookie Jar
      • Notes for HTTP::State!
        • See RfcGuide for rfc6265
      • HTTP::Status - HTTP Status code processing
      • HTTP::StructuredFieldValues - Encode and decode HTTP Structured Field Values (RFC 9651) in Perl
      • Notes for HTTP::StructuredFieldValues!
        • See RfcGuide for rfc9651
      • HTTP::Tiny - A small, simple, correct HTTP/1.1 client
      • Notes for HTTP::Tiny!
        • Uses IO::Socket::IP (or IO::Socket::INET)
        • Uses HTTP::CookieJar
        • Eschew HTTP::Lite
        • Contains interesting See Also
      • HTTP::Tinyish - HTTP::Tinyish is a wrapper module for HTTP client modules LWP, HTTP::Tiny and HTTP client software curl and wget
      • Notes for HTTP::Tinyish!
        • Backends are searched in the order of: LWP, HTTP::Tiny, curl and wget
        • HTTP::Tinyish will auto-detect if the backend also supports HTTPS, and use the appropriate backend based on the given URL to the request methods
      • HTTP::Tiny::Mech - Wrap a WWW::Mechanize instance in an HTTP::Tiny compatible interface.
      • HTTP::Tiny::UA - Higher-level UA features for HTTP::Tiny
      • HTTP::Validate - Validate and clean HTTP parameter values according to a set of rules
      • HTTP::XSHeaders - Fast XS Header library, replacing HTTP::Headers and HTTP::Headers::Fast
      • LightTCP::Server - A configurable TCP server with HTTP, CGI, and threading support (Pure Perl OOP)
      • LightTCP::SSLclient - SSL/TLS HTTP client with proxy support and certificate pinning
      • LWP::Protocol::https - Provide https support for LWP::UserAgent
      • LWP::Protocol::Net::Curl - The power of libcurl in the palm of your hands!
      • LWP::UserAgent - REST::Client
      • LWP::UserAgent::Throttled - Throttle requests to a site
      • Net::Amazon::Glacier - An implementation of the full Amazon Glacier RESTful 2012-06-01 API.
      • Net::Amazon::Signature::V4 - Implements the Amazon Web Services signature version 4, AWS4-HMAC-SHA256
      • Net::Curl - Perl interface for libcurl
      • Notes for Net::Curl!
        • Contains Net::Curl::Easy, but see Net::Curl::Easier
      • Net::Curl::Easier - Convenience wrapper around Net::Curl::Easy
      • Net::Curl::Easy - Perl interface for curl_easy_* functions
      • Notes for Net::Curl::Easy!
        • But see Net::Curl::Easier
      • Net::Firewall::BlockerHelper - Helps with managing firewalls for banning IPs
      • PApp - Multi-page-state-preserving web applications
      • Notes for PApp!
        • Expects you to use modperl or similar
      • Parse::HTTP::UserAgent - Parse User-Agent strings - Deprecated
      • Notes for Parse::HTTP::UserAgent!
        • Use HTTP::BrowserDetect instead
      • Protocol::Tus - Tus protocol handling
      • Query::Param - Lightweight object interface for parsing and creating query strings
      • Router::Boom - Fast routing engine for web applications
      • Router::R3 - URL router library with high performance
      • Router::Ragel - A high-performance URL router using the Ragel finite state machine
      • Router::Simple - Simple HTTP router
      • Router::XS - Fast URI path to value lookup
      • Slug - XS URL slug generator with custom ops
      • Squid::Guard - Redirector for the Squid web proxy
      • Terse - Lightweight Web Framework. Requires Plack
      • URI - Uniform Resource Identifiers (absolute and relative)
      • URI::_punycode - Encodes Unicode string in Punycode
      • Notes for URI::_punycode!
        • See also Net::IDN::Encode in DnsStuff
      • URI::AnyService - Simple /etc/services-driven URI objects
      • URI::Based - Define a base URI and then generate variations on it
      • URI::Escape - Percent-encode and percent-decode unsafe characters
      • URI::Escape::XS - Drop-In replacement for URI::Escape
      • URI::Router - Highest performance powerful URI router (URI path to value lookup) for HTTP frameworks
      • URI::Shortener - Shorten URIs so that you don't have to rely on external services
      • URI::Split - Parse and compose URI strings
      • URI::Template - Object for handling URI templates (RFC 6570)
      • Notes for URI::Template!
        • See RfcGuide for rfc6570
      • URL::Encode - Encoding and decoding of application/x-www-form-urlencoded encoding
      • URL::Encode::XS - XS implementation of URL::Encode
      • VAPID - Voluntary Application Server Identification
      • Web::Components - MVC pattern for Web::Simple
      • Web::ComposableRequest - Composable request class for web frameworks
      • Web::Simple - A quick and easy way to build simple web applications
      • WWW::Chain - A web request chain
      • WWW::Mechanize - You must, after every action that GETs or POSTs a page, check that Mech succeeded, or all bets are off
    • HTTPServers
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • PlackServer -For what came before PAGI
        • http://nginx.org/en/docs/
        • https://github.com/h2o/picohttpparser
        • http://savage.net.au/misc/Mojo.Wiki.html
      • Amon2 - Lightweight web application framework
      • Amon2::Plugin::Web::CSRFDefender - Anti CSRF filter - Deprecated
      • Apertur::SDK - Official Perl SDK for the Apertur API
      • App::HTTPThis - Export the current directory over HTTP
      • Notes for App::HTTPThis!
        • Actually, this is just a very thin wrapper around Plack::App::DirectoryIndex
      • Async::Microservice - Async HTTP Microservice Moose Role
      • CGI::ACL - Decide whether to allow a client to run this script
      • CGI::Alternatives - Documentation for alternative solutions to CGI.pm
      • ClearPress - Simple, vaguely-MVC web application framework
      • Notes for ClearPress!
        • http://clearpress.net/
      • Furl - Lightning-fast URL fetcher
      • Notes for Furl!
        • Furl is a replacement for LWP
      • Gears - A framework to build web frameworks
      • HTTP::CryptoCookie - Perl extension for encrypted HTTP cookies
      • HTTP::Handy - A tiny HTTP/1.0 server for Perl 5.5.3 and later
      • Notes for HTTP::Handy!
        • See also HP::Handy
      • HTTP::ServerEvent - Create strings for HTTP Server Sent Events
      • HTTP::Server::Simple - Lightweight HTTP server
      • HTTP::Server::Simple::CGI - CGI.pm-style version of HTTP::Server::Simple
      • Hypersonic - Blazing fast HTTP server with JIT-compiled C event loop

      • Notes for Mojolicious!

        • Data::Object
        • FlightRecorder
        • Mojo::UserAgent::Cached
      • Monoceros - PSGI/Plack server with event driven connection manager, preforking workers
      • Net::Async::HTTP - Use HTTP with IO::Async
      • Net::Async::HTTP::Server - Serve HTTP with IO::Async
      • Net::Async::MCP::Server - Async MCP server base class
      • Net::HTTP - Low-level HTTP connection (client)
      • Notes for Net::HTTP!
        • Contains Net::HTTPS
      • Net::HTTP::API - Easily create client for net API
      • Net::HTTP::NB - Non-blocking HTTP client
      • Net::HTTP2 - HTTP/2 in Perl, simplified
      • Net::HTTP2::nghttp2 - Perl XS bindings for nghttp2 HTTP/2 library
      • Net::Respite - Representational State (Perl Iscool) Transfer Engine
      • Net::Server - Extensible, general Perl server engine
      • Notes for Net::Server!
        • Has a massive amount of flexibility
      • NetServer::Generic - Generic TCP/IP server class
      • Net::Server::HTTP - Very basic Net::Server based HTTP server class
      • Notes for Net::Server::HTTP!
        • See also Net::Server::Fork, Net::Server::INET, Net::Server::PreFork, Net::Server::PreForkSimple, Net::Server::MultiType, Net::Server::Single Net::Server::SIG Net::Server::Daemonize Net::Server::Proto
      • Net::Server::PreFork - Net::Server personality
      • Net::Server::SS::PreFork - A hot-deployable variant of Net::Server::PreFork

      • PAGI::Middleware - Base class for PAGI middleware

      • PAGI::Middleware::Builder - DSL for composing PAGI middleware
      • PAGI::Middleware::Session::Store::Cookie - Encrypted client-side session store
      • PAGI::Server - PAGI Reference Server Implementation
      • PAGI::Tools - Application toolkit for the PAGI specification
      • PAGI::Tutorial - A comprehensive guide to building async web applications with PAGI
      • Protocol::HTTP - Very fast HTTP protocol incremental parser and serializer
      • Protocol::HTTP::Compression::Brotli - Brotli compression plugin for Protocol::HTTP
      • Protocol::HTTP::Response - HTTP response class
      • Protocol::HTTP2 - HTTP/2 protocol implementation (RFC 7540)

      • Notes for Shuvgey!
        • See TheRFCGuide for rfc7540
      • PSGI - Perl Web Server Gateway Interface Specification
      • PSGI::Handy - A tiny dependency-free PSGI web framework for Perl 5.005_03 and later
      • Server::Starter - A superdaemon for hot-deploying server programs
      • Session::Storage::Secure - Encrypted, expiring, compressed, serialized session data with integrity
      • Starlet - A simple, high-performance PSGI/Plack HTTP server
      • Starman - High-performance preforking PSGI/Plack web server
      • Task::WebDyne::Apache - Prerequisite modules for running WebDyne under Apache
      • Task::WebDyne::PAGI - Install PAGI dependencies for WebDyne
      • Task::WebDyne::Plack - Optional Plack integration for WebDyne
      • Task::WebDyne::Starman - Optional Starman integration for WebDyne
      • Thunderhorse - A no-compromises brutally-good web framework
      • Trickster - A modern, battle-tested micro-framework for Perl web applications
      • Tvh::Htsp::Client - A Tvheadend HTSP client library written in perl
      • Twiggy - AnyEvent HTTP server for PSGI
      • UniEvent::HTTP::Manager - Extremely fast asynchronous preforking / threading event based web server
      • WebDyne - Dynamic Perl web application framework with template compilation and modular design
      • WWW::Session::Storage::Redis - Redis storage for WWW::Session

    • IoLayers
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
      • IO::Divert - Divert STDOUT to capture and process output
      • IO::Pty - Pseudo TTY object class
      • IO::Stty - Change and print terminal line settings
      • IO::Tty - Low-level allocate a pseudo-Tty, import constants
      • IOLayer::QuotedPrint - PerlIO layer for quoted-printable strings
      • PerlIO::gzip - Perl extension to provide a PerlIO layer to gzip/gunzip
      • PerlIO::utf8_strict - Fast and correct UTF-8 IO
      • PerlIO::Via::Base64 - PerlIO layer for base64 (MIME) encoded strings
      • Notes for PerlIO::Via::Base64!
        • Beware the capital 'V' in the name, unlike the following, which use 'v'
      • PerlIO::via::Include - PerlIO layer for including other files
      • PerlIO::via::LineNumber - PerlIO layer for prefixing line numbers
      • PerlIO::via::MD5 - PerlIO layer for creating an MD5 digest of a file
      • Notes for PerlIO::via::MD5!
        • Uses Digest::MD5
        • Handles b64digest, digest and hexdigest
      • PerlIO::via::Pod - PerlIO layer for extracting plain old documentation
      • PerlIO::via::QuotedPrint - PerlIO layer for quoted-printable strings
      • PerlIO::via::Rotate - PerlIO layer for encoding using rotational deviation (rot13 etc)
      • PerlIO::via::StripHTML - PerlIO layer to strip HTML tags from an input file
      • PerlIO::via::UnComment - PerlIO layer for removing comments
      • PerlIO::via::UnPod - PerlIO layer for removing plain old documentation
      • PerlIO::via:xz - PerlIO layer for XZ (de)compression
      • Notes for PerlIO::via:xz!
        • Uses IO::Compress::Xz and IO::Uncompress::UnXz

        • See RfcGuide for rfc7049
      • CBOR::XS - Concise Binary Object Representation (CBOR, RFC7049)
      • Class::JSON_Object - Role for Class::JSON_Object
      • Cpanel::JSON::XS - cPanel fork of JSON::XS, fast and correct serializing
      • Notes for Cpanel::JSON::XS!
        • Recommended despite the old comments below
        • Marc Lehmann has (long ago?) said bad things about this module. See the POD for JSON::SIMD, under Interoperability as above
        • It can be necessary. See ~/bin/test.cpanel.json.xs.type.pl
      • Data::Ref::JSON - Provide Perl reference code to access values in an arbitrary JSON data structure
      • JSON::Any - Wrapper Class for the various JSON classes - Deprecated
      • JSON::API - Module to interact with a JSON API
      • JSON::Create - Encodes Perl variables into JSON
      • JSON::GRDDL - Transform JSON to RDF
      • JSON::Hyper - Extract links from JSON via a schema
      • JSON::JSONFold - Compact, readable JSON formatting
      • JSON::JsonModel - Perl Runtime for JSON Model
      • JSON::Lines - Parse JSONLines with perl
      • JSON::LINQ - LINQ-style query interface for JSON and JSONL files
      • JSON::MaybeXS - Use Cpanel::JSON::XS with a fallback to JSON::XS and JSON::PP
      • JSON::Path - Implements JSONPath, an XPath-like language for searching JSON-like structures
      • Notes for JSON::Path!
        • JSONPath is described at http://goessner.net/articles/JsonPath/
      • JSON::Pointer - A Perl implementation of JSON Pointer (RFC6901)
      • JSON::PP - JSON::XS compatible pure-Perl module
      • JSON::Relaxed - An extension of JSON that allows for better human-readability
      • JSON::RPC - JSON RPC 2.0 Server Implementation
      • JSON::RPC::Common - Transport agnostic JSON RPC helper objects
      • JSON::RPC::Dispatcher - A JSON-RPC 2.0 server
      • JSON::RPC::Simple - Simple JSON-RPC client and dispatcher (WD 1.1 subset only currently)
      • JSON::RPC::Simple::Lite - A simple and lite JSON-RPC client
      • JSON::RPC::Spec - Yet another JSON-RPC 2.0 Implementation
      • JSON::RPC2 - Transport-independent implementation of JSON-RPC 2.0
      • JSON::Schema - Validate JSON against a schema
      • JSON::Schema::AsType - Generates Type::Tiny types out of JSON schemas
      • JSON::Schema::Generate - Generate JSON Schemas from data!
      • JSON::Schema::Modern - Validate data against a schema using a JSON Schema
      • JSON::Schema::Tiny - Validate data against a schema, minimally
      • JSON::Schema::Types - Create Type::Tiny types defined by JSON Schemas
      • JSON::Schema::Validate - Lean, recursion-safe JSON Schema validator (Draft 2020-12)
      • JSON::SIMD - JSON serialising/deserialising, done correctly and faster
      • JSON::Structure - JSON Structure validation library for Perl
      • JSON::Syck - JSON is YAML (but consider using JSON::XS instead!)
      • Notes for JSON::Syck!
        • See also YAML::Syck
      • JSON::T - Transform JSON using JsonT
      • Notes for JSON::T!
        • http://goessner.net/articles/jsont/
      • JSON::Tiny - Minimalistic JSON. No dependencies
      • JSON::ToHTML - Render JSON-based Perl datastructures as HTML tables
      • JSON::Typist - Replace mushy strings and numbers with rigidly typed replacements
      • JSON::Validator - Validate data against a JSON schema
      • JSON::WebToken - JSON Web Token (JWT) implementation
      • JSON::XS - JSON serialising/deserialising, done correctly and fast
      • JSON::YY - Fast JSON encoder/decoder with document manipulation API, backed by yyjson
      • JSONL::Subset - Extract a percentage of lines from a JSONL file
      • JSV - A perl implementation of JSON Schema (draft-04) validator
      • JSV::Validator - A perl implementation of JSON Schema (draft-04) validator
      • mb::JSON - A simple JSON parser for multibyte string
      • RPC::JSON - JSON-RPC Client Library
      • Schema::Validator - Tools for validating and loading Schema.org vocabulary definitions
      • Voxgig::Struct - Perl port of the canonical TypeScript implementation

    • LaTeX
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
      • BeamerReveal - Manage the media files generated for the Reveal HTML presentation
      • Notes for BeamerReveal!
        • Converts LaTeX Beamer (PDF-centric) presentations into Reveal.js (Web-centric) HTML presentations
        • In this ecosystem, the .rvl file is a Reveal Configuration/Manifest file. It acts as the "instruction manual" for the conversion process
      • LaTeX::Driver - Latex driver
      • LaTeX::Easy::Templates - Easily format content into PDF/PS/DVI with LaTeX templates
      • Notes for LaTeX::Easy::Templates!
        • Format text content, living in a Perl data structure, into printer-ready documents (PDF/Postscript/DVI)
        • Documents how to install LaTeX
        • Uses Text::Xslate
      • LaTeX::Encode - Encode characters for LaTeX formatting
      • LaTeX::Replicase - Perl extension implementing a minimalistic engine for filling real TeX-LaTeX files that act as templates
      • LaTeX::Table - Perl extension for the automatic generation of LaTeX tables
      • LaTeX::ToUnicode - Convert LaTeX commands to Unicode
      • Template::Latex - LaTeX support for the Template Toolkit

        • Yes, licence is spelled that way in Australia
      • App::Licensecheck - Functions for a simple license checker for source files

      • Enterprise::Licence - Licence or License
      • License::SPDX - Object for SPDX licenses handling
      • Module::LicenseScanner - This file is used to gather all CPAN licenses from third party dependencies
      • Regexp::Pattern::License - Regular expressions for legal licenses
      • Software::Copyright - Copyright class

      • Software::Policies - Create policy files: CODE_OF_CONDUCT, CONTRIBUTING, FUNDING, GOVERNANCE, SECURITY, SUPPORT, etc
      • Software::Policy::CodeOfConduct - Generate a Code of Conduct policy

      • App::Gimei - A CLI app for Data::Gimei, a module generating fake Japanese names and addresses

      • CGI::Lingua - Create a multilingual web page
      • Convert::CEGH - Coptic/Ethiopic/Greek/Hebrew Gematria & Transliteration
      • Convert::Number::Coptic - Convert Between Western and Coptic Numeral Systems
      • Convert::Number::Digits - Convert Digits Between the Scripts of Unicode
      • Convert::Number::Ethiopic - Convert Between Western and Ethiopic Numeral Systems
      • Notes for Convert::Number::Ethiopic!
        • See also https://ethiopic.com/
      • Convert::Number::Roman - Convert Between Western and Roman Numeral Systems
      • Date::Ethiopic - ICalendar for the Ethiopic Calendar System

      • Data::HanConvert - The data for converting between traditional and simplified Chinese languages
      • Data::Validate::Japanese - Validate Japanese Input
      • DateTime::Calendar::Coptic - DateTime Module for the Coptic Calendar System.
      • I18N::Langinfo - Query locale information
      • I18N::LangTags - Functions for dealing with RFC3066-style language tags
      • Notes for I18N::LangTags!
        • See TheRFCGuide for rfc3066
      • I18N::LangTags::Detect - Detect the user's language preferences
      • I18N::LangTags::List - Tags and names for human languages
      • Jacode - Perl program for Japanese character code conversion
      • Jacode4e - Converts Character Encodings for Enterprise in Japan
      • Jacode4e::RoundTrip - Converts JIS X 0213 Encodings with Round-Trip
      • Koha::Contrib::Tamil - Various scripts to manage Koha instances
      • Lingua::Boolean::Tiny - A smaller Lingua::Boolean, with support for more languages
      • Lingua::AFR::Numbers - Converts numeric values into their Afrikaans equivalents
      • Lingua::AFR::Word2Num - Converts Afrikaans words into numbers
      • Lingua::DEU::Word2Num - Word 2 Number conversion in DEU
      • Lingua::EN::ABC - American, British, and Canadian English
      • Lingua::EN::AddressParse - Extract components of a street address from free format text
      • Lingua::EN::GivenNames - An SQLite database of derivations of English given names
      • Lingua::EN::Infinitive - Determine the infinitive form of a conjugated word
      • Lingua::EN::Inflexion - Inflect English nouns, verbs, adjectives, and articles
      • Lingua::EN::NameCase - Correctly case a person's name from UPPERCASE or lowcase
      • Lingua::EN::NameParse - Extract the components of a person or couples full name, from free form text
      • Lingua::EN::Nickname - Genealogical nickname matching (Liz=Beth)
      • Lingua::EN::Segmenter - Subdivide texts into passages that represent subtopics
      • Lingua::EN::StopWords - Typical stop words for an English corpus
      • Notes for Lingua::EN::StopWords!
        • See Lingua::StopWords below
      • Lingua::EN::StopWordList - A sorted list of English stop words
      • Lingua::EN::Titlecase - Titlecase English words by traditional editorial rules
      • Lingua::EN::TitleParse - Parse titles in people's names
      • Lingua::EN::Titlecase::Simple - John Gruber's headline capitalization script
      • Notes for Lingua::EN::Titlecase::Simple!
        • Lingua::EN::Titlecase provides a much more heavyweight, modular solution for the same problem
      • Lingua::famibeib - Module to interact with the famibeib artificial language
      • Lingua::FR::Numbers::Ordinate - Go from cardinal number (4) to ordinal ("4Ã¨me")
      • Lingua::Interset - DZ Interset is a universal morphosyntactic feature set to which all tagsets of all corpora/languages can be mapped
      • Lingua::JA::KanjiTable - User-Defined Character Properties for Joyo Kanji and Jinmeiyo Kanji
      • Lingua::JA::Moji - Handle many kinds of Japanese characters
      • Lingua::JA::Name::Splitter - Split a Japanese name into given and family
      • Lingua::JPN::Word2Num - Word to number conversion in Japanese
      • Lingua::ManagementSpeak - Tool to generate managerial-sounding text and full documents
      • Lingua::NameUtils - Identify given/family names and capitalize correctly
      • Lingua::RO::Numbers - Convert numeric values into their Romanian string equivalents and viceversa
      • Lingua-RU-Numeral - Perl extension for generate Russian wording of numerals from the natural numbers and 0 (zero)
      • Lingua::RU::Num2Word - Numbers to words in Russian (without currency, but with specified gender)
      • Lingua::RUS::Num2Word - Converts numbers to money sum in words (in Russian roubles)
      • Lingua::Stem - Stemming of words
      • Lingua::Stem::Snowball - Perl interface to Snowball stemmers
      • Lingua::StopWords - Stop words for several languages
      • Notes for Lingua::StopWords!
        • See Lingua::EN::StopWords above
      • Lingua::String - Class to contain a string in many different languages
      • Lingua::Text - Class to contain text in many different languages
      • Locale::Maketext - Framework for localization
      • Locale::Maketext::TPJ13 - Article about software localization
      • String::Equivalence::Amharic - Normalization Utilities for Amharic
      • String::ProperCase::Surname - Converts Surnames to Proper Case
      • Task::Lingua::Num2Word - Install all PetaMem number-to-word conversion modules
      • Task::Lingua::PetaMem - Install all PetaMem Lingua number conversion modules
      • Task::Lingua::Word2Num - Install all PetaMem word-to-number conversion modules
      • Text::DoubleMetaphone - Phonetic encoding of words.
      • Text::Metaphone - A modern soundex. Phonetic encoding of words
      • Text::Metaphone::Amharic - The Metaphone Algorithm for Amharic
      • Text::Phonetic::Metaphone - Metaphone algorithm
      • Text::TransMetaphone - Translingual Phonetic Encoding of Words
      • Text::WagnerFischer::Amharic - The Wagner-Fischer Algorithm for Amharic
      • Unicode::Japanese - Convert encoding of Japanese text
      • Win32::Locale - Get the current MSWin locale or language

      • App::RPM::Spec::License - Base class for rpm-spec-license tool
      • Linux::Clone - An interface to the linux clone, unshare, setns, pivot_root and kcmp syscalls
      • Linux::Event - Front door for the Linux::Event ecosystem
      • Linux::Event::Clock - Cached monotonic time and deadline math for Linux::Event schedulers
      • Linux::Event::Connect - Non-blocking outbound socket connect for Linux::Event
      • Linux::Event::Fork - Minimal async child spawning on top of Linux::Event
      • Linux::Event::Listen - Listening sockets for Linux::Event
      • Linux::Event::Loop - Linux-native event loop (epoll + timerfd + signalfd + eventfd + pidfd)
      • Linux::Event::Stream - Buffered, backpressure-aware I/O for nonblocking file descriptors
      • Linux::Event::Timer - Thin wrapper around Linux::FD::Timer (timerfd)
      • Linux::Event::Watcher - Mutable watcher handle for Linux::Event::Loop
      • Linux::FD - Linux specific special filehandles
      • Notes for Linux::FD!
        • Contains Linux::FD::Event, Linux::FD::Signal, Linux::FD::Timer
      • Linux::FD::Mem - Memory file descriptors
      • Linux::Inotify - Classes for supporting inotify in Linux Kernel >= 2.6.13
      • Linux::Inotify2 - Scalable directory/file change notification
      • Linux::Perl - Linux system calls with pure Perl
      • Linux::Prctl - Perl extension for controlling process characteristics
      • Linux::Socket::Accept4 - Accept4(2) bindings for Perl5
      • Parse::RPM::Spec - Perl extension to parse RPM spec files
      • RPM::Query - Perl object overlay of the RPM query command
      • Sys::OsRelease - Read operating system details from standard /etc/os-release file
      • Notes for Sys::OsRelease!
        • Under Debian that file is /usr/lib/os-release

      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • For Locale::TextDomain::FAQ etc
      • Convert::Number::Digits - Convert Digits Between the Scripts of Unicode.
      • Notes for Convert::Number::Digits!
        • Methods included
      • Data::Localize::Format::Maketext - Maketext Formatter
      • Notes for Data::Localize::Format::Maketext!
        • Warning: POD is void
      • Data::Localize::Format::Sprintf - Format strings using regular sprintf
      • DateTime::Locale - Localization support for DateTime.pm
      • Locale::AU - Abbreviations for territory and state identification in Australia and vice versa
      • Locale::CA - Two letter codes for province identification in Canada and vice versa
      • Locale::Codes - A distribution of many modules to handle locale codes
      • Notes for Locale::Codes!
        • For example, there are codes associated with different countries, different currencies, different languages, etc
      • Locale::Country - Module for dealing with country code sets
      • Locale::Country::Multilingual - To translate country names
      • Locale::gettext - Message handling functions
      • Locale::Intl - A Web Intl.Locale Class Implementation
      • Locale::Maketext - Framework for localization
      • Locale::Maketext::Lexicon - Use other catalog formats in Maketext
      • Locale::Maketext::Lexicon::DB - Dynamically load lexicon from a database table
      • Locale::Maketext::Lexicon::Gettext - PO and MO file parser for Maketext
      • Locale::Maketext::Utils - Adds some utility functionality and failure handling to Local::Maketext handles
      • Locale::Messages - Gettext Like Message Retrieval
      • Locale::Object - An object-oriented representation of locale information.
      • Notes for Locale::Object!
        • Contains Locale::Object::Continent, Locale::Object::Country, Locale::Object::Currency, Locale::Object::CurrencyConverter, Locale::Object::DB, Locale::Object::Language
      • Locale::Places - Translate places between different languages using http://download.geonames.org/
      • Locale::Simple - Functions for translate text based on gettext data, also in JavaScript
      • Locale::SubCountry - Convert state, province, county etc. names to/from ISO 3166-2 codes, get all states in a country
      • Locale::TextDomain - Perl Interface to Uniforum Message Translation
      • Notes for Locale::TextDomain!
        • Included in https://metacpan.org/dist/libintl-perl
        • The latter also contains Locale::Util - Portable l10n and i10n functions, and many others
      • Locale::TextDomain::OO::Extract - Extracts internationalization data
      • Notes for Locale::TextDomain::OO::Extract!
        • Includes a long list of examples
      • Locale::Unicode - Unicode Locale Identifier compliant with BCP47 and CLDR
      • Notes for Locale::Unicode!
        • Locale::Unicode::Boolean Boolean: Representation Class
        • Locale::Unicode::Exception Locale: Unicode Exception C
      • Locale::Unicode::Data - Unicode CLDR SQL Data
      • Locale::US - Two letter codes for state identification in the United States and vice versa
      • Locale::VersionedMessages - Handle all aspects of the localization process
      • Telugu::TGC - Tailored Grapheme Clusters for Telugu language
      • Unicode::Japanese - Convert encoding of japanese text

      • eris - eris exists to transform unstructured, chaotic log data into structured messages
      • Log::Abstraction - Logging Abstraction Layer
      • Log::Any - Bringing loggers and listeners together
      • Log::Any::Adapter::Base - No description
      • Log::Any::Adapter::MacOS::OSLog - Log to macOS' unified logging system
      • Log::Any::Adapter::MojoLog - Log::Any integration for Mojo::Log
      • Log::Any::Adapter::Util - Common utility functions for Log::Any
      • Log::Dispatch - Dispatches messages to one or more outputs
      • Log::Dispatch::Email - Base class for objects that send log messages via email
      • Log::Dispatch::Email::Mailer - Log::Dispatch::Email subclass that sends mail using Email::Mailer
      • Log::Dispatch::TAP - Log to TAP output
      • Log::Dispatch::UnixSyslog - Log events to syslog with Unix::Syslog
      • Log::Dispatch::WarnDie - Log standard Perl warnings and errors
      • Log::Dispatch::XML - Collect one or more messages in XML format
      • Log::Dispatchouli - A simple wrapper around Log::Dispatch
      • Notes for Log::Dispatchouli!
        • Contains Log::Fmt, etc
      • Log::Fmt::XS - XS components to speed up Log::Fmt
      • Log::Handler - Log messages to several outputs
      • Notes for Log::Handler!
        • This is my favourite
      • Log::Handler::Plugin::DBI - A plugin for Log::Handler using Log::Hander::Output::DBI
      • Log::JSON::Lines - Log in JSONLines format
      • Notes for Log::JSON::Lines!
        • https://jsonlines.org
      • Log::Journald - Send messages to a systemd journal
      • Log::Log4perl - Log4j implementation for Perl
      • Notes for Log::Log4perl!
        • Warning: Use this if you want maximum complexity in your code
      • Log::Log4perl::Appender::AmazonSES - Send via Amazon SES (SMTP over TLS)
      • Log::Log4perl::Appender::TAP - Append to TAP output
      • Log::Log4perl::Config::YamlConfigurator - Reads Log4perl YAML configurations
      • Log::LogMethods - Writes your logging code for you!
      • Log::Mini - It's a very simple logger which can log your messages to a file or STDERR
      • Log::Report - Report a problem, with exceptions and translation support
      • Log::Report::Lexicon - Translation component of Log::Report
      • Log::Report::Optional - Pick Log::Report or ::Minimal
      • Log::Report::Template - Template Toolkit with translations
      • Log::Syslog::Constants - Contains syslog priority constants as defined in RFC3164
      • Notes for Log::Syslog::Constants!
        • See TheRFCGuide for rfc3164
      • Log::Syslog::Fast - Send syslog messages over TCP, UDP, or UNIX sockets with minimal CPU overhead
      • Log::Timer - Track nested timing information
      • Log::WarnDie - Log standard Perl warnings and errors on a log handler
      • LWP::ConsoleLogger - LWP tracing and debugging
      • LWP::CurlLog - Log LWP::UserAgent / HTTP::Tiny requests as curl commands
      • No::Worries::Log - Logging without worries
      • No::Worries::Syslog - Syslog handling without worries
      • Parse::Syslog::Line - Simple syslog line parser
      • Readonly::Values::Syslog - Syslog Constants
      • Sys::Syslog - Perl interface to the UNIX syslog(3) calls
      • SyslogScan::Daemon - Watch log files
      • SyslogScan::Daemon::BlacklistDetector - Notice when a mail server has been blacklisted
      • SyslogScan::Daemon::SpamDetector - Notice spammers in the log files

        • [Topic]

        • [Topic]

      • Specifications and Tutorials - http://neilb.org/reviews/markdown.html
      • Notes for Specifications and Tutorials!
        • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::MultiMarkdown
        • https://fletcherpenney.net/multimarkdown/
        • https://www.markdowntutorial.com/
      • App::Greple::md - Greple module for Markdown syntax highlighting
      • App::md2html - Blah blah blah
      • App::mdee - Markdown, Easy on the Eyes
      • Chandra::Markdown - Render Markdown in Chandra apps
      • CommonMark - Interface to the CommonMark C library
      • Liveman - Compiler from Markdown to tests and documentation
      • Markdent - An event-based Markdown parser toolkit
      • Markdent::Dialect::GitHub - Markdown extensions implemented on GitHub
      • Markdown::ODF - Create ODF documents from Markdown
      • Markdown::Parser - Markdown Parser Only
      • Markdown::Perl - Very configurable Markdown processor written in pure Perl, supporting the CommonMark spec and many extensions
      • Markdown::Render - Render markdown as HTML
      • Markdown::Simple - Markdown to HTML converter
      • Notes for Markdown::Simple!
        • Code generated by co-pilot
        • Uses XS
      • mdcat - Fancy cat for Markdown (that is, CommonMark)
      • Notes for mdcat!
        • Release binaries: https://github.com/swsnr/mdcat/releases/
        • An add-on which generates docs from code comments: https://github.com/EvanCarroll/p5-doc-comments
      • Mermaid - Mermaid is a JavaScript based diagramming and charting tool that takes Markdown-inspired text definitions and creates diagrams dynamically in the browser
      • Notes for Mermaid!
        • Warning: It outputs to an iFrame within your browser
      • Regexp::Common::Markdown - Markdown Common Regular Expressions
      • Notes for Regexp::Common::Markdown!
        • Used by Markdown::Parser
      • Term::ANSIEncode - A markup language to generate basic ANSI text
      • Text::HTML::Turndown - Convert HTML to Markdown
      • Text::Markdown - Convert Markdown syntax to (X)HTML. Buggy
      • Text::MarkdownAdoc - Convert Markdown (GFM + kramdown) to AsciiDoc
      • Text::Markdown::Discount - Fast function for converting markdown to HTML (requires C compiler)
      • Text::Markdown::Hoedown - Hoedown for Perl5
      • Notes for Text::Markdown::Hoedown!
        • https://github.com/hoedown/hoedown
      • Text::Markup - Parse text markup into HTML
      • Notes for Text::Markup!
        • This distribution includes support for 15 markup formats
      • Text::Markup::Asciidoc - Asciidoc parser for Text::Markup
      • Text::MultiMarkdown - Convert MultiMarkdown syntax to (X)HTML
    • MarlinStuff
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
      • Marlin - ð Pretty fast class builder with most Moo/Moose features ð

      • Marlin::X::UndefTolerant - Marlin extension to make your constructor forgive undefs

      • GraphViz2 - A wrapper for AT&T's Graphviz

      • GraphViz2::Marpa::Extractor - Extracts a simplified AST from GraphViz2::Marpa tree
      • GraphViz2::Marpa::PathUtils - Provide various analyses of Graphviz dot files

      • Notes for JSON::Pointer::Marpa!
        • See TheRFCGuide for rfc6901
        • JSON Pointer identifies a specified value within a JSON document, and is similar to XPath
      • Marpa::R2 - Release 2 of Marpa
      • MarpaX::Grammar::GraphViz2 - Convert a Marpa grammar into an image
      • MarpaX::Grammar::Parser - Converts a Marpa grammar into a tree using Tree::DAG_Node
      • MarpaX::Languages::Dash - A Marpa-based parser for the DASH language
      • MarpaX::Languages::SVG::Parser - A nested SVG parser, using XML::SAX and Marpa::R2
      • Unicode bracketing - https://www.unicode.org/notes/tn39/

      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]

        • [Topic]

        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • For the Octave Manual
