      • Alien::Bit - Find or install the Bit library
      • Alien::Qhull - Build and Install the Qhull library
      • Algorithm::Combinatorics - Efficient generation of combinatorial sequences
      • Algorithm::Kademlia - Pure Perl implementation of the Kademlia DHT algorithm
      • Algorithm::Loops - Looping constructs: NestedLoops, MapCar*, Filter, and NextPermute*
      • Algorithm::FastPermute - Rapid generation of permutations
      • Algorithm::Permute - Handy and fast permutation with object oriented interface
      • Algorithm::TimelinePacking - Arrange time intervals into non-overlapping lines
      • App::ArithmeticsUtils - Utilities related to arithmetics
      • App::GUI::Cellgraph - Draw pattern by cellular automata
      • App::GUI::Harmonograph - Sculpting beautiful circular drawings
      • App::GUI::Juliagraph - Drawing Mandelbrot and Julia fractals
      • bigint - Transparent big integer support for Perl
      • Notes for bigint!
        • Contains bigfloat, bigint, bignum, bigrat
      • Bijection - Bijection of an integer
      • Bijection::XS - Bijection of an integer faster
      • Bit::Set - Perl interface for bitset functions from the 'bit' C library
      • Bit::Vector - Efficient bit vector, set of integers and "big int" math library
      • Notes for Bit::Vector!
        • If true & false are a problem, see https://blogs.perl.org/users/dean/2026/05/installing-bitvector-on-debian-13-trixie.html
      • List::PowerSet - Generate the power set of a list
      • Math::AnyNum - Arbitrary size precision for integers, rationals, floating-points and complex numbers
      • Math::BaseCalc - Convert numbers between various bases
      • Math::Base::Convert - Very fast base to base conversion
      • Notes for Math::Base::Convert!
        • See also Math::BaseConvert below
      • Math::BaseConvert - Fast functions to CoNVert between number Bases
      • Notes for Math::BaseConvert!
        • See also Math::Base::Convert above
      • Math::Basic - Basic math
      • Math::Basic::XS - The great new Math::Basic::XS!
      • Math::BigInt - Arbitrary size integer math package
      • Notes for Math::BigInt!
        • Contains Math::BigFloat and Math::BigRat etc
      • Math::BigInt::BitVect - A math backend library based on Bit::Vector
      • Math::BigInt::FastCalc - Math::BigInt::Calc with some XS for more speed
      • Math::BigInt::GMP - Backend library for Math::BigInt etc. based on GMP
      • Math::BigInt::GMPz - A math backend library based on Math::GMPz
      • Math::BigInt::Lite - What Math::BigInts are before they become big
      • Math::BigInt::Pari - A math backend library based on Math::Pari
      • Math::BigRat - Arbitrary size rational number math package
      • Math::Cephes - Perl interface to the cephes math library
      • Math::Complex - Complex numbers and associated mathematical functions
      • Math::FakeBfloat16 - Emulate bf16 floating point arithmetic
      • Math::FakeDD - DoubleDouble precision arithmetic for all architectures
      • Math::FakeFloat16 - Emulate _Float16 floating point arithmetic.
      • Math::BigFloat - Arbitrary size floating point math package
      • Math::Cartesian::Product - Generate the Cartesian product of zero or more lists
      • Math::Combinatorics - Perform combinations and permutations on lists
      • Math::Disarrange::List - Generate all the disarrangements of a list
      • Math::Float32 - Perl interface to float (aka _Float32) floating point type
      • Math::Formula - Expressions on steroids
      • Math::GMatrix - Extension of Math::Matrix for (2D graphics-)vector manipulation
      • Math::GMP - High speed arbitrary size integer math
      • Math::GMPf - Perl interface to the GMP library's floating point (mpf) functions
      • Math::GMPq - Perl interface to the GMP library's rational (mpq) functions
      • Math::GMPn - Fixed length integer arithmetic
      • Math::GMPz - Perl interface to the GMP library's integer (mpz) functions
      • Math::GMP_OLOAD - Assist in the overloading of Math::GMP objects
      • Math::GSL::Alien - Easy installation of the GSL library
      • Notes for Math::GSL::Alien!
        • GSL means Gnu Scientific Library
        • It has a See Also section
      • Math::Int128 - Manipulate 128 bits integers in Perl
      • Math::Int64 - Manipulate 64 bits integers in Perl
      • Math::Int64::C_API::Sample - C API Usage sample for Math::Int64
      • Math::Interpolator - Interpolate between lazily-evaluated points
      • Math::MPC - Perl interface to the MPC (multi precision complex) library
      • Math::MPFI - Perl interface to the MPFI (interval arithmetic) library
      • Math::MPFR - Perl interface to the MPFR (floating point) library.
      • Math::NLopt - Math::NLopt - Perl interface to the NLopt optimization library
      • Math::Pari - Perl interface to PARI
      • Math::Permute::List - Generate all permutations of a list
      • Math::Permute::Lists - Generate all the permutations of zero or more nested lists
      • Math::Permute::Partitions - Generate all the permutations of a partitioned list.
      • Math::Permutation - Pure Perl implementation of functions related to the permutations
      • Math::PlanePath - Points on a path through the 2-D plane
      • Math::Polygon - Class for maintaining polygon data
      • Math::Polynomial - Perl class for polynomials in one variable
      • Math::Polynomial::Solve - Find the roots of polynomial equations
      • Math::Prime::FastSieve - Generate a list of all primes less than or equal to $n
      • Math::Prime::Util - Utilities related to prime numbers, including fast sieves and factoring
      • Math::Prime::Util::GMP - Utilities related to prime numbers and factoring, using GMP
      • Math::ReedSolomon::Encoder - Calculate Reed-Solomon Error Correction Codes
      • Math::Round - Perl extension for rounding numbers
      • Math::RPN - Perl extension for Reverse Polish Math Expression Evaluation
      • Math::SigFigs - Do math with correct handling of significant figures
      • Math::Subsets::List - Generate all the subsets of a list
      • Math::Symbolic - Symbolic calculations
      • Notes for Math::Symbolic!
        • This module comes with a huge list of modules
      • Math::Symbolic::Custom::Factor - Re-arrange a Math::Symbolic expression into a product of factors
      • Math::Symbolic::Custom::Collect - Collect up Math::Symbolic expressions
      • Math::Symbolic::Custom::CollectSimplify - Simplify Math::Symbolic expressions using Math::Symbolic::Custom::Collect
      • Math::Symbolic::Custom::Equation - Work with equations of Math::Symbolic expressions
      • Math::Symbolic::Custom::Matrix - Matrix routines for Math::Symbolic
      • Math::Symbolic::Custom::Polynomial - Polynomial routines for Math::Symbolic
      • Math::Symbolic::Custom::ToShorterString - Shorter string representations of Math::Symbolic trees
      • Math::Symbolic::Custom::ToTallString - Pretty-print Math::Symbolic expressions
      • Math::Transform::List - Generate specified transformations of a list
      • Math::Trig - Trigonometric functions
      • Math::Vector::Similarity - Cosine similarity, euclidean distance and other vector comparison functions
      • Math::VectorReal - Module to handle 3D Vector Mathematics
      • Number::AnyBase - Converts decimals to and from any alphabet of any size (for shortening IDs, URLs etc.)
      • Number::Equation - Track how a number is calculated progammatically
      • Number::Equation::XS - Track how a number is calculated progammatically
      • Number::Fraction - Perl extension to model fractions
      • Numeric::Matrix - SIMD-accelerated 2D matrices with BLAS GEMM
      • Numeric::Vector - SIMD-accelerated numeric vectors
      • Parse::RPN - A minimalist RPN parser/processor (a little like FORTH)
      • PERLANCAR::Permute::Named - Permute multiple-valued key-value pairs
      • Permute::Named - Permute multiple-valued key-value pairs
      • Permute::Named::Iter - Permute multiple-valued key-value pairs
      • Permute::Unnamed - Permute multiple-valued lists
      • Permute::Unnamed::Iter - Permute multiple-valued lists
      • Qhull - Interface to the Qhull convex hull, Delauny triangulation, Voronoi diagram software suite
      • Shannon::Entropy - Calculate the Shannon entropy H of a given input string
      • Shannon::Entropy::XS - Calculate the Shannon entropy H of a given input string faster
      • smallnum - Transparent "SmallNumber" support for Perl
      • smallnum::XS - Faster transparent "SmallNumber" support for Perl
      • uinteger - Like "use integer", but unsigned
    • MeasureMetrics
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • Query, visualize, alert on, and explore your metrics, logs, and traces wherever they are stored
        • InfluxDB
        • Time Series
        • Perl application observability platform
        • StatsD Metrics Export Specification
        • Telegraf
      • Devel::Cover - Code coverage metrics for Perl
      • Devel::Cover::Report::Codecovbash - Generate a JSON file to be uploaded with the codecov bash script
      • Grafana - Is an open-source analytics and visualisation platform. It lets you create dashboards that visualise data from various sources
      • Notes for Grafana!
        • https://theweeklychallenge.org/blog/grafana-prometheus/ - For sample code
      • Lab::Measurement::Manual - API documentation manual for Lab::Measurement
      • Notes for Lab::Measurement::Manual!
        • Lab::Measurement is a huge package with support for many types of hardware
      • Linux::Statm::Tiny - Simple access to Linux /proc/../statm
      • Metrics::Any - Abstract collection of monitoring metrics
      • Metrics::Any::Adapter::Prometheus - A metrics reporting adapter for Prometheus
      • Metrics::Any::Adapter::Statsd - A metrics reporting adapter for statsd
      • Measure::Everything - Log::Any for Stats
      • Net::Async::Statsd - Asynchronous API for Etsy's statsd protocol
      • Net::Prometheus - Export monitoring metrics for prometheus
      • Net::Statsd - Perl client for Etsy's statsd daemon
      • Notes for Net::Statsd!
        • This module was forked from Net::Statsd::Tiny
      • Net::Statsd::Lite - A lightweight StatsD client that supports multimetric packets
      • Net::Statsd::Tiny - A tiny StatsD client that supports multimetric packets
      • Perl::Metrics::Halstead - Compute Halstead complexity metrics
      • Notes for Perl::Metrics::Halstead!
        • https://ology.github.io/2019/03/19/halstead-software-complexity-of-perl-code/
      • Prometheus - Is an open-source systems monitoring and alerting toolkit
      • Notes for Prometheus!
        • https://theweeklychallenge.org/blog/grafana-prometheus/ - For sample code
      • SlapbirdAPM::Agent::Dancer2 - The SlapbirdAPM user-agent for Dancer2/Mojo/Plack applications
      • Time::Progress - Elapsed and estimated finish time reporting
    • MemoryUsage
      • Devel::Size - Perl extension for finding the memory usage of Perl variables
      • File::Map - Memory mapping made simple and safe
      • Notes for File::Map!
        • Contains map_anonymous (for when your processes have a parent-child relationship)
      • Linux::Smaps - A Perl interface to /proc/PID/smaps
      • Linux::Smaps::Tiny - A minimal and fast alternative to Linux::Smaps
      • Memory::Usage - Tools to determine actual memory usage
      • Memchmark - Check memory consumption
      • SysV::SharedMem - SysV Shared memory made easy
      • Notes for SysV::SharedMem!
        • But see the caveats discussed in https://metacpan.org/pod/POSIX::RT::SharedMem
      • Task::MemManager - A memory allocated and manager for low level code in Perl
      • Task::MemManager::CMalloc - Allocates buffers using C's malloc
      • Task::MemManager::Device - Device-specific memory management extensions for Task::MemManager
      • Task::MemManager::View - Provides convenient views for Task::MemManager buffers
      • Task::MemManager::View::PDL - Create PDL views of Task::MemManager buffers

      • Acme::AutoLoad - Automatically load uninstalled CPAN modules on the fly
      • Acme::Damn - 'Unbless' Perl objects
      • Acme::ExtUtils::XSOne::Test::Calculator - A scientific calculator demonstrating ExtUtils::XSOne
      • Acme::Intraweb - Allows you to use modules not yet installed on your system
      • Affix - A Foreign Function Interface eXtension
      • Aion - A postmodern object system for Perl 5, such as âMouseâ, âMooseâ, âMooâ, âMoâ and âMâ, but with improvements
      • Aion::Fs - Utilities for the file system: reading, writing, searching, replacing files, etc
      • aliased - Use shorter versions of class names
      • Alien - External libraries wrapped up for your viewing pleasure!
      • Alien::Base - Base classes for Alien:: modules
      • Alien::Base::ModuleBuild - A Module::Build subclass for building Alien:: modules and their libraries
      • Alien::Build - Build external dependencies for use in CPAN
      • Notes for Alien::Build!
        • This module contains a huge list of plugins, including Alien::Base
      • Alien::Build::Plugin::Build::CMake - CMake plugin for Alien::Build
      • Alien::CMake - Build and make available CMake library - http://cmake.org/
      • Alien::cmake3 - Find or download or build cmake 3
      • Alien::cmake4 - Find or download or build cmake 4
      • Notes for Alien::cmake4!
        • Contains an important See also
      • Alien::DuckDB - Find or build DuckDB
      • Notes for Alien::DuckDB!
        • See also FFI::Platypus::Record below
      • Alien::fpm - Convert directories, rpms, python eggs, rubygems, and more to rpms, debs, solaris packages and more
      • Alien::libwebsockets - Find or build libwebsockets C library
      • Alien::libui - Build and Install libui: A portable GUI library
      • Alien::TinyCC - Retrieve useful information about the Alien installation of tcc
      • Alien::TinyCCx - Retrieve useful information about the Alien installation of tcc with extended symbol tables
      • Alien::Wslay - Discover or download and install Wslay
      • Notes for Alien::Wslay!
        • Wslay is a WebSocket library written in C. It implements the protocol version 13 described in RFC 6455
        • See also Net::WebSocket::EVx below
      • Alien::Xmake - Locate, Download, or Build and Install Xmake
      • Alt - Alternate Module Implementations
      • App::codefork - Worlds dumbest code forker
      • App::cpanminus - Get, unpack, build and install modules from CPAN
      • App::cpanminus::reporter - Send cpanm output to CPAN Testers
      • App::cpanoutdated - Detect outdated CPAN modules in your environmen
      • Notes for App::cpanoutdated!
        • Installs cpan-outdated
      • App::depak - Pack dependencies onto your script file
      • Notes for App::depak!
        • See also App::FatPacker::Simple
      • App::DualLivedList - Perl extension to determine if a module is Dual-Lived
      • App::FatPacker - Pack your dependencies onto your script file
      • App::FatPacker::Simple - Only fatpack a script
      • App::hopen - hopen build system command-line interface
      • App::LintPrereqs - Check extraneous/missing/incorrect prerequisites in dist.ini
      • App::ModuleBuildTiny - A standalone authoring tool for Module::Build::Tiny and Dist::Build
      • Notes for App::ModuleBuildTiny!
        • Can update version #s and push to CPAN
        • See also Module::Build::Tiny
        • Leon's articles
        • https://blogs.perl.org/users/leon_timmermans/2025/03/an-introduction-to-appmodulebuildtiny-part-1-setting-things-up.html
        • https://blogs.perl.org/users/leon_timmermans/2025/03/an-introduction-to-appmodulebuildtiny-part-2-authoring.html
      • App::Module::Lister - List the Perl modules in @INC
      • App::newver - Scan upstream for new software versions
      • App::perlimports - Make implicit imports explicit
      • App::PerlPackage2PlantUMLClassDiagram - Generate Plant UML's class diagraom from Perl packages
      • App::PP::Autolink - Run the pp (PAR Packager) utility while automatically finding dynamic libs to link
      • App::prepare4release - Prepare a Perl distribution for release (skeleton)
      • App::PrereqGrapher - Generate dependency graph using Perl::PrereqScanner
      • App::Project::Doctor - Unified pre-release health check for Perl CPAN distributions
      • App::ThisDist - Print Perl {distribution,module,author,...} associated with current directory
      • Aspect - Aspect-Oriented Programming (AOP) for Perl
      • Assert::Conditional - Conditionally-compiled code assertions
      • authority - Loads a module only if it has a particular authority
      • autovivification - Lexically disable autovivification
      • B::CallChecker - Custom B-based op checking for subroutines
      • Notes for B::CallChecker!
        • See also Devel::CallParser
      • B::LintSubs - Perl compiler backend to check sub linkage
      • Badger - Perl Application Programming Toolkit
      • bareword::filehandles - Disables bareword filehandles
      • base - Establish an ISA relationship with base classes at compile time
      • Blessed::Merge - Merge Blessed Refs
      • BsDiPa - Create or apply binary difference patch
      • C::Blocks - Embedding a fast C compiler directly into your Perl parser
      • C::DynaLib - Dynamic Perl interface to C compiled code
      • C::TCC - An interface to the TCC(Tiny C Compiler)
      • C::TinyCompiler - Full C JIT compiling using the Tiny C Compiler
      • C::Tokenize - Reduce a C file to a series of tokens
      • CallBackery - Appliance Frontend Builder
      • Caller::Reverse - Reverse the caller stack
      • Class::Abstract - Enforce abstract (non-instantiable) base classes for plain-Perl OO
      • Class::Accessor - Automated accessor generation
      • Class::Accessor::Fast - Faster, but less expandable, accessors
      • Class::Accessor::Lite - A minimalistic variant of Class::Accessor
      • Class::Accessor::Validated - Drop-in constructor validation for Class::Accessor::Fast-based classes
      • Class::Autouse - Run-time load a class the first time you call a method in it.
      • Class::EHierarchy - This is intended for use as a base class for objects that need support for class or object hierarchies
      • Notes for Class::EHierarchy!
        • See also https://metacpan.org/pod/Parse::PlainConfig
      • Class::Inspector - Get information about a class and its structure
      • Class::Mite - A minimal, integrated Class and Role system for Perl
      • Class::MOP::Class - Class Meta Object
      • Notes for Class::MOP::Class!
        • See also Class::MOP::Module, Class::MOP::Package
      • Class::MOP::Module - Module Meta Object
      • Notes for Class::MOP::Module!
        • See also Class::MOP::Class, Class::MOP::Package
      • Class::MOP::Package - Package Meta Object
      • Notes for Class::MOP::Package!
        • See also Class::MOP::Class, Class::MOP::Module
      • Class::SingletonMethod - Extend individual objects with additional methods
      • Class::Tiny - Minimalist class construction
      • Class::Unload - Unload a class
      • Class::XSAccessor - Generate fast XS accessors without runtime compilation
      • Class::XSAccessor::Array - Generate fast XS accessors without runtime compilation
      • Class::XSConstructor - A super-fast (but limited) constructor in XS
      • common::sense - Save a tree AND a kitten, use common::sense!
      • Compiler::Lexer - Lexical Analyzer for Perl5
      • Cons - A Software Construction System
      • Contextual::Return - Create context-sensitive return values
      • cpan2rpm - A Perl module packager
      • CPAN::API::BuildPL - Documentation of the API for using Build.PL
      • CPAN::Meta - The distribution metadata for a CPAN dist
      • CPAN::Meta::Check - Verify requirements in a CPAN::Meta object
      • CPAN::Meta::Requirements - A set of version requirements for a CPAN dist
      • CPAN::Meta::YAML - Read and write a subset of YAML for CPAN Meta files
      • CPAN::Requirements::Dynamic - Dynamic prerequisites in meta files
      • CPAN::Static::Install - Static CPAN installation reference implementation
      • CPAN::Static::Spec - Static install specification for CPAN distributions
      • DBIx::Class::DeploymentHandler - Extensible DBIx::Class deployment
      • DBIx::Class::DynamicSubclass - Convenient way to use dynamic subclassing
      • DBIx::VersionedSubs - All your code are belong into the DB
      • Destructure::Declare - Lexically scoped structural destructuring let
      • Dev::Util - Base modules for Perl Development
      • Devel::AssertOS - Require that we are running on a particular OS
      • Devel::CheckLib - Check that a library is available
      • Devel::CheckOS - Check what OS we're running on
      • Devel::FindPerl - Find the path to your perl
      • Devel::Hide - Forces the unavailability of specified Perl modules (for testing)
      • Notes for Devel::Hide!
        • See also Test::Without::Module
      • Devel::Hook - Mess around with BEGIN/CHECK/INIT/END blocks
      • Devel::MaintBlead - Handle maint / blead code paths for distributions
      • Devel::OverrideGlobalRequire - Override CORE::GLOBAL::require safely
      • Devel::PPPort - Perl/Pollution/Portability
      • Devel::Required - Automatic update of required modules documentation
      • Devel::ThreadsForks - Check for availability of threads or forks
      • Developer::Dashboard - A local home for development work
      • Dios - Declarative Inside-Out Syntax
      • Dist::Build - A modern module builder, author tools not included!
      • Dist::Build::DynamicPrereqs - Support dynamic prerequisites in Dist::Build
      • Dist::Mgr - Automation for Perl distribution creation, integration, pre-release, release and post-release tasks
      • DynaLoader - Dynamically load C libraries into Perl code
      • Notes for DynaLoader!
        • See also XSLoader
      • English - Use nice English (or awk) names for ugly punctuation variables
      • English::Script - Parse English subset and convert to data or code
      • Eval::Closure - Safely and cleanly create closures via string eval
      • Export::These - Terse Module Configuration and Symbol (Re)Exporting
      • Export::XS - Replacement for Exporter.pm + const.pm in XS, with C++ API
      • Exporter - Implements default import method for modules
      • Exporter::Almighty - Combining Exporter::Tiny with some other stuff for added power
      • Exporter::Lexical - Exporter for lexical subs
      • Exporter::Tiny - An exporter with the features of Sub::Exporter but only core dependencies
      • Notes for Exporter::Tiny!
        • Includes Exporter::Shiny
      • ExtUtils::Builder - An overview of the foundations of the ExtUtils::Builder Plan framework
      • ExtUtils::Builder::Compiler - An interface around different compilers
      • ExtUtils::CBuilder - Compile and link C code for Perl modules
      • ExtUtils::CChecker - Configure-time utilities for using C headers, libraries, or OS features
      • ExtUtils::CFeatureTest - Test a host for available C language features and libraries
      • ExtUtils::Config - A wrapper for perl's configuration
      • ExtUtils::CppGuess - Guess C++ compiler and flags
      • ExtUtils::Depends - Easily build XS extensions that depend on XS extensions
      • ExtUtils::HasCompiler - Check for the presence of a compiler
      • ExtUtils::Helpers - Various portability utilities for module builders
      • ExtUtils::InstallPaths - Build.PL install path logic made easy
      • ExtUtils::MakeMaker - Create a module Makefile
      • ExtUtils::MakeMaker::FAQ - Beware directives INSTALL_BASE and PREFIX
      • ExtUtils::ParseXS - Converts Perl XS code into C code
      • ExtUtils::PkgConfig - Simplistic interface to pkg-config
      • Notes for ExtUtils::PkgConfig!
        • But see Alien::libtickit
      • ExtUtils::Typemaps::Misc - A collection of miscelaneous typemap templates
      • ExtUtils::XSOne - Combine multiple XS files into a single shared library
      • ExtUtils::XSpp - XS for C++
      • Factory::Sub - Generate a factory of subs
      • Faster - Do some things faster
      • Feature::Compat::Class - Make class syntax available
      • Notes for Feature::Compat::Class!
        • Probably prefer this to Object::Pad, but is not as featureful as the latter
      • Feature::Compat::Defer - Make defer syntax available
      • FFI::C::Stat - Object-oriented FFI interface to native stat and lstat
      • FFI::CheckLib - Check that a library is available for FFI
      • FFI::Platypus - Write Perl bindings to non-Perl libraries with FFI. No XS required
      • FFI::Platypus::Lang::V - Documentation and tools for using Platypus with the V programming language
      • FFI::Platypus::Lang::Zig - Documentation and tools for using Platypus with the Zig programming language
      • FFI::Platypus::Record - FFI support for structured records data
      • FFI::Raw - Perl bindings to the portable FFI library (libffi)
      • FFI::TinyCC - Tiny C Compiler for FFI
      • Function::Parameters - Define functions and methods with parameter lists ("subroutine signatures")
      • Glib::Object::Introspection - Dynamically create Perl language bindings
      • Hades - Less is more, more is less!
      • Hades::Realm::Exporter - Hades realm for Exporter
      • Hades::Realm::Import::Export - Hades realm for Import::Export
      • Hades::Realm::OO - Hades realm for object orientation
      • Hash::AsObject - Treat hashes as objects, with arbitrary accessors/mutators
      • Notes for Hash::AsObject!
        • But see Object::Adhoc
      • Hash::Objectify - Create objects from hashes on the fly
      • Notes for Hash::Objectify!
        • But see Object::Adhoc
      • Import::Box - Box up imports into an object or unified function.
      • Import::Into - Import packages into other packages
      • indirect - Lexically warn about using the indirect method call syntax
      • Inline - Write Perl Subroutines in Other Programming Languages
      • Notes for Inline!
        • https://metacpan.org/pod/Inline-Support
      • Inline::C - C Language Support for Inline
      • Inline::CPP - Write Perl subroutines and classes in C++
      • Inline::Java - Write Perl classes in Java.
      • Inline::Lua - Embed Lua and Fennel code in your Perl scripts
      • Inline::Module - Support for Inline-based CPAN Extension Modules
      • InlineX::CPP2XS - Convert from Inline C++ code to XS
      • JavonetPerlSdk - Javonet allows you to reference and use modules or packages written in many languages
      • JCM::Boilerplate - Default Boilerplate for Joelle Maslak's Code
      • LaunchDarkly::Server - Perl server side SDK for LaunchDarkly, a fancy release manager
      • Notes for LaunchDarkly::Server!
        • See https://launchdarkly.com/docs/sdk/server-side/c-c--
      • Lexical::Accessor - True private attributes for Moose/Moo/Mouse
      • Lexical::Import - Clean imports from package-exporting modules
      • lib::archive - Load pure-Perl modules directly from TAR archives
      • Long::Jump - Mechanism for returning to a specific point from a deeply nested stack
      • Medusa - Subroutine auditing via attributes
      • Medusa::XS - High-performance XS audit logging with the :Audit attribute
      • Meow - Object à¸^â¢ï»â¢^à¸ Orientation
      • meta - Meta-programming API
      • Notes for meta!
        • This package provides an API for metaprogramming; that is, allowing code to inspect or manipulate parts of its own program structure
      • Minilla - CPAN module authoring tool
      • Notes for Minilla!
        • Contains Minilla::Tutorial
      • Mite - Moose-like OO, fast to load, with zero dependencies
      • Notes for Mite!
        • Demo: https://metacpan.org/dist/Acme-Mitey-Cards
      • Module::Build - Build and install Perl modules
      • Module::Build::CleanInstall - Subclass of Module::Build which removes the old module before installing the new one
      • Module::Build::Convert - Makefile.PL to Build.PL converter
      • Module::Build::Tiny - A tiny replacement for Module::Build
      • Notes for Module::Build::Tiny!
        • But see App::ModuleBuildTiny
      • Module::CheckVersion - Check module (e.g. check latest version) with CPAN (or equivalent repo)
      • Module::CheckLatestVersion - Warn/die when a module is not the latest version
      • Module::ExtractUse - Find out what modules are used
      • Notes for Module::ExtractUse!
        • This is not Module::Extract::Use, below
      • Module::Extract::Use - Discover the modules a module explicitly uses
      • Notes for Module::Extract::Use!
        • This is not Module::ExtractUse, above
      • Module::Extract::VERSION - Extract a module version safely
      • Module::Load - Runtime require of both modules and files
      • Module::Features - Define features for modules
      • Module::Features::PluginSystem - Features of modules that generate text tables
      • Module::Find - Find and use installed modules in a (sub)category
      • Module::Generate - Assist with module generation
      • Module::Generic - Generic Module to inherit from
      • Module::Install - Standalone, extensible Perl module installer
      • Module::Install::AssertOS - A Module::Install extension to require that we are running on a particular OS
      • Module::List - Module 'directory' listing
      • Notes for Module::List!
        • The POD helpfully contains a long list of similar modules
      • Module::Manifest::Skip - MANIFEST.SKIP Manangement for Modules
      • Module::Patch - Patch package with a set of patches
      • Module::Release - Automate software releases
      • Module::Runtime - Runtime module handling
      • Module::Signature - Module signature file manipulation - Deprecated
      • Notes for Module::Signature!
        • See also Test::Signature in TestingHelp
      • Module::Starter - A simple starter kit for any module
      • Notes for Module::Starter!
        • See also Module::Starter::Plugin
      • Module::Starter::AddModule - Add a new module to a distribution
      • Module::Starter::Plugin - How Module::Starter plugins work
      • Module::Starter::Simple - A simple, comprehensive Module::Starter plugin
      • Notes for Module::Starter::Simple!
        • Part of Module::Starter
      • Module::Starter::Smart - A Module::Starter plugin for adding new modules into an existing distribution
      • Notes for Module::Starter::Smart!
        • See Module::Starter::AddModule
      • Module::Starter::PBP - Create a module as recommended in "Perl Best Practices"
      • Moo - Minimalist Object Orientation (with Moose compatibility)
      • Notes for Moo!
        • MooX::Press, which contains Zydeco::Lite
        • MooX::Pression
        • MooX::Singleton
      • Mooish::Base - Importer for Mooish classes
      • Mooish::AttributeBuilder - Build Mooish attribute definitions with less boilerplate
      • Moo::Role - Minimal Object Orientation support for Roles
      • Moonshine::Magic - d[ o_0 ]b - has, extends, lazy_components
      • MooseX::Declare - Declarative syntax for Moose - Deprecated
      • MooseX::XSConstructor - Glue between Moose and Class::XSConstructo
      • MooX::Keyword::Field - Field attributes that cannot be set via the constructor
      • MooX::LazierAttributes - Lazier Attributes
      • MooX::Private::Attribute - Private attributes
      • MooX::StrictConstructor - Make your Moo-based object constructors blow up on unknown attributes
      • MooX::Traits - Automatically apply roles at object creation time
      • MooX::TypeTiny - Optimized type checks for Moo + Type::Tiny
      • MooX::XSConstructor - Glue between Moo and Class::XSConstructor
      • MOP4Import::Declare - Boilerplate base class for Runnable Module
      • Mouse - Moose minus the antlers
      • MouseX::OO_Modulino - Turn your Mouse class into JSON-aware Object-Oriented Modulino
      • MouseX::SingletonMethod - Mouse with Singleton Method facility
      • MouseX::Traits - Automatically apply roles at object creation time
      • multidimensional - Disables multidimensional array emulation
      • Multiple, separate Perls - perlbrew --install-multi
      • namespace::allclean - Avoid imports all subroutines into your namespace
      • namespace::clean - Keep imports and functions out of your namespace
      • Nefarious - Wicked or criminal objects
      • Net::WebSocket::EVx - Perl wrapper around Wslay websocket library
      • Notes for Net::WebSocket::EVx!
        • See also Alien::Wslay above
      • noop - A no-operation function, optimized to a single custom op
      • No::Worries - Coding without worries
      • Object::Adhoc - Make objects without the hassle of defining a class first
      • Notes for Object::Adhoc!
        • POD contains an important See Also
      • Object::Anon - Create objects on the fly
      • Object::Configure - Runtime Configuration for an Object
      • Notes for Object::Configure!
        • Was called Class::Debug
      • Object::HashBase - Build hash based classes
      • Object::ID - A unique identifier for any object
      • Object::InsideOut - Comprehensive inside-out object support module
      • Object::InsideOut::Metadata - Introspection for Object::InsideOut classes
      • Object::Pad - This module is essentially the basis of Perl's new keyword 'class'!
      • Notes for Object::Pad!
        • A simple syntax for lexical field-based objects
        • https://metacpan.org/dist/Object-Pad/view/lib/Object/Pad/Guide/MigratingFromClassicalPerl.pod
      • Object::Proto - Objects with prototype chains
      • Object::Proto::Sugar - Moo-se-like syntax for Object::Proto
      • ODS - Object Data Store
      • Object::Extend - Add and override per-object methods
      • Object::Realize::Later - Delayed creation of objects
      • Object::Result - Allow subs to build and return objects on-the-fly
      • Notes for Object::Result!
        • But see Object::Adhoc
      • Object::Simple - Simplest class builder, Mojo::Base porting, fast and less memory
      • Object::Tap - Tap into a series of method calls to alter an object
      • Object::Util - A selection of utility methods that can be called on blessed objects
      • Package::Stash - Routines for manipulating stashes
      • Package::Subroutine - Minimalistic import/export and other util methods
      • Package::Variant - Parameterizable packages
      • PAR::Packer - PAR Packager
      • parent - Establish an ISA relationship with base classes at compile time
      • parent::versioned - Establish an ISA relationship with base classes at compile time, with version checking
      • Parse::H - A parser for C header files that calls the given subroutines when a symbol of a specified type is encountered
      • Perl::APIReference - Programmatically query the perlapi
      • Perl::OSType - Map Perl operating system names to generic types
      • Perl::PrereqScanner - A tool to scan your Perl code for its prerequisites
      • Notes for Perl::PrereqScanner!
        • Command-line tool: scan-perl-prereqs
        • See also App::PrereqGrapher
      • Perl::PrereqScanner::Lite - Lightweight Prereqs Scanner for Perl
      • Perl::PrereqScanner::NotQuiteLite - A tool to scan your Perl code for its prerequisites
      • Perl::Squish - Reduce Perl code to a few characters as possible
      • Perl::Strip - Reduce file size by stripping whitespace, comments, pod etc
      • Perlbrew - See http://savage.net.au/misc/Debian.Wiki.html for curl
      • PerlBuildSystem - Make replacement with rules written in perl
      • PerlX::Maybe - Return a pair only if they are both defined
      • Notes for PerlX::Maybe!
        • Ship with Syntax::Feature::Maybe
      • PerlX::Perform - Syntactic sugar for if (defined ...) { ... }
      • Perl7::Handy - Handy Perl7 scripting environment on Perl5
      • PPI::Dumper - Dumping of PDOM trees
      • Preproc::Tiny - Minimal stand-alone preprocessor for code generation using perl
      • Probe::Perl - Information about the currently running perl
      • Quiq - Class library for rapid development
      • Notes for Quiq!
        • POD in German
      • Role::Basic - Just roles. Nothing else
      • Role::Commons - Roles that can be commonly used, for the mutual benefit of all
      • Notes for Role::Commons!
        • Uses Moo::Role. See Role::TinyCommons below
      • Role::Commons::Authority - A class method indicating who published the package
      • Role::Commons::Tap - An object method which helps with chaining, inspired by Ruby
      • Notes for Role::Commons::Tap!
        • Part of Role::Commons
        • DO NOT USE THIS MODULE! Use Object::Tap or Object::Util instead
      • Role::Tiny - Roles: a nouvelle cuisine portion size slice of Moose
      • Notes for Role::Tiny!
        • Included in Role::Tiny::With
      • Role::TinyCommons - Roles that can be commonly used, for the mutual benefit of all
      • Notes for Role::TinyCommons!
        • Uses Role::Tiny. See Role::Commons
      • Role::TinyCommons::Iterator::Basic - A basic iterator
      • Role::TinyCommons::Iterator::Resettable - A resettable iterator
      • Role::Tiny::With - Neat interface for consumers of Role::Tiny roles
      • Notes for Role::Tiny::With!
        • Included in Role::Tiny
      • Safe - Compile and execute code in restricted compartments
      • Safe::Hole - Make a hole to the original main compartment in the Safe compartment
      • Safe::Isa - Call isa, can, does and DOES safely on things that may not be objects
      • Scalar::Andand - Guarded method invocation
      • Simple::Accessor - Very simple, light and powerful accessor
      • Simple::Tuple - Because tuples should be simple and simple to use!
      • Singleton::Method - Utility to define singleton methods
      • SourceCode::LineCounter::Perl - Count lines in Perl source code
      • Stefo - Compile Perl subs to optimized bytecode via optree walking
      • Sub::Abstract - Abstract (virtual) methods for plain-Perl OO
      • Sub::Delete - Perl module enabling one to delete subroutines
      • Sub::Identify - Retrieve names of code references
      • Sub::Mutate - Examination and modification of subroutines
      • Sub::Name - (Re)name a sub
      • Sub::Private - Private subroutines and methods
      • Sub::Protected - Enforce protected subroutine access (Java/C++ semantics)
      • Sub::Quote - Efficient generation of subroutines via string eval
      • Sub::Signatures - Use proper signatures for subroutines, including dispatching
      • Sub::StrictDecl - Detect undeclared subroutines in compilation
      • Sub::Uplevel - Apparently run a function in a higher stack frame
      • Sub::Util - A selection of utility subroutines for subs and CODE references
      • Sub::Versions - Subroutine versioning syntactic sugar
      • Subclass::Of - Import a magic subclass
      • syntax - Activate syntax extensions
      • Syntax::Construct - Explicitly state which non-feature constructs are used in the code
      • Syntax::Keyword::Wielding - Adds a "wielding" keyword to make it easier to call the same function or method multiple times
      • UNIVERSAL::AUTHORITY::Lexical - An AUTHORITY method for every class, within a lexical scope
      • URI::PackageURL - Perl extension for Package URL (aka "purl")
      • Venus - Standard Library
      • Xerarch - Introspection of a package
      • Notes for Xerarch!
        • List all methods, scalars, arrays, hashes and globs
      • XS::Framework - Framework for building C++ XS modules with shared code, typemaps and C++ perl API
      • XS::Install - ExtUtils::MakeMaker based module installer for XS modules
      • XS::JIT - Lightweight JIT compiler for XS code
      • XS::Manifesto - Shared XS modules manifesto
      • XS::TCC - Embed, wrap & compile C code in Perl without going to disk
      • XSLoader - Dynamically load C libraries into Perl code
      • Notes for XSLoader!
        • See also DynaLoader
      • YAOO - Yet Another Object Orientation
      • Zydeco - Jazz up your Perl
      • Notes for Zydeco!
        • http://zydeco.toby.ink/
        • http://blogs.perl.org/users/toby_inkster/2020/02/mooxpression-now-much-faster.html
        • Was Moox::Pression

        • Note that "commandline" does not necessarily mean "plain-text running in a terminal"

      • Module::Pluggable - Automatically give your module the ability to have plugins
      • Module::Pluggable::Object - Automatically give your module the ability to have plugins
      • next::XS - Replacement for next::method, performing very fast, and super::method (next-by-mro), with Perl and C++ interface
      • Plugins - Generic plugins framework
      • Plugins::API - Inter-plugin callback API

    • MusicStuff
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Forte_number

        • https://lilypond.org/
      • Alien::RtMidi - Install RtMidi
      • App::DrivePlayer - GTK3 music player for Google Drive
      • App::Music::ChordPro - A lyrics and chords formatting program
      • App-MusicTools - Command line utilities for music composition and analysis
      • Business::ISMN - Work with International Standard Music Numbers
      • idi - Easy, command-line MIDI
      • LyricFinder - Fetch song lyrics from several internet lyric sites
      • MIDI - Read, compose, modify, and write MIDI files
      • MIDI::Drummer::Tiny - Glorified metronome
      • MIDI::Event - MIDI events
      • MIDI::RtController - Control your MIDI controller
      • MIDI::RtController::Filter::CC - Control-change based RtController filters
      • MIDI::RtController::Filter::Tonal - Tonal RtController filters
      • MIDI::RtMidi::FFI - Bindings for librtmidi - Realtime MIDI library
      • MIDI::RtMidi::FFI::Device - OO interface for MIDI::RtMidi::FFI
      • MIDI::RtMidi::ScorePlayer - Play a MIDI score in real-time
      • MIDI::Simple - Procedural/OOP interface for MIDI composition
      • MIDI::Stream - MIDI bytestream decoding and encoding
      • MIDI::Util - MIDI Utilities
      • Music::AtonalUtil - Atonal music analysis and composition
      • Music::Canon - Routines for musical canon construction
      • Music::Chord::Note - Get Chord Tone List from Chord Name
      • Music::Chord::Positions - Generate various chord inversions and voicings
      • Music::Duration - Add 32nd, 64th, 128th and tuplet durations to MIDI-Perl
      • Music::Duration::Partition - Partition a musical duration into rhythmic phrases
      • Music::Drummer - Use MIDI::Drummer::Tiny
      • Music::Harmonica::TabsCreator - Convert tunes into harmonica tabs
      • Music::Interval::Barycentric - Compute barycentric musical interval space
      • Music::Intervals - Breakdown of musical intervals
      • Music::LilyPondUtil - Utility methods for lilypond data
      • Music::MelodicDevice::Arpeggiation - Apply arpeggiation patterns to groups of notes
      • Music::Note - Representation of a single musical note, and various manipulation routines
      • Music::PitchNum - Note name and pitch number utility roles
      • Music::Scala - Scala scale support for Perl
      • Music::Scales - Supply necessary notes / offsets for musical scales
      • Music::ScaleNote - Manipulate the position of a note in a scale
      • Music::SimpleDrumMachine - Simple 16th-note-phrase Drummer
      • Music::Tempo - Various conversions to and from BPM
      • Music::Tension - Music tension analysis
      • Music::Tension::Cope - Tension analysis for equal temperament music
      • Music::ToRoman - Convert notes and chords to Roman numeral notation
      • Music::VoiceGen - Musical voice generation
      • Music::VoicePhrase - Construct a measured phrase of notes
      • Seed::Audio::AI::SiteKit - Unofficial metadata and URL helpers for Seed Audio AI
      • Types::MIDI - Type library for MIDI
      • WebService::MusicBrainz - API to search the musicbrainz.org database

      • Acme::UPnP - Cheap UPnP Port Mapping (IGD)
      • Alien::SNMP - Alien package for the Net-SNMP library
      • App::Netdisco - An open source web-based network management tool
      • App::NetdiscoX::Web::Plugin::Observium - Port Traffic Links and Thumbnail Graphs from Observium
      • App::ReslirpTunnel - A wrapper for the reslirp-tunnel application over SSH for remote connections
      • Check::NetworkSpans - See if bidirectional traffic is being seen on spans
      • Check::SuricataFlows - Make sure Suricata is seeing data via reading the Suricata flows json
      • Control::CLI - Command Line Interface I/O over either Telnet or SSH (IPv4 & IPv6) or Serial port
      • Control::CLI::Extreme - Interact with CLI of Extreme Networking products over any of Telnet, SSH or Serial port
      • Data::Throttler - Limit data throughput
      • Device::MAC - Handle hardware MAC Addresses (EUI-48 and EUI-64)
      • Device::OUI - Resolve an Organizationally Unique Identifier
      • Device::WWN - Encode/Decode Fiber Channel World Wide Names
      • FTN::Addr - Working with FTN addresses
      • Gearman::Client - Client for gearman distributed job system
      • Gearman::Objects - A parent class for Gearman::Client and Gearman::Worker
      • Gearman::Server - Function call "router" and load balancer
      • Gearman::Worker - Worker for gearman distributed job system
      • GraphQL - Perl implementation of GraphQL
      • GraphQL::Plugin::Convert - GraphQL plugin API abstract class
      • GraphQL::Plugin::Convert::DBIC - Convert DBIx::Class schema to GraphQL schema
      • GraphQL::Plugin::Convert::OpenAPI - Convert OpenAPI schema to GraphQL schema
      • grepcidr - Filter IPv4 and IPv6 addresses matching CIDR patterns
      • Notes for grepcidr!
        • See Acronyms
      • libnet - This is a collection of perl5 modules which all related to network programming
      • Notes for libnet!
        • The majority of the modules available provide the client side of popular server-client protocols
        • See also Net::LMTP2 below
      • Mnet - Testable network automation and reporting
      • NetAddr::IP - Manages IPv4 and IPv6 addresses and subnets
      • Net::BART - Balanced Routing Tables for IPv4 and IPv6
      • Net::CIDR::Lite - Perl extension for merging IPv4 or IPv6 CIDR addresses
      • Net::CIDR::Set - Manipulate sets of IP addresses
      • Net::DHCP - Object methods to create a DHCP packet
      • Net::DHCPv6 - DHCPv6 packet decoder/encoder
      • Net::EmptyPort - Find a free TCP/UDP port
      • Net::FTP - FTP Client class
      • Net::IP - Perl extension for manipulating IPv4/IPv6 addresses
      • Net::IP::XS - IPv4/IPv6 address library
      • Net::LMTP2 - A client class for the LMTP protocol which claims to fix Net::SMTP
      • Notes for Net::LMTP2!
        • See also libnet above
      • Net::MAC - Perl extension for representing and manipulating MAC addresses
      • Net::MacMap - A module used to determine the vendor of an interface based on the MAC addres
      • Notes for Net::MacMap!
        • But see the next module
      • Net::MAC::Vendor - Look up the vendor for a MAC
      • Net::Netmask - Parse, manipulate and lookup IP network blocks
      • Net::Nostr - Perl client and relay library for the Nostr protocol
      • Net::Nostr::Client - WebSocket client for Nostr relays
      • Net::Nostr::Core - Core Perl tooling for the Nostr protocol
      • Net::Nostr::Relay - Nostr WebSocket relay server
      • Net::Packet - A framework to easily send and receive frames from layer 2 to layer 7
      • Net::Patricia - Patricia Trie perl module for fast IP address lookups
      • Net::SIP - Framework SIP (Voice Over IP, RFC3261)
      • Notes for Net::SIP!
        • See TheRFCGuide for rfc321
      • Net::SNMP - Object oriented interface to SNMP
      • Net::UPnP - Perl extension for UPnP
      • Net::UPnP::AV::MediaServer - Perl extension for UPnP
      • Net::UPnP::ControlPoint - Perl extension for UPnP control point
      • Net::Versa::Director - Versa Director REST API client library
      • NetAddr::MAC - MAC hardware address functions and object (EUI48 and EUI64)
      • NetBox::API - Perl interface to NetBox API
      • Notes for NetBox::API!
        • https://netboxlabs.com/docs/netbox/
      • NetBox::Client - Perl5 interface to NetBox API
      • NetPacket::LACP - Assemble and disassemble IEEE 802.1D Spanning Tree protocol packets
      • NetPacket::LLC - Assemble and disassemble IEEE 802.3 LLC protocol packets
      • NetPacket::SpanningTree - Assemble and disassemble IEEE 802.1D Spanning Tree protocol packets
      • OSPF::LSDB - Class containing OSPF link state database
      • Ryu - Asynchronous Stream Building Blocks
      • Notes for Ryu!
        • Has good SEE ALSO
      • SNMP - The Perl5 'SNMP' Extension Module for the Net-SNMP SNMP package
      • SNMP::Info - OO Interface to Network devices and MIBs through SNMP
      • socat - Socat is a command line based utility that establishes two bidirectional byte streams and transfers data between them
      • Socket - Networking constants and support functions
    • NewOperators
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
      • Devel::Declare - Adding keywords to perl, in perl - Deprecated
      • Infix::Custom - Custom infix operators
      • Keyword::API - This module provides a pure perl interface for the keywords API added to the perl core in 5.12
      • Keyword::Declare - Declare new Perl keywords...via a keyword...named keyword
      • Keyword::Simple - Define new keywords in pure Perl
      • PerlX::SafeNav - Safe-navigation for Perl aka Optional Chaining
      • Notes for PerlX::SafeNav!
        • See also https://github.com/Perl/PPCs/blob/main/ppcs/ppc0021-optional-chaining-operator.md
      • List::Keywords - A selection of list utility keywords
      • Notes for List::Keywords!
        • first, all, any, none, notall, reduce, reductions, ngrep, nmap
      • Syntax::Feature::Loop - Provides the loop BLOCK syntax for unconditional loops
      • Syntax::Feature::Maybe - Use syntax qw/maybe/
      • Notes for Syntax::Feature::Maybe!
        • Included in PerlX::Maybe
      • Syntax::Feature::QwComments - Pragma to allow comments in qw()
      • Syntax::Feature::Void - Provides the void operator to force void context
      • Syntax::Feature::With - Simulate Pascal's "with" statement in Per
      • Syntax::Infix::ConditionalSplice - A short-circuiting < ?> operator for conditional list elements
      • Syntax::Infix::EqualityInsensitive - Case-insensitive eqi and nei infix operators
      • Syntax::Infix::OptionalChain - A safe-navigation < ?- >> operator for objects, hashes and arrays
      • Syntax::Keyword::Dynamically - Dynamically change the value of a variable
      • Syntax::Keyword::PhaserExpression - Phasers as arbitrary expressions rather than blocks
      • Syntax::Operator::Elem - Element-of-list operators
      • Notes for Syntax::Operator::Elem!
        • Samples
        • $s elem @strs # like any { $s eq $_ } @strs
        • $n â @nums # like any { $n == $_ } @nums
        • Yes, this is a UTF-8 named operator. No I will not apologise
      • Syntax::Operator::Equ - Equality operators that distinguish undef
      • Notes for Syntax::Operator::Equ!
        • $str equ $str # like eq but cares about undef
        • $num === $num # like == but cares about undef
      • Syntax::Operator::Elem - elem and similar operators, and the `in` meta-operator allow you to easily ask the question "does this string appear in that set of strings?" or similar for numbers. Useful combined with match/case
      • Notes for Syntax::Operator::Elem!
        • Samples
        • $x elem @strings # is a stringy comparison
        • $x â @numbers # is a numbery comparison
        • https://metacpan.org/pod/Syntax::Operator::In
        • $x in:isa @classes
      • Syntax::Operator::Is - Match operator using Data::Checks constraints
      • Syntax::Operator::Zip - Infix operator to compose two lists together
      • Notes for Syntax::Operator::Zip!
        • @list Z @list # like List::Util::zip but faster
        • @list M @list # like List::Util::mesh but faster
      • XS::Parse::Keyword - XS functions to assist in parsing keyword syntax
      • XS::Parse::Sublike - XS functions to assist in parsing sub-like syntax
    • OfficeFiles
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
      • App::OfficeUtils - Utilities related to Office suite files (.doc, .docx, .odt, .xls, .xlsx, .ods, etc)
      • AsposeCellsCloud::CellsApi - Allow developers to process and manipulate spreadsheet documents (like Microsoft Excel, OpenDocument Spreadsheet, CSV, etc.) without Microsoft Office
      • AsposeSlidesCloud::SlidesApi - No description
      • Document::OOXML - Manipulation of Open Office XML files
      • Notes for Document::OOXML!
        • Similar to MsOffice::Word::Surgeon
      • ODF::lpOD - An OpenDocument management interface
      • MsOffice::Word::Surgeon - Tamper with the guts of Microsoft docx documents, with regexes
      • Notes for MsOffice::Word::Surgeon!
        • For .doc: Text::Extract::Word
        • For .docx: https://blogs.perl.org/users/c_y_fung/2023/03/textextractword-msofficewordsurgeon---weekly-travelling-in-cpan.html
        • POD contains an important See Also
      • ODF::lpOD - An OpenDocument management interface
      • Notes for ODF::lpOD!
        • Possibly unmaintained. See comments at end of ODF::lpOD_Helper
      • ODF::lpOD_Helper - Fix and enhance ODF::lpOD
      • ODF::MailMerge - "Mail Merge" or just substitute tokens in ODF documents
      • MsOffice::Word::Template - Generate Microsoft Word documents from Word templates
    • OpenGL
      • See also!
        • Graphcomp is dedicated to the capture, processing, transport and rendering of online multimedia
      • OpenGL - v0.70
      • Notes for OpenGL!
        • Contains OpenGL::Array and OpenGL::Tessellation
      • OpenGL::GLFW - Perl bindings for the GLFW library
      • OpenGL::GLUT - Perl bindings to GLUT/FreeGLUT GUI toolkit
      • OpenGL::Modern - Perl extension to Modern OpenGL API up to 4.6
      • Notes for OpenGL::Modern!
        • Perl OpenGL (POGL) and Perl OpenGL::Modern (POGL2) development share common resources. At some point OpenGL::Modern will replace the legacy OpenGL implementation. Together they are referred to as POGL
    • OpenGraphProtocol
      • See also!
        • https://ogp.me/
        • https://json-ld.org/
        • https://schema.org/
        • For JSON_LD
        • For MooX::Role::SEOTags
        • The Open Graph protocol enables any web page to become a rich object in a social graph
      • Web::PageMeta - Get page open-graph / meta data
    • OpenTelemetry
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • Active
        • Archived
        • The Mojolicious Wiki
      • OpenTelemetry - A Perl implementation of the OpenTelemetry standard
      • Notes for OpenTelemetry!
        • See also https://opentelemetry.io/
      • OpenTelemetry::Exporter::OTLP - An OpenTelemetry Protocol exporter
      • OpenTelemetry::Instrumentation - Top-level interface for OpenTelemetry instrumentations
      • OpenTelemetry::Instrumentation::namespace - OpenTelemetry instrumentation for a namespace
      • OpenTelemetry::SDK - An implementation of the OpenTelemetry SDK for Perl
      • OpenTracing::Interface - API definition for OpenTransport implementations. But see OpenTelemetry
      • OpenTracing::Implementation::DataDog - Sending traces to DataDog using Agent. But see OpenTelemetry
    • OrgDocHandling
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • http://orgmode.org/
      • App::orgadb - An opinionated Org addressbook toolset
      • App::OrgUtils - Some utilities for Org documents
      • OpenAPI::Linter - Validate and lint OpenAPI specifications
      • OpenAPI::Modern - Validate HTTP requests and responses against an OpenAPI v3.1 document
      • Notes for OpenAPI::Modern!
        • Contains JSON::Schema::Modern::Document::OpenAPI and JSON::Schema::Modern::Vocabulary::OpenAPI
      • Org::Document - Represent an Org document
      • Org::Parser - Parse Org documents
      • Org::To::HTML - Export Org document to HTML
      • OrgINI - OrgINI file format specification
    • PacketCapture
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
      • File::PCAP - A pure Perl library to handle libpcap based files
      • Image::ExifTool::PCAP - Read CAP, PCAP and PCAPNG Packet Capture files
      • Metabrik::Forensic::Pcap - forensic::pcap Brik
      • Notes for Metabrik::Forensic::Pcap!
        • This is part of the vast Metabrik repo
      • Net::Inspect - Library for inspection of data on various network layers
      • Net::Pcap - Interface to the pcap(3) LBL packet capture library
      • Net::Pcap::Easy - Net::Pcap is awesome, but it's difficult to bootstrap
      • Sniffer::HTTP - Multi-connection sniffer driver
    • ParallelProcessing
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
      • Consumer::NonBlock - Send data between processes without blocking
      • Coro - The only real threads in perl
      • Coro::LWP - Make LWP non-blocking - as much as possible
      • Notes for Coro::LWP!
        • The pod discusses AnyEvent::HTTP, AnyEvent::HTTP::LWP::UserAgent, LWP::Protocol::AnyEvent::http, LWP::Protocol::Coro::http
      • Coro::Multicore - Make coro threads on multiple cores with specially supported modules
      • Notes for Coro::Multicore!
        • This module uses AnyEvent and Coro
      • Coro::Select - A (slow but coro-aware) replacement for CORE::select
      • Data::Fetch - Prime method calls for background execution using threads
      • Data::PubSub::Shared - High-performance shared-memory pub/sub for Linux
      • Data::ReqRep::Shared - High-performance shared-memory request/response IPC for Linux
      • IPC::Manager - Decentralized local IPC through various protocols
      • IPC::Manager::Client - Base class for all client protocols
      • IPC::Manager::Client::SharedMem - SysV shared memory as a message store
      • IPC::Manager::Client::UnixSocket - Use UNIX sockets for message transfers
      • IPC::Manager::DBI - Database based clients for IPC::Manager
      • IPC::ReadpipeX - Also, see Also within that module's POD
      • IPC::Run3 - Run a subprocess with input/output redirection
      • IPC::System::Options - Perl's system(), readpipe()/qx, IPC::Run's run(), start() (with more options)
      • IPC::System::Simple - Run commands simply, with detailed diagnostics
      • Linux::Epoll - O(1) multiplexing for Linux
      • MCE - Many-Core Engine for Perl providing parallel processing capabilities
      • Minion - Job queue
      • NBI::Slurm - NBI Slurm module
      • Net::Async::MCP - Async MCP (Model Context Protocol) client for IO::Async
      • Net::Waiter - Compact INET socket server
      • Parallel::DataPipe - Parallel data processing conveyor
      • Parallel::ForkManager - A simple parallel processing fork manager
      • Parallel::Iterator - Simple parallel execution
      • Parallel::Loops - Execute loops using parallel forked subprocesses
      • Parallel::Pipes - Parallel processing using pipe(2) for communication and synchronization
      • Notes for Parallel::Pipes!
        • Contains Parallel::Pipes::App
      • Parallel::Prefork - A simple prefork server framework
      • Parallel::Subs - Simple way to run subs in parallel and process their return value in Perl
      • Parallel::WorkUnit - Provide multi-paradigm forking with ability to pass back data
      • Notes for Parallel::WorkUnit!
        • There are many other Parallel::* modules on CPAN
      • POE::Loop::EV - A bridge that supports EV from POE
      • POSIX::AtFork - Hook registrations at fork(2)
      • prefork - Optimized module loading for forking or non-forking processes
      • Protocol::DBus - D-Bus in pure Perl
      • Notes for Protocol::DBus!
        • Includes Protocol::DBus::Client::Mojo, among others
        • See also https://dbus.freedesktop.org/doc/dbus-specification.html
      • Resque - Redis-backed library for creating background jobs, placing them on multiple queues, and processing them later
      • Sub::Genius - Declarative concurrency planning for sequential Perl execution
      • subs::parallel - Enables subroutines to seamlessly run in parallel
      • Thread::Bless - Make blessed objects thread-aware
      • Thread::Conveyor - Transport of any data-structure between threads
      • Thread::Conveyor::Monitored - Monitor a belt for specific content
      • Thread::CSP - Communicating sequential processes threading for Perl
      • Thread::Deadlock - Report deadlocks with stacktrace
      • Thread::Exit - Provide thread-local exit(), BEGIN {}, END {} and exited()
      • Thread::Needs - Remove unneeded modules from CLONEd memory
      • Thread::Pool - Group of threads for performing similar jobs
      • Thread::Pool::Resolve - Resolve logs asynchronously
      • Thread::Queue::Any - Thread-safe queues for any data-structure
      • Thread::Queue::Any::Monitored - Monitor a queue for any specific content
      • Thread::Rand - Repeatable random sequences between threads
      • Thread::Running - Provide non-blocking check whether threads are running
      • Thread::Serialize - Serialize data-structures between threads
      • Thread::Status - Report stack status of all running threads
      • Thread::Subs - Execute selected subs concurrently in worker threads
      • Notes for Thread::Subs!
        • The POD is very sophisticated
      • Thread::Synchronized - Synchronize subroutine calls between threads
      • Thread::Use - Use a module inside a thread only
      • threads::farm - Group of threads for performing similar jobs

      • See also!
        • [Topic]

        • https://universaldependencies.org/
      • Analizo - Multi-language source code analysis toolkit
      • Attean - A Semantic Web Framework
      • DMS::Parser - Pure-Perl parser for DMS, a data syntax with strong typing, ordered maps, multi-line heredocs, and front-matter metadata
      • DMS::Parser::XS - XS (C-backed) parser for DMS, a data syntax with strong typing, ordered maps, multi-line heredocs, and front-matter metadata
      • DMS::XS::Parser - Thin Perl shim over the C DMS parser
      • Grammar::Improver - A Perl module for improving grammar using LanguageTool API
      • Lingua::Word::Parser - Parse a word into scored known and unknown parts
      • Parse::FixedLength - Parse an ascii string containing fixed length fields into component parts
      • Parse::LocalDistribution - Parses local .pm files as PAUSE does
      • Parse::PMFile - Parses .pm file as PAUSE does
      • Parse::Taxonomy - Validate hierarchical data stored in CSV format
      • Parse::Tokens - Class for parsing text with embedded tokens
      • Parse::Yapp - Perl extension for generating and using LALR parsers
      • Parser::MGC - Build simple recursive-descent parsers
      • Pegex - Acmeist PEG Parser Framework
      • Perl500503Syntax::OrDie - Validate that source code is compatible with Perl 5.005_03
      • Query::Tags - Raku-inspired query language for attributes
      • Syntax::Highlight::Engine::Kate - A port to Perl of the syntax highlight engine of the Kate text editor
      • Syntax::Kamelon - A versatile and fully programmable textual content parser that is extremely well suited for syntax highlighting and code folding
      • Ufal::UDPipe - Bindings to UDPipe library http://ufal.mff.cuni.cz/udpipe
    • PdfStuff
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • PDF::FromHTML - Below. Contains html2pdf.pl. Does not handle CSS
        • [Topic]
        • WWW::Mechanize::Chrome::Webshot - Below. Converts HTML to PDF, with CSS. Uses Chrome
        • https://mupdf.com/
        • Includes qpdf and qpdfview (a browser for PDFs)
        • Offers a huge array of free online PDF tools
      • Adobe PDF reference - Versions of PDF are 1.0, 1.1, 1.2, 1.3, 1.4, 1.5, 1.6, 1.7 and now 2.0
      • Notes for Adobe PDF reference!
        • There was never a public release using version number 1.06 (from an email from Deven T. Corzine)
      • Alien::PDFio - Alien distribution for PDFio
      • Notes for Alien::PDFio!
        • See PDFio::FFI below
      • App::DiffPDFText - Diff the text of two PDF documents
      • App::Mxpress::PDF - A utility application for generating PDFs
      • App::paperback - Copy and transform pages from a PDF into a new PDF
      • App::PDFLibrarian - Manage a library of academic papers in PDF format with embedded BibTeX metadata
      • App::pdfresize - Resize each page of PDF file to a new dimension
      • App::PDFUtils - Command-line utilities related to PDF files
      • Notes for App::PDFUtils!
        • Includes 10 utilities
      • CAM::PDF - PDF manipulation library
      • CtrlO::PDF - High level PDF creator
      • Mojo::PDF - Generate PDFs with the goodness of Mojo!
      • Mxpress::PDF - A factory package and the entry point for Mxpress::PDF::* objects
      • PDF::API2 - Create, modify, and examine PDF files
      • Notes for PDF::API2!
        • See PDF::API2::Resource::XObject::Image::Imager for important comments about PDF::API2
      • PDF::API2::Resource::XObject::Image::Imager - Import Imager images into PDF
      • PDF::Builder - Facilitates the creation and modification of PDF files
      • Notes for PDF::Builder!
        • Use PDF::Builder via JSON config: https://codeberg.org/polettix/pdfunnel
        • See also https://www.catskilltech.com/FreeSW/product/PDF-Builder/title/PDF::Builder/freeSW_full
        • Example code: https://www.catskilltech.com/Examples/PDF/Builder.html
      • PDF::Collage - Layer stuff, e.g. SVG images, over PDFs
      • PDF::Cairo - Simple API for creating PDF files using the Cairo library
      • PDF::Create - Create PDF files
      • PDF::CreateSimple - Create simple PDF files using a set of easy-to-use functions
      • PDF::Data - Manipulate PDF files and objects as data structures
      • Notes for PDF::Data!
        • Includes pdf_data for dumping stuff
        • For a huge amount of info, see email from Deven T. Corzine, Friday 13:22 2024-09-27
      • PDF::FacturX - Generate Factur-X / ZUGFeRD-compatible PDF/A-3 invoices
      • PDF::FromHTML - Convert HTML documents to PDF
      • PDF::Haru - Perl interface to Haru Free PDF Library. Haru is a free, cross platform, open-sourced software library for generating PDF
      • Notes for PDF::Haru!
        • Supports Chinese, Japanese, Korean
      • PDF::Make - PDF generation, parsing, and editing
      • PDF::Report - A wrapper written for PDF::API2
      • PDF::Reuse - Reuse and mass produce PDF documents
      • Notes for PDF::Reuse!
        • This module has an important See Also
      • PDF::Reuse::Barcode - Create barcodes for PDF documents with PDF::Reuse
      • PDF::Reuse::OverlayChart - Produce simple or mixed charts with PDF::Reuse
      • PDF::Reuse::Tutorial - How to produce PDF-files with PDF::Reuse
      • PDF::Sign - Sign PDF files with CMS/CAdES signatures and RFC3161 timestamps
      • PDF::Table - A utility class for building table layouts in a PDF::Builder (or PDF::API2) object
      • Notes for PDF::Table!
        • See also https://www.catskilltech.com/FreeSW/product/PDF-Table/title/PDF::Table/freeSW_full
        • Example code: https://www.catskilltech.com/Examples/PDF/Table.html
      • PDF::WebKit - Use WebKit to Generate PDFs from HTML (via wkhtmltopdf)
      • PDFio::Architect - Creating and manipulating PDF files
      • PDFio::FFI - Perl FFI bindings for the PDFio C library
      • Notes for PDFio::FFI!
        • It allows you to read, write, and manipulate PDF files from Perl by exposing the PDFio API
      • Poppler - Bindings to the poppler PDF rendering library
      • Notes for Poppler!
        • http://poppler.freedesktop.org
        • Could not install this under Debian bookworm even after using
        • cpanm ExtUtils::PkgConfig and
        • sudo apt-get install libpoppler-glib-dev and
        • sudo apt-get install gobject-introspection and
        • PKG_CONFIG_PATH=/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu and
        • PKG_CONFIG_PATH=/usr/share and
        • cpanm Glib::Object::Introspection The latter fails with
        • Package gobject-introspection-1.0 was not found in the pkg-config search path
        • Sigh
        • But see PkgConfig - Pure-Perl Core-Only replacement for pkg-config
      • qpdf - QPDF is a command-line tool and C++ library that performs content-preserving transformations on PDF files
      • Notes for qpdf!
        • Here is the Git repository for qpdf: https://github.com/qpdf/qpdf
        • Documentation for qpdf is here: https://qpdf.readthedocs.io/en/stable/
        • Debian-specific notes follow. I'm using bookworm
        • libqpdf29 is installed by default for some reason
        • qpdf and qpdfview and libqpdf-dev can be installed with apt-get
        • qpfdview is a very convenient multi-doc (tabbed) viewer
        • Sample code, given x.pdf is say in v 1.6 format s output by LibreOffice
        • qpdf --force-version=1.4 x.pdf x-1.4.pdf
      • SVGPDF - Create PDF XObject from SVG data
      • Notes for SVGPDF!
        • This module processes SVG data and produces one or more PDF XObjects to be placed in a PDF document. This module is intended to be used with PDF::Builder, PDF::API2 and compatible PDF packages
      • WWW::Mechanize::Chrome::Webshot - Cheap and cheerful html2pdf converter, take a screenshot of rendered HTML, complete with CSS and Javascript
    • PdlLanguage
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]

      • CXC::PDL::Bin1D - One Dimensional Binning Utilities
      • PDL - The Perl Data Language
      • PDL::Drawing::Prima - PDL-aware drawing functions for Prima widgets
      • PDL::Graphics::PLplot - Object-oriented interface from perl/PDL to the PLPLOT plotting library
      • PDL::Fit - Various fitting implementations for PDL
      • PDL::Fit::Levmar - Levenberg-Marquardt fit/optimization routines
      • PDL::GSL - PDL interface to the GNU Scientific Library
      • PDL::LinearAlgebra - Linear Algebra utils for PDL
      • PDL::Minuit - A PDL interface to the Minuit library
      • PDL::NiceSlice - Toward a nicer slicing syntax for PDL
      • PDL::Opt::GLPK - PDL interface to the GNU Linear Programming Kit
      • PDL::Opt::Simplex::Simple - A simplex optimizer for the rest of us (who may not know PDL)
      • Notes for PDL::Opt::Simplex::Simple!
        • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Simplex_algorithm
      • PDF::Perldl2 - Simple shell (version 2) for PDL
      • PDL::Stats - A collection of statistics modules in Perl Data Language, with a quick-start guide for non-PDL people
      • PDL::Transform::Cartography - Useful cartographic projections
      • PDL::Transform::Proj4 - PDL::Transform interface to the Proj4 projection library

        • Requires: Perl 5.8.0

      • Perl6::Signature - Parse, query, and pretty-print Perl 6 signatures

    • PerlDeltas
      • See also!
        • https://phoenixtrap.com/2021/05/25/perl-can-do-that-now/
      • Syntax::Construct - This module documents which features (incl regexps) first appeared in which version of Perl
      • Notes for Syntax::Construct!
        • Explicitly state which non-feature constructs are used in the code
    • PerlDistros
      • See also!
        • This para does not try to list all distros, but only those which somehow stand out
      • App::perl::distrolint - Distribution-wide linting tools for Perl distributions
      • App::Staticperl - Perl, libc, 100 modules, all in one standalone 500kb file
      • PerlBench - Compare several perls' performances
      • Perl::Dist::APPerl - Actually Portable Perl
      • Notes for Perl::Dist::APPerl!
        • This distro runs on several x86_64 operating systems via the same binary
        • It builds to a single binary with perl modules packed inside of it
      • Portable - Perl on a Stick
      • Portable::Dist - Modify a Perl distribution to make it portable
    • PerlDocumentation
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • Module::CoreList - For modules which ship with Perl
        • [Topic]
        • List of resources about Perl
        • For the XS Language Reference Manual
        • For the Introduction to the Perl API
        • For Perl Mailing Lists
        • Interactive terminal-based viewer for App::sdview
        • Recent modules
        • Japanese
        • https://man.he.net/man3/- Searchable man pages
        • For Perl secret operators and constants
        • https://perldoc.perl.org/
        • https://perldoc.perl.org/perlpodspec
      • App::ManUtils - Utilities related to man(page)
      • App::sdview - A terminal document viewer for Pod and other syntaxes
      • Notes for App::sdview!
        • Contains sdview which supports POD and Markdown
      • Config::Perl::V - Structured data retrieval of perl -V output
      • Module::CoreList - What modules shipped with versions of perl
      • perlfaq - Frequently asked questions about Perl
      • perlxs - The XS Language Reference Manual
      • perlxstut - Tutorial for writing XSUBs

        • See also https://github.com/FractalBoy/perl-language-server

        • Contains podview

      • B - The Perl Compiler Backend
      • Notes for B!
        • Used by ogd, below
      • B::CC - Perl compiler's optimized C translation backend
      • B::Concise - Walk Perl syntax tree, printing concise info about ops
      • B::Deparse - Perl compiler backend to produce perl code
      • B::Hooks::AtRuntime - Lower blocks from compile time to runtime
      • B::Hooks::AtRuntime::OnlyCoreDependencies - It is just B::Hooks::AtRuntime but with only core dependencies
      • B::Hooks::EndOfScope - Execute code after a scope finished compilation
      • B::Hooks::EndOfScope::XS - Execute code after a scope finished compilation - XS implementation
      • B::Hooks::OP::Check - Wrap OP check callbacks
      • B::Hooks::Parser - Interface to perl's parser variables
      • B::Keywords - Lists of reserved barewords and symbol names
      • B::Lint - Perl lint - Deprecated
      • CXC::Exporter::Util - Tagged Based Exporting
      • Devel::MAT - Perl Memory Analysis Tool
      • Devel::PatchPerl - Patch perl source a la Devel::PPPort's buildperl.pl
      • Dist::Surveyor - Survey installed modules and determine the specific distribution versions they came from
      • ExtUtils::Typemaps::Magic - Typemap for storing objects in magic
      • Notes for ExtUtils::Typemaps::Magic!
        • See also Variable::Magic below
      • ExtUtils::Typemaps::Signal - A typemap bundle for dealing with signal related types
      • Filter::Simple - Simplified source filtering
      • Filter::Util::Call - Perl Source Filter Utility Module
      • Notes for Filter::Util::Call!
        • Contains 8 Filter::* modules
      • Hook::AfterRuntime - Run code at the end of the compiling scope's runtime
      • Internals - Write-protect variables, manipulate refcounts
      • ogd - Ordered global destruction
      • Opcode - Disable named opcodes when compiling perl code
      • Opcodes - More Opcodes information from opnames.h and opcode.h
      • PadWalker - Play with other peoples' lexical variables
      • Perl5::Build::Warnings - Parse make output for build-time warnings
      • Perl5::TestEachCommit - Test each commit in a pull request to Perl core
      • PPI - Parse, Analyze and Manipulate Perl (without perl)
      • Safe - Compile and execute code in restricted compartments
      • Scope::OnExit - Running code on scope exit - Deprecated
      • Sub::Exporter - A sophisticated exporter for custom-built routines
      • Sub::Exporter::Lexical - To export lexically-available subs with Sub::Exporter
      • Variable::Magic - Magic is Perl's way of enhancing variables
      • Notes for Variable::Magic!
        • This mechanism lets the user add extra data to any variable and hook syntactical operations (such as access, assignment or destruction) that can be applied to it
        • With this module, you can add your own magic to any variable without having to write a single line of XS
        • See also ExtUtils::Typemaps::Magic above

      • Number::Phone - Base class for Number::Phone::* modules
      • Notes for Number::Phone!
        • This module contains dozens of sub-modules

      • Plack::Middleware::Debug - Display information about the current request/response
      • Plack::Middleware::DebugLogging - Catalyst style console debugging for plack apps
      • Plack::Middleware::DirIndex - Middleware to use with Plack::App::Directory and the like
      • Plack::Middleware::DirIndex::Htaccess - Serve default html files for directory URLS. Includes checking of .htaccess file for DirectoryIndex
      • Plack::Middleware::DirListing - Display a listing of a directory in html
      • Plack::Middleware::Expires - mod_expires for plack (see Apache webserver)
      • Plack::Middleware::Headers - Modify HTTP response headers
      • Plack::Middleware::HTTPExceptions - Catch HTTP exceptions
      • Plack::Middleware::OpenTelemetry - Plack middleware to setup OpenTelemetry spans
      • Plack::Middleware::ProofOfWork - Proof-of-Work based bot protection for Plack applications
      • Plack::Middleware::ReverseProxy - Supports app to run as a reverse proxy backend
      • Plack::Middleware::Security::Simple - A simple security filter for Plack
      • Plack::Middleware::Session - Middleware for session management
      • Notes for Plack::Middleware::Session!
        • Contains Plack::Session
      • Plack::Middleware::Session::Cookie - Session middleware that saves session data in the cookie
      • Plack::Middleware::Session::SerializedCookie - Session middleware that saves session data in the customizable serialized
      • Plack::Middleware::Session::Simple - Make Session Simple
      • Plack::Middleware::Static - Serve static files with Plack
      • Plack::Middleware::Static::OpenFileCache - Plack::Middleware::Static with open file cache
      • Plack::Middleware::Statsd - Send statistics to statsd
      • Plack::Middleware::Text::Minify - Remove HTML indentation on the fly
      • Plack::Middleware::Zitadel - Verify Bearer tokens via ZITADEL OIDC in Plack apps
      • Plack::Request - Portable HTTP request object from PSGI env hash
      • Plack::Response - Portable HTTP Response object for PSGI response
      • PlackX::Framework - A thin framework for PSGI/Plack web apps
      • WebServer::DirIndex - Directory index data for web server listings
