      • App::podtohtml - Convert POD to HTML
      • Notes for App::podtohtml!
        • Provides podtohtml
      • App::sdview - A terminal-based structured document viewer for POD and other formats
      • Pod::Abstract - Abstract document tree for Perl POD documents
      • Pod::AsciiDoctor - Convert from POD to AsciiDoc
      • Pod::CopyrightYears - Object for copyright years changing in POD
      • Pod::Coverage - Checks if the documentation of a module is comprehensive
      • Pod::Coverage::TrustMe - Pod::Coverage but more powerful
      • Pod::Coverage::TrustPod - Allow a module's pod to contain Pod::Coverage hints
      • Pod::Example - Module for getting example from POD
      • Pod::Extract - Remove pod from file
      • POD::Generate - Programmatically generate plain old documentation
      • Pod::Github - Convert POD to Github markdown
      • Pod::Html - Module to convert pod files to HTML
      • Notes for Pod::Html!
        • See also Pod::L10N below
      • Pod::L10N - Pod with L10N
      • Pod::LOL - Parse Pod into a list of lists (LOL)
      • Pod::Man - Convert POD data to formatted *roff input
      • Pod::Markdown - Convert POD to Markdown
      • Notes for Pod::Markdown!
        • Uses Pod::Simple
      • Pod::Markdown::Github - Convert POD to Github's specific markdown
      • Pod::Markdown::Githubert - Convert POD to Github-flavored Markdown
      • Pod::Parser - Base class for creating POD filters and translators
      • Notes for Pod::Parser!
        • But see Pod::Simple
      • Pod::Perldoc::ToToc - Translate Pod to a Table of Contents
      • Pod::Query - Query pod documents
      • Pod::Markdown::Githubert - Convert POD to Github-flavored Markdown
      • Pod::Readme::Brief - A short simple README with just the essentials
      • Pod::Simple - Framework for parsing Pod
      • Notes for Pod::Simple!
        • Recommended by Pod::Parser as being preferred to the latter
      • Pod::Simple::HTML - Convert Pod to HTML
      • Pod::Simple::Wiki - A class for creating Pod to Wiki filters
      • Pod::Spell - A formatter for spellchecking Pod
      • Pod::Spell::CommonMistakes - Catches common typos in POD
      • Pod::Spelling - Send POD to a spelling checker
      • Notes for Pod::Spelling!
        • See also Test::Pod::Spelling
      • Pod::Text - Convert POD data to formatted text
      • Pod::Text::Color::Delight - Delight Light Highlight the POD
      • Notes for Pod::Text::Color::Delight!
        • Uses Pod::Text::Color
      • Pod::Text::Overstrike - Convert POD data to formatted overstrike text
      • Pod::Usage - Extracts POD documentation and shows usage information
      • Pod::Weaver - Weave together a Pod document from an outline
      • Pod::Weaver::Section::SourceGitHub - Add SOURCE pod section for a github repository
      • Pod::Webserver - Minimal web server for local Perl documentation
      • Notes for Pod::Webserver!
        • Contains podwebserver which listens on port 8020
      • Pod::Wordlist - English words that come up in Perl documentation
      • Text::AsciidocDown - Lightweight AsciiDoc to Markdown conversion

      • HiPi - Modules for Raspberry Pi GPIO
      • RPi::ADC::ADS - Interface to ADS 1xxx series analog to digital converters (ADC) on Raspberry Pi
      • RPi::ADC::MCP3008 - Interface to the MCP3008 analog to digital converter (ADC) on Raspberry Pi
      • RPi::BMP180 - Interface to the BMP180 barometric pressure sensor
      • RPi::Const - Constant variables for embedded programming, including the RPi:: family of modules
      • RPi::DAC::MCP4922 - Interface to the MCP49x2 series digital to analog converters (DAC) over the SPI bus
      • RPi::DHT11 - Fetch the temperature/humidity from the DHT11 hygrometer sensor on Raspberry Pi
      • RPi::DigiPot::MCP4XXXX - Interface to the MCP4xxxx series digital potentiometers on the Raspbery Pi
      • RPi::GPIOExpander::MCP23017 - Interface to the MCP23017 GPIO Expander Integrated Circuit over I2C
      • RPi::HCSR04 - Interface to the HC-SR04 ultrasonic distance measurement sensor on the Raspberry Pi
      • RPi::I2C - Interface to the I2C bus
      • RPi::LCD - Perl interface to Raspberry Pi LCD displays via the GPIO pins
      • RPi::OLED::SSD1306::128_64 - Interface to the SSD1306-esque 128x64 OLED displays
      • RPi::Pin - Access and manipulate Raspberry Pi GPIO pin
      • RPi::RTC::DS3231 - Interface to the DS3231 Real-Time Clock IC over I2C
      • RPi::Serial - Basic read/write interface to a serial port
      • RPi::SPI - Communicate with devices over the Serial Peripheral Interface (SPI) bus on Raspberry Pi
      • RPi::StepperMotor - Control a typical stepper motor with the Raspberry Pi
      • RPi::SysInfo - Retrieve hardware system information from a Raspberry Pi
      • RPi::WiringPi - Perl interface to Raspberry Pi's board, GPIO, LCDs and other various items
      • WiringPi::API - API for wiringPi, providing access to the Raspberry Pi's board, GPIO and connected peripherals

      • See also!
        • For Best Practical
      • Module::Install::RTx - RT extension installer
      • RT::Authen::OAuth2 - External authentication for OAuth 2 sources, like Google, X, Authentik, okta, GitHub, etc
      • RT::Client::REST - Client for RT using REST API
      • RT::Extension::ActivityReports - Additional reports to show Activity in RT

      • RT::Extension::Announce - Display announcements as a banner on RT pages
      • RT::Extension::Assets::Import::CSV - RT Assets Import from CSV
      • RT::Extension::AttachmentViewer - View full size attachments from the dropzone
      • RT::Extension::AutomaticAssignment - Automatically assign tickets based on rules
      • RT::Extension::AWS::Assets - Manage AWS resources in RT assets
      • RT::Extension::BooleanCustomField - CF with checkbox to set or unset its value
      • RT::Extension::Captcha - Use Google reCAPTCHA v3 to verify users before some actions in RT
      • RT::Extension::ChangeManagement - Change Management configuration for RT
      • RT::Extension::CommandByMail - Change ticket metadata via email
      • RT::Extension::CommentOnCreate - Adds an optional Comment box to Ticket Creation
      • RT::Extension::ConditionalCustomFields - CF conditioned by the value of another CF
      • RT::Extension::CSAT - Customer Satisfaction Feedback extension for Request Tracker
      • RT::Extension::DynamicWebPath - Dynamic WebPath
      • RT::Extension::EmailReplyDelimiter - Strip text from emails after a delimiter
      • RT::Extension::ExcelFeed - Generate RT reports in MS Excel XSLX format
      • RT::Extension::ExtractCustomFieldValues - Extract CF values from email headers or body
      • RT::Extension::FormTools - Create multi-page ticket creation wizards for RT
      • RT::Extension::GroupSummary - Display a summary of a group
      • RT::Extension::HelpDesk - Default Help desk configuration for Request Tracker
      • RT::Extension::Import::CSV - Import data from a comma-separated value (CSV) file, or any other sort of delimited file, into RT
      • RT::Extension::InlineAudioVideoAttachments - Display audio/video attachments inline
      • RT::Extension::InlineHelp - Provides the ability to add inline help to RT web pages
      • RT::Extension::JSGantt - Gantt charts for your tickets
      • RT::Extension::MandatoryOnTransition - Require core fields and ticket custom fields on status transitions
      • RT::Extension::MandatorySubject - Enforce users to fill the subject when creating a ticket
      • RT::Extension::Memo - Add a memo widget to tickets
      • RT::Extension::MergeUsers - Merges two users into the same effective user
      • RT::Extension::ModifyUsersPrefs - Allow modification of other users' preferences
      • RT::Extension::PreviewInSearch - Preview tickets right from search results page
      • RT::Extension::QuickAssign - Add owner change links on ticket display page
      • RT::Extension::QuickCalls - Quickly create tickets in specific queues with default values
      • RT::Extension::RepeatTicket - Repeat tickets based on schedule
      • RT::Extension::RepliesToResolved - Intercept replies to resolved tickets
      • RT::Extension::ResetPassword - Add "forgot your password?" link to RT instance
      • RT::Extension::ShowTransactionSquelching - Show which users were squelched per transaction
      • RT::Extension::SwitchUsers - Switch current logged in user to others
      • RT::Extension::Tags - Provides simple tagging using custom fields
      • RT::Extension::TicketLocking - Enables users to place advisory locks on tickets
      • RT::Extension::ToggleTheme - Toggle elevator light and dark theme
      • RT::Extension::TOTPMFA - Multi-factor authentication with time-based one-time passcodes
      • RTIR::Extension::MISP - Integrate RTIR with MISP
      • Notes for RTIR::Extension::MISP!

      • RTx::Calendar - Calendar view for RT ticket dates and custom fields

      • Dist::PolicyFiles - Generate CONTRIBUTING.md and SECURITY.md
      • Net::CVE - Fetch CVE (Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures) information from cve.org
      • Net::OSV - Search known vulnerabilities on the Open Source Vulnerabilities Database (OSV)
      • Software::Security::Policy - Packages that provide templated Security Policies

      • Perlsac::rwsac - A module to read and write SAC files
    • SeismicOS
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
      • App::SeismicUnixGui - A graphical user interface for Seismic Unix
    • SemanticWeb
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Named_graph
      • AtteanX::Store::DBI - Database quad-store
      • AtteanX::Store::LDF - Linked Data Fragment RDF store
      • AtteanX::Store::LMDB - LMDB-based RDF store
      • Notes for AtteanX::Store::LMDB!
        • This module operates at the Data Interchange (RDF) and Query (SPARQL) layers. It provides the physical location where the "Knowledge Graph" actually lives
        • Quadstores are typically queried using SPARQL
        • Common Quadstore Implementations are Apache Jena (TDB), Blazegraph, GraphDB, Virtuoso
      • AtteanX::Store::Memory - Simple in-memory RDF store
      • AtteanX::Store::SPARQL - Attean SPARQL store
    • SetStuff
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
      • Bit::Vector - Efficient bit vector, set of integers and "big int" math library
      • Radamsa - Perl 5 bindings for the Radamsa mutational fuzzer
      • Set::Array - Arrays as objects with lots of handy methods
      • Set::Bag - Bag (multiset) class
      • Set::CrossProduct - Work with the cross product of two or more sets
      • Set::Infinite - Sets of intervals
      • Set::Light - (Memory efficient) unordered set of strings
      • Set::Object - A set of objects and strings
      • Notes for Set::Object!
        • Slowish. See Set::Light
      • Set::Product - Generates the cartesian product of a set of lists
      • Set::Product::XS - Speed up Set::Product
      • Set::Relation - Relation data type for Perl
      • Set::Scalar - Basic set operations
      • Notes for Set::Scalar!
        • Slowish. See Set::Light
      • Set::Select - Select common subsets from two or more sets
      • Set::CrossProduct - Work with the cross product of two or more sets
      • Set::Tiny - Simple sets of strings

      • App::bsky - A Command-line Bluesky Client
      • At - The AT Protocol for Social Networking
      • Bluesky - Bluesky Client Library in Perl
      • Bluesky::Poster - Simple interface for posting to Bluesky (AT Protocol)
      • HTML::SocialMeta - Module to generate Social Media Meta Tags
      • Notes for HTML::SocialMeta!
        • It allows you to optimize sharing on platforms such as Twitter, Facebook, Google+ and Pinerest
    • SortModules
      • See also!
        • djbsort homepage
      • App::subsort - Sort lines of text using Sort::Sub routine
      • Notes for App::subsort!
        • Contains App::sortsub
        • Uses Sort::Sub below
      • File::Util::Sort - Routines related to sorting files in one or more directories
      • Sort::DJB - Fast sorting using Daniel J. Bernstein's djbsort bitonic sorting networks
      • Sort::Fields - Provides a general purpose technique for efficiently sorting lists of lines that contain data separated into fields
      • Sort::Maker - A simple way to make efficient sort subs
      • Sort::Naturally - Sort lexically, but sort numeral parts numerically
      • Sort::Sub - Collection of sort subroutines
      • Sort::Versions - Sorting of revision-like numbers
      • SortExample::Letter::ByFrequency::ID - Ordered list of letters by usage frequency in Indonesian words
    • SpreadsheetStuff
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
      • App::diff_spreadsheets - Show differences between spreadsheets/csvs readably
      • Data::XLSX::Parser - Faster XLSX parser
      • Notes for Data::XLSX::Parser!
        • See Excel::ValueReader::XLSX
      • Excel::PowerPivot::Utils - Utilities for scripting Power Pivot models within Excel workbooks
      • Excel::ValueReader::XLSX - Extracting values from Excel workbooks in XLSX format, fast
      • Excel::ValueWriter::XLSX - Generating data-only Excel workbooks in XLSX format, fast
      • Excel::Writer::XLSX - Create a new file in the Excel 2007+ XLSX format
      • Notes for Excel::Writer::XLSX!
        • This module has a huge number of methods
      • Excel::XLSX - Read and write Excel XLSX data
      • Number::Base::SpreadsheetColumn - Convert spreadsheet column name (e.g. "A", "Z", "AA") to number (e.g. 0, 25, 26) and vice versa
      • Spreadsheet::CSV - Drop-in replacement for Text::CSV_XS with spreadsheet support
      • Spreadsheet::Edit - Slice and dice spreadsheets, optionally using tied variables
      • Notes for Spreadsheet::Edit!
        • Contains Spreadsheet::Edit::IO. Convert between spreadsheet and csv files
      • Spreadsheet::Engine::Function::PROPER - Spreadsheet funtion PROPER()
      • Spreadsheet::Examples - A collection of various examples of spreadsheet files
      • Spreadsheet::HTML - Just another HTML table generator
      • Notes for Spreadsheet::HTML!
        • Used by DBIx::HTML
        • Transform Excel, HTML, JSON, CSV, YAML, PNG, JPEG and GIF files instantly into HTML tables
      • Spreadsheet::Open - Open spreadsheet in a spreadsheet program
      • Spreadsheet::ParseExcel - Read information from an Excel file
      • Spreadsheet::ParseXLSX - Parse XLSX files
      • Notes for Spreadsheet::ParseXLSX!
        • See Excel::ValueReader::XLSX
      • Spreadsheet::Read - Read the data from a spreadsheet. Contains xlsx2csv
      • Notes for Spreadsheet::Read!
        • Spreadsheet::Read offers a uniformed wrapper to Spreadsheet::ParseExcel and Spreadheet::ReadSXC
        • For OpenOffice and/or LibreOffice this module uses Spreadsheet::ParseODS or Spreadsheet::ReadSXC
        • For Microsoft Excel this module uses Spreadsheet::ParseExcel, Spreadsheet::ParseXLSX, Excel::ValueReader::XLSX, or Spreadsheet::XLSX (strongly deprecated)
        • For CSV this module uses Text::CSV_XS or Text::CSV_PP
        • For Gnumeric this module uses Spreadsheet::ReadGnumeric
        • For SquirrelCalc there is a very simplistic built-in parser
      • Spreadsheet::Write - Writer for spreadsheet files (CSV, XLS, XLSX)
      • Notes for Spreadsheet::Write!
        • But see the next module
      • Spreadsheet::WriteExcel - Write to a cross-platform Excel binary file
      • Spreadsheet::Wright - Simple spreadsheet worker
      • Notes for Spreadsheet::Wright!
        • Writes files in CSV, Microsoft Excel, HTML and OpenDocument formats
        • Formats - 'csv', 'xls', 'xlsx', 'html', 'xhtml', 'xml', 'ods', 'json', or 'auto' (default)

      • SPVM::Digest::MD5 - MD5

      • SPVM::Errno - Error Numbers
      • SPVM::File::Glob - The BSD glob Porting

      • SPVM::Getopt::Long - Parsing Command Line Options
      • SPVM::Go - Goroutines of The Go Programming Language
      • SPVM::HTTP::Tiny - HTTP Client
      • SPVM::IO - File IO, Sockets, Directory Manipulation, Select/Polling
      • SPVM::IO::Socket::SSL - Sockets for SSL Communication
      • SPVM::Math - Mathematical Calculations
      • SPVM::MIME::Base64 - Base64 Encoding/Decoding

      • SPVM::Mojolicious - Real-time web framework
      • SPVM::Net::SSLeay - OpenSSL Binding to SPVM
      • SPVM::R - Porting R language Features
      • SPVM::Re - More Perlish Regular Expressions
      • SPVM::Regex - Regular Expressions
      • SPVM::Resource::Eigen - Resource for C++ Eigen library
      • SPVM::Resource::Libpng - The Resource of The libpng Library
      • SPVM::Resource::RE2 - The Resource of Google/RE2
      • SPVM::Resource::SocketUtil - Resource for Socket Utilities
      • SPVM::Resource::SQLite - A resource that provides the SQLite header and source code
      • SPVM::Resource::Utf8proc - Bundle of the utf8proc library
      • SPVM::Resource::Zlib - Zlib Resources
      • SPVM::Sys - System Calls for File IO, Sockets, Time, Process, Signals, Users
      • SPVM::Thread - Native Thread
      • SPVM::Time::Local - Reverse Operations of localtime and gmtime
      • SPVM::Time::Piece - Dates and Times
      • SPVM::Unicode - Unicode Utilities
      • SPVM::Unicode::Normalize - Normalizing UTF-8

      • App::RemoteCommand - Simple remote command launcher via ssh

      • App::sshca - A minimal SSH Certificate Authority
      • Libssh::Session - Support for the SSH protocol via libssh.
      • Net::FullAuto - Fully Automate any workload with *persistent* SSH/SFTP from one host
      • Net::LibSSH - Perl binding for libssh â SSH without SFTP dependency
      • Net::OpenSSH - Perl SSH client package implemented on top of OpenSSH
      • Notes for Net::OpenSSH!
        • POD contains this comparison: Net::OpenSSH Vs. Net::SSH::.* modules
      • Net::SFTP::Foreign - SSH File Transfer Protocol client
      • Net::SSH2 - Support for the SSH 2 protocol via libssh2
      • Net::SSH::Any - SSH client module
      • Net::SSH::Perl - Perl client Interface to SSH
      • Net::SSH2::SFTP - SSH 2 Secure FTP object
      • ssh_config - OpenSSH client configuration file

      • App::Aphra - A simple static sitebuilder in Perl
      • App::sitelenmute - A static image gallery creator
    • StatisticsStuff
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
      • Acme::Selection::RarestFirst - Rarest-First Selection Algorithm
      • App::freqtable - Print frequency table of lines/words/characters/bytes/numbers
      • App::wordstat - Return word statistics of a text
      • Statistics::CaseResampling - Efficient resampling and calculation of medians with confidence intervals
      • Math::LiveStats - Pure perl module to make mean, standard deviation, and p-values available for given window sizes in streaming data
      • Stats::LikeR - Get basic statistical functions, like in R, but with Perl using XS for performance
      • Statistics::Descriptive - Module of basic descriptive statistical functions
      • Statistics::Descriptive::PDL - A close to drop-in replacement for Statistics::Descriptive using PDL as the back-end
      • Statistics::Distributions - Perl module for calculating critical values and upper probabilities of common statistical distributions
      • Statistics::Krippendorff - Calculate Krippendorff's alpha
      • Statistics::Running - Basic descriptive statistics (mean/stdev/min/max/skew/kurtosis) and discrete Probability Distribution (via histogram) over data without the need to store data points
      • Statistics::Running::Tiny - Basic descriptive statistics (mean/stdev/min/max/skew/kurtosis) over data without the need to store data points
      • Statistics::Welford - Standard statistics using Welford's algorithm
    • StradaStuff
      • See also!
        • https://strada-lang.github.io/
      • Strada - Call compiled Strada shared libraries from Perl
    • StreamingMedia
      • App::MHFS - A Media HTTP File Server. Stream your own music and video library via your browser and standard media players
    • StringStuff
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
      • App::CommonPrefixUtils - Utilities related to common prefix
      • App::CommonSuffixUtils - Utilities related to common suffix
      • Basic::Coercion::XS - Creates a coercion type that converts a string into an array reference
      • String::Binary::Interpolation - Make it easier to interpolate binary bytes into a string
      • String::Checker - An extensible string validation module (allowing commonly used checks on strings to be called more concisely and consistently)
      • String::CommonPrefix - Find prefix common to all strings
      • Notes for String::CommonPrefix!
        • Also contains String::CommonSuffix
      • String::CommonSuffix - Return suffix common to all strings
      • String::CRC32C - Castagnoli CRC
      • String::Elide::Lines - Elide lines from a string, with options
      • String::Elide::Parts - Elide a string with multiple parts of different priorities
      • String::Elide::Tiny - A very simple text truncating function, elide()
      • String::Expand - String utility functions for expanding variables in self-referential sets
      • String::Fuzzy - Python-style fuzzy string matching (fuzzywuzzy port)
      • String::Interpolate - Wrapper for builtin the Perl interpolation engine.
      • String::Interpolate::RE - Interpolate variables into strings using regular expressions
      • String::Interpolate::Shell - Variable interpolation, shell style
      • String::Lookup - Convert strings to ID's authoritatively and vice-versa
      • String::MatchInterpolate - Named regexp capture and interpolation from the same template
      • String::Print - printf alternative
      • String::Random::Regexp::regxstring - Generate random strings from a regular expression
      • String::Redactable - A string that automatically redacts itself
      • String::Scanf - Emulate sscanf() of the C library
      • String::Sprintf - Custom overloading of sprintf
      • String::Super - Compactor for superstrings
      • String::Tagged - String buffers with value tags on extents
      • String::Tagged::Formatting - An API specification for simple formatted strings
      • String::Tagged::HTML - Handle HTML content using String::Tagged
      • String::Trim::More - Various string trimming utilities
      • String::Truncate - A module for when strings are too long to be displayed in...
      • String::Util - String processing utility functions. About 22 of them
      • String::Util::Match - String utilities related to matching
      • Text::Elide - Perl module with simple "word" aware string truncating routine
      • Text::Fuzzy - Partial string matching using edit distances
      • Notes for Text::Fuzzy!
        • It handles both byte strings and character strings (strings containing Unicode), treating each Unicode character as a single entity
        • The POD contains a great list of string processing modules
      • Text::Levenshtein - Calculate the Levenshtein edit distance between two strings
      • Notes for Text::Levenshtein!
        • Contains a marvellous list of similar modules
      • Text::Levenshtein::Damerau - Damerau Levenshtein edit distance
      • Text::Levenshtein::Flexible - XS Levenshtein distance calculation with bounds and costs
      • Text::Levenshtein::XS - Calculate edit distance based on insertion, deletion, and substitution
      • Text::sprintfn - Drop-in replacement for sprintf(), with named parameter support
      • Text::Sprintf::Named - sprintf-like function with named conversions
      • Text::Truncate - Perl module with simple string truncating routine
      • Text::Wrap - Line wrapping to form simple paragraphs
    • SwitchStatements
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • A reference to smartmatch
        • [Topic]
      • Dispatch::Class - Dispatch on the type (class) of an argument
      • Exception::Class::TryCatch - Syntactic try/catch sugar for use with Exception::Class
      • Feature::Compat::Try - Make try/catch syntax available
      • Hustle::Table - Cached general purpose dispatch and routing table
      • match::simple - Simplified clone of smartmatch operator
      • Notes for match::simple!
        • Obsolete. See Syntax::Operator::Matches below
      • Multi::Dispatch - Multiple dispatch for Perl subs and methods
      • Nice::Try - A real Try Catch Block Implementation Using Perl Filter
      • Notes for Nice::Try!
        • Very comprehensive. Works well with Want. See WantArray
      • Smart::Dispatch - First-class switch statements
      • Struct::Match - Exact Match (SCALAR|HASH|ARRAY)'s
      • Sub::MultiMethod - Yet another implementation of multimethods
      • Switch::Again - Keyword for either generating a reusable switch statement or calling the generated switch function directly
      • Switch::Back - Given/when for a post-given/when Perl
      • Switch::Declare - Compile-time, lexically-scoped switch/case
      • Switch::Right - Switch and smartmatch done right this time
      • Syntax::Feature::Try - Try/catch/finally statement for exception handling
      • Syntax::Infix::Smartmatch - Requires Module::Build::Tiny which will not install under GCC V 8.3.0
      • Syntax::Keyword::Match - A match/case syntax for perl
      • Syntax::Keyword::Try - A try/catch/finally syntax for perl
      • Notes for Syntax::Keyword::Try!
        • Cannot do some things Nice::Try can do
      • Syntax::Operator::Matches - match::simple but as a real infix operator
      • Syntax::Keyword::MultiSub - Multiple dispatch on subroutines
      • Switch::Plain - A simple switch statement for Perl
      • TryCatch - First class try catch semantics for Perl, without source filters
      • Notes for TryCatch!
        • But see Try::Tiny
      • Try::ALRM - Try/catch-style semantics for handling timeouts using CORE::alarm
      • Try::Catch - Try Catch exception handler based on Try::Tiny but faster
      • Try::Tiny - Minimal try/catch with proper preservation of $@
      • Try::Tiny::Tiny - Slim Try::Tiny down as far as reasonably possible

      • See also!
        • [Topic]
      • Nix::Proc::Meminfo - Access /proc/meminfo with core classes

      • Sys::CpuLoad - Retrieve system load averages
      • Notes for Sys::CpuLoad!
        • Note: On Debian I can run w, which is /usr/bin/w
      • Sys::Export - Export a subset of an OS file tree, for chroot/initrd
      • Sys::Info::Base - Base class for Sys::Info
      • Sys::Info::Device::CPU - Collects and returns information about the Central Processing Unit (CPU) on the host machine
      • Sys::Info::Driver::Linux - Supplies detailed operating system information
      • Sys::Hostname - Try every conceivable way to get hostname
      • Sys::Hostname::Long - Try every conceivable way to get full hostname
      • Sys::Info - Extracts and collects information from the host system
      • Sys::Info::Device - This is an interface to the available devices such as the CPU
      • System::CPU - Cross-platform CPU information / topology with no dependencies
      • Notes for System::CPU!
        • Pure Perl
        • Has notes about old code such as System::Info below and
        • Unix::Processors, Sys::Info, various *::Sysinfo
      • System::Info - Factory for system specific information objects
      • Notes for System::Info!
        • The POD contains a good list of alternatives

        • For a modular WebSSO (Single Sign On)

      • Amazon::API - A generic base class for AWS Services
      • Amazon::CreatorsAPI - The Amazon Creators API Helper
      • Amazon::S3 - A portable client library for working with and managing Amazon S3 buckets and keys
      • Amazon::S3::Lite - A lightweight Amazon S3 client for common operations
      • Amazon::S3::Thin - A thin, lightweight, low-level Amazon S3 client
      • Amazon::Signature4::Lite - Lightweight AWS Signature Version 4 signing
      • Amazon::Sites - A class to represent Amazon sites
      • App::FargateStack - A lightweight deployment framework for Amazon ECS on Fargate
      • App::ipinfo - A command-line tool for IPinfo.io
      • Notes for App::ipinfo!
        • Interface to https://ipinfo.io/ - Accurate IP address data that keeps pace with secure, specific, and forward-looking use cases
      • App::rdapper - A simple console-based RDAP client
      • App::traveller - A webserver that serves Traveller RPG maps
      • AWS::Signature4 - Create a version4 signature for Amazon Web Services
      • HV::Monitor - A generalized module for gathering stats for a hypervisor
      • IPCamera::Reolink - Reolink API provides access to the System, Security, Network, Video input, Enc, Record, PTZ, and Alarm functions of a Reolink IP Camera or NVR via HTTP(S)/REST
      • Lemonldap::NG::Common - Common files for Lemonldap::NG infrastructure
      • Lemonldap::NG::Handler - The Apache protection module part of Lemonldap::NG Web-SSO system
      • Lemonldap::NG::Manager - Perl extension for managing Lemonldap::NG Web-SSO system
      • Notes for Lemonldap::NG::Manager!
        • Web Single Sign On and Access Management Software
      • Lemonldap::NG::Portal - The authentication portal part of Lemonldap::NG Web-SSO system
      • Lilith - Work with Suricata/Sagan EVE logs and PostgreSQL
      • Map::Metro - Public transport graphing
      • Map::Metro::Graph - An entire graph
      • Map::Metro::Plugin::Map::Berlin - Map::Metro map for Berlin
      • Map::Metro::Plugin::Map::Lyon - Map::Metro map for Lyon
      • Map::Tube - Lightweight Routing Framework
      • Map::Tube::API - Interface to Map::Tube REST API
      • Map::Tube::Beijing - Interface to the Beijing tube map
      • Map::Tube::Berlin - Interface to the Berlin S- and U-Bahn map
      • Map::Tube::Chicago - Interface to the Chicago L system map
      • Map::Tube::CLI - Command Line Interface for Map::Tube::* map
      • Map::Tube::Exception - Base exception package as Moo Role for Map::Tube::* family
      • Map::Tube::Generic - Interface to a map specified at runtime
      • Map::Tube::Glasgow - Interface to the Glasgow tube map
      • Map::Tube::GraphViz - GraphViz output for Map::Tube
      • Map::Tube::Hamburg - Interface to the Hamburg U- and S-Bahn maps including AKN
      • Map::Tube::KoelnBonn - Interface to the Koeln-Bonn (Cologne/Bonn) area U- and S-Bahn and tram maps
      • Map::Tube::London - Interface to the London Tube Map.
      • Map::Tube::Lyon - Interface to the Lyon metro, funicular and tramway map
      • Map::Tube::Muenchen - Interface to the Muenchen (Munich) U- and S-Bahn and tram maps
      • Map::Tube::Napoli - Interface to the Napoli tube and funicular map
      • Map::Tube::Oslo - Interface to the Oslo tube map
      • Map::Tube::Paris - Interface to the Paris metro, RER, Transiliens, and tram lines map
      • Map::Tube::Plugin::FuzzyFind - Map::Tube add-on for finding stations and lines by inexact name
      • Map::Tube::Plugin::Graph - Graph plugin for Map::Tube
      • Map::Tube::RheinRuhr - Interface to the German Rhein/Ruhr area U- and S-Bahn and tram maps
      • Map::Tube::Rome - Interface to the Rome tube map
      • Map::Tube::SanFrancisco - Interface to the San Francisco rapid transport, underground, trams and cable cars
      • Map::Tube::Server - Dancer2 based server for Map::Tube
      • Map::Tube::Stockholm - Interface to the Stockholm tube and tram map
      • Map::Tube::Stuttgart - Interface to the Stuttgart tube map
      • Map::Tube::Sydney - Interface to the Sydney Rail Network
      • Map::Tube::Toulouse - Interface to the Toulouse tube map
      • Monitoring::Livestatus - Perl API for check_mk livestatus to access runtime data from Nagios and Icinga
      • Monitoring::Sneck - A boopable LibreNMS JSON style SNMP extend for remotely running nagios style checks
      • Net::Amazon::S3 - Use the Amazon S3 - Simple Storage Service
      • Net::Cloudflare::DNS - DNS API for Cloudflare API v4
      • Net::Fortinet::FortiManager - Fortinet FortiManager REST API client library
      • Net::LDAP - Lightweight Directory Access Protocol
      • Net::Ping - Check a remote host for reachability
      • Net::Ping::External - Cross-platform Perl interface to "ping" utilities
      • Net::RDAP - Provides an interface to the Registration Data Access Protocol
      • Notes for Net::RDAP!
        • RDAP is replacing Whois as the preferred way of obtainining information about Internet resources (IP addresses, autonymous system numbers, and domain names)
        • See also App::rdapper
      • Net::RDAP::Server - An RDAP server framework
      • Net::Whois::IANA - A universal WHOIS data extractor
      • Net::Whois::IP - Perl extension for looking up the whois information for ip addresses
      • Net::Whois::Raw - Get Whois information of domains and IP addresses
      • OSLV::Monitor - OS level virtualization monitoring extend for LibreNMS
      • Paws - A Perl SDK for AWS (Amazon Web Services) APIs
      • Notes for Paws!
        • The POD has a huge list of services
      • Paws::S3::PutObject - Arguments for method PutObject on Paws::S3
      • PawsX::S3::Uploader - Upload to S3 from a streaming source
      • Suricata::Ping - Reads in a suricata config and sends a ping to the specified interface
      • Sys::Monitor::Lite - Lightweight system monitoring toolkit with JSON output
      • Sys::OsPackage - Install OS packages and determine if CPAN modules are packaged for the OS
      • Notes for Sys::OsPackage!
        • See also https://wiki.archlinux.org/title/Pacman/Rosetta
      • Sys::Syscall - Access system calls that Perl doesn't normally provide access to
      • Task::Map::Tube - Install the Map::Tube modules
      • Task::Map::Tube::Bundle - Bundles Map::Tube::* map packages
      • Task::Map::Tube::Metro - Install the Map::Tube concrete metro modules
      • Task::MapMetro::Maps - Map::Metro maps in a bundle
      • Travel::Routing::DE::DBRIS - Interface to the bahn.de itinerary service
      • Travel::Routing::DE::EFA - Unofficial interface to EFA-based itinerary services
      • Travel::Routing::DE::VRR - Unofficial interface to the efa.vrr.de German itinerary service
      • Travel::Status::DE::DBRIS - Interface to bahn.de / bahnhof.de departure monitors
      • Travel::Status::DE::HAFAS - Interface to HAFAS-based online arrival/departure monitors
      • Travel::Status::DE::IRIS - Interface to IRIS based web departure monitors
      • Travel::Status::MOTIS - An interface to the MOTIS routing service
      • Travel::Status::DE::VRR - Unofficial VRR departure monitor

      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
      • App::KBLIUtils - Utilities related to Indonesian KBLI ("Klasifikasi Baku Lapangan Usaha Indonesia" a.k.a. the Indonesian ISIC "International Standard Industrial Classification of All Economic Activities")
      • ArrayData::Lingua::Word::ID::KBBI::ByClass::Adjective - Indonesian adjective words from KBBI (Kamus Besar Bahasa Indonesia)
      • ArrayData::Lingua::Word::ID::KBBI::ByClass::Verb - Indonesian verb words from KBBI (Kamus Besar Bahasa Indonesia)
      • ArrayData::Lingua::Word::ID::KBBI::FigureOfSpeech - Indonesian figure of speech (kiasan) entries from KBBI (Kamus Besar Bahasa Indonesia), 3e
      • ArrayData::Lingua::Word::ID::KBBI::Proverb - Indonesian proverb (peribahasa) entries from KBBI (Kamus Besar Bahasa Indonesia), 3e
      • Notes for ArrayData::Lingua::Word::ID::KBBI::Proverb!
        • Contains the same data as WordList::ID::KBBI::Proverb
      • TableData - Specification for TableData::*, modules that contains table data
      • TableDataBundle::Business::ID::KBLI - Collection of TableData:: modules related to KBLI (Kode Baku Lapangan Usaha, a.k.a. Standard Code of Business Field)
      • TableDataBundle::Lingua::Word::EN::ByClass::Adjective - Collection of TableData:: modules that contain English adjectives
      • TableDataBundle::Lingua::Word::EN::ByClass::Adverb - Collection of TableData:: modules that contain English adverbs
      • TableDataBundle::Lingua::Word::EN::ByClass::Noun - Collection of TableData:: modules that contain English nouns
      • TableDataBundle::Perl::CPAN::Release - Collection of TableData:: modules that contain CPAN releases data
      • TableDataBundle::Perl::Release - Collection of TableData:: modules that contain Perl releases data
      • TableDataBundle::Software::License - Collection of TableData:: modules related to software license
      • TableData::Lingua::Word::EN::Adjective::TalkEnglish - List of words that are used as adjectives only, from talkenglish.com
      • TableData::Lingua::Word::EN::Adverb::TalkEnglish - List of words that are used as adverbs only, from talkenglish.com
      • TableData::Lingua::Word::EN::ByClass::Adverb::TalkEnglish - List of words that are used as adverbs only, from talkenglish.com
      • TableData::Lingua::Word::EN::Noun::TalkEnglish - List of words that are used as nouns only, from talkenglish.com
      • TableData::Locale::US::State - US states
      • TableDataRole::Spec::Basic - Basic interface for all TableData::* modules
      • TableDataRoles::Standard - Standard set of roles for TableData
      • WordList::ID::KBBI::ByClass::Adverb - Indonesian adverb words from Kamus Besar Bahasa Indonesia (KBBI), 3e
      • WordList::ID::KBBI::Proverb - Indonesian proverb (peribahasa) entries from Kamus Besar Bahasa Indonesia (KBBI), 3e
      • Notes for WordList::ID::KBBI::Proverb!
        • Contains the same data as ArrayData::Lingua::Word::ID::KBBI::Proverb
      • WordListBundle::EN::ByCategory - Collection of English words of various categories
      • Notes for WordListBundle::EN::ByCategory!
        • Contains Animal, Bird, Flower, Food, Fruit, Insect, MusicalInstrument, Vegetable, WaterAnimal
      • WordListBundle::EN::ByClass::Adjective - Collection of English adjectives
      • WordListBundle::EN::ByClass::Adverb - Collection of English adverbs
      • WordListBundle::EN::ByClass::Noun - Collection of English nouns
      • WordListBundle::ID::ByCategory - Collection of Indonesian words of various categories
      • Notes for WordListBundle::ID::ByCategory!
        • Contains Animal, Bird, Flower, Food, Fruit, Insect, MusicalInstrument, Vegetable, WaterAnimal
    • TemplateStuff
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • https://mustache.github.io/
      • App::Aphra - Uses Moose and YAML
      • Notes for App::Aphra!
        • Uses Template::Toolkit and Markdown and Pandoc
        • https://aphra.perlhacks.com/
      • App::Dapper - A publishing tool for static websites
      • App::url - Format a URL according to a sprintf-like template
      • App::Wikidata::Template::CS::CitaceMonografie - Base class for Wikidata command line tool wd-citace-monografie
      • BarefootJS - Engine- and framework-agnostic server runtime for BarefootJS marked templates
      • BarefootJS::Backend::Xslate - Text::Xslate (Kolon) rendering backend for BarefootJS
      • Business::LetterWriter - Templates in JSON format
      • Data::JPack - Offline/Online Web Application Packer
      • Notes for Data::JPack!
        • Uses Template::Plexsite below
      • HP::Handy - A tiny Jinja2-compatible template engine for Perl 5.5.3 and later
      • Notes for HP::Handy!
        • See also HTTP::Handy
      • HTML::Blitz - High-performance, selector-based, content-aware HTML template engine
      • Notes for HTML::Blitz!
        • Contains HTML::Blitz::Builder
        • Pod has a discussion of performance
      • HTML::Blitz::Builder - Create HTML code dynamically and safely
      • HTML::Template::Associate - Associate relevant packages with HTML::Template
      • HTML::Template::Nest - Manipulate a nested html template structure via a perl hash
      • HTML::Zoom - Selector based streaming template engine
      • Notes for HTML::Zoom!
        • Can be extremely slow when processing large amounts of data
        • See HTML::Blitz above
      • Jinja2::TT2 - Transpile Jinja2 templates to Template Toolkit 2
      • Mason - Powerful, high-performance templating for the web and beyond
      • Mojo::Template - Perl-ish templates
      • Mustache::Simple - A simple Mustache Renderer
      • Pango - Layout and render international text
      • Notes for Pango!
        • https://developer.gnome.org/pygtk/stable/pango-markup-language.html
      • Plate - Fast templating engine with support for embedded Perl
      • String::Interpolate::Named - Interpolated named arguments in string
      • String::Interpolate::RE - Interpolate variables into strings using regular expressions
      • Templates in general - Template::Liquid
      • Template - Front-end module to the Template Toolkit
      • Notes for Template!
        • See also Template::Tiny, Text::Template::Tiny below
      • Template::AutoFilter - Template::Toolkit with automatic filtering
      • Template::EmbeddedPerl - A template processing engine using embedding Perl code
      • Template::Liquid - A Simple, Stateless Template System
      • Template::Mustache - Drawing Mustaches on Perl for fun and profit
      • Template::Nest - Manipulate a generic template structure via a perl hash
      • Template::Plex - (P)erl (L)exical and (EX)tendable Templating
      • Template::Plexsite - Templating for interlinked templates
      • Template::Plugin::Autoformat - Interface to Text::Autoformat module
      • Template::Plugin::CSV - Plugin for generating CSV
      • Template::Plugin::Latex - Template Toolkit plugin for Latex
      • Template::Plugin::StringTree - Access tree-like groups of strings naturally in code and Templates
      • Template::Provider - Provider module for loading/compiling templates
      • Template::Provider::Pandoc - Pre-process templates with Pandoc
      • Template::Sluz - A minimalistic Perl templating engine with Smarty-like syntax
      • Template::Tiny - Template Toolkit re-implemented in as little code as possible
      • Notes for Template::Tiny!
        • See Template above and Text::Template::Tiny below
      • Text::Caml - Mustache template engine
      • Text::Handlebars - http://handlebarsjs.com/ for Text::Xslate
      • Text::Hogan - A mustache templating engine statement-for-statement cloned from hogan.js
      • Text::Layout - Pango style markup formatting
      • Notes for Text::Layout!
        • This module will co-operate with PDF::API2, PDF::Builder, Cairo, and Pango
      • Text::MustacheTemplate - Fast and specification compliant Mustache template engine for Perl
      • Notes for Text::MustacheTemplate!
        • See also https://mustache.github.io/
      • Text::NeatTemplate - A fast, middleweight template engine
      • Template::Plugin::Number::Format - Plugin/filter interface to Number::Format
      • Text::Template - Expand template text with embedded Perl
      • Text::Template::Tiny - Variable substituting template processor
      • Notes for Text::Template::Tiny!
        • See Template and Template::Tiny above
      • Text::Textile - A humane web text generator
      • Text::Textile2MarcdownStandalone - Standalone converter from Textile markup to Markdown
      • Text::XSlate - Scalable template engine for Perl5
      • Notes for Text::XSlate!
        • Even faster than HTML::Blitz
      • YATT::Lite - Template with "use strict"
    • TestingHelp
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • Test::InDomain within DataValidation
        • test.openssl.pl - Test regexps
        • [Topic]
        • [Topic]
        • For test results for any module
        • https://magpie.cpantesters.org/
        • https://perlmaven.com/devel-cover-for-markua-parser
      • App::CPANTS::Lint - Front-end to Module::CPANTS::Analyse
      • App::local::lib::helper - Make it easy to run code against a local-lib
      • App::Prove - Implements the prove command
      • Notes for App::Prove!
        • Part of Test::Harness (below)
      • App::Prove::Plugin::Metrics - Emit metrics when running prove
      • Notes for App::Prove::Plugin::Metrics!
        • Contains TAP::Harness::Metrics, TAP::Parser::Metrics
      • App::Prove::Plugin::SetEnv - A prove plugin to set environment variables
      • App::ReportPrereqs - Report prerequisite versions
      • App::Run::Command::ToFail - Base class for run-command-to-fail tool
      • App::SimpleScan - simple_scan's core code
      • Notes for App::SimpleScan!
        • Contains simple_scan and simple_report and many examples
        • simple_scan reads test specifications from standard input and generates Perl code based on these specifications
      • App::Test::Generator - Generate fuzz and corpus-driven test harnesses
      • App::Yath::Plugin::Utilization - System-utilization gating for yath test runs
      • App::Yath::Script - Script initialization and utility functions for Test2::Harness
      • Archive::Merged - Virtually merge two archives
      • Notes for Archive::Merged!
        • Meaning you can interrogate one or more archives or directories
      • Assert::Refute - Unified testing and assertion tool
      • assertions - Select assertions in blocks of code
      • Carp::Assert - Executable comments
      • Carp::Assert::More - Convenience assertions for common situations
      • Chicken::Ipsum - Generate random chicken noises
      • circular::require - Detect circularity in use/require statements
      • Notes for circular::require!
        • See also Test::CircularDependencies
      • Config::Model::Tester - Test framework for Config::Model
      • CPAN test results for a typical module - https://matrix.perl-magpie.org/dist.php?dist=Tree-DAG_Node
      • CPAN::Test::Dummy::Perl5::ModuleBuildTiny - Test distribution that depends on Module::Build::Tiny
      • CPAN::Test::Dummy::Perl5::ExtUtilsMakeMaker - Test distribution that depends on ExtUtils::MakeMaker
      • Cucumber::Messages - Library of classes to encapsulate Cucumber messages
      • Cucumber::TagExpressions - Tag expression parser
      • Cucumber::Test::Release::Automation - In lib/Cucumber/Test/Release/Automation.pm
      • Data::Compare - Compare Perl data structures
      • Data::Maker - Simple, flexible and extensible generation of realistic data - Iincludes gender
      • Data::Person - Data object for person - Includes gender
      • Data::Random - Perl module to generate random data
      • Data::RandomPerson - Create random person data - Includes gender
      • Data::Random::Person - Random person objects
      • Notes for Data::Random::Person!
        • Could use Mock::Person::US instead of Mock::Person:CZ
        • Uses Data::Person
        • Includes gender
      • Data::Random::String::Matches - Generate random strings matching a regex
      • Data::Random::Weighted - Get weighted random data
      • Notes for Data::Random::Weighted!
        • Limited to picking 1 item
      • Date::Range - Work with a range of dates
      • Date::Simple - A simple date object
      • Data::SimplePassword - Simple random password generator
      • DateTime::Event::Random - DateTime extension for creating random datetimes
      • DateTime::Event::Recurrence - DateTime::Set extension for create basic recurrence sets
      • DBD::Mock::Session::GenerateFixtures - This distribution generates fixtures for DBD-Mock-Session module
      • DBIx::Class::MockData - Generate mock test data for DBIx::Class schemas
      • Environment::Is - Detect environments like Docker or WSL
      • Fennec - A testers toolbox, and best friend - Deprecated
      • Gherkin - A parser and compiler for the Gherkin language
      • Notes for Gherkin!
        • See Test::BDD::Cucumber below
      • Getopt::Kingpin - Command line options parser (like golang kingpin)
      • HealthCheck - A health check for your code
      • HealthCheck::Parallel - A HealthCheck that uses parallelization for running checks
      • JMAP::Tester - A JMAP client made for testing JMAP servers
      • JMAP::Tester::WebSocket - A WebSocket JMAP client made for testing JMAP servers
      • Keyword::DEVELOPMENT - Have code blocks which don't exist unless you ask for them.
      • Mock::Config - Temporarily set Config or XSConfig values
      • Mock::Data - Extensible toolkit for generating mock data
      • Notes for Mock::Data!
        • Has a dozen subclasses
      • Mock::Data::Regex - Generator that uses a Regex as a template to generate strings
      • Notes for Mock::Data::Regex!
        • Similar to String::Random::Regexp::regxstring in StringStuff
      • Mock::Person - Generates random last, first and middle name of person
      • Mock::Populate - Mock data creation
      • Mockery - Mock objects for testing purposes
      • Module::CPANTS::Analyse - Generate Kwalitee ratings for a distribution
      • Notes for Module::CPANTS::Analyse!
        • See also App::CPANTS::Lint
      • Module::Mask - Pretend certain modules are not installed
      • Noise - Pure Perl implementation of the Noise Protocol Framework
      • Perl::MinimumVersion::Fast - Find a minimum required version of perl for Perl code
      • PerlX::Assert - Yet another assertion keyword
      • Plack::Test - Test PSGI applications with various backends
      • Plack::Test::Agent - OO interface for testing low-level Plack/PSGI apps
      • Random::Day - Class for random day generation
      • Random::Day::InTheFuture - Class for random day generation in the future
      • Random::Day::InThePast - Class for random day generation in the past
      • Random::Set - Class for random set generation
      • Result::Simple - A dead simple perl-ish Result like F#, Rust, Go, etc
      • Notes for Result::Simple!
        • Result represents a function's return value as success or failure, enabling safer error handling and more effective control flow management
      • Scientist - Test new code against old.
      • SimpleMock - A simple mocking framework for Perl
      • Sub::Assert - Subroutine pre- and postconditions, etc
      • Subs::Trace - Trace all calls in a package
      • Syntax::Keyword::Assert - Assert keyword for Perl
      • TAP::DOM - TAP as Document Object Model
      • TAP::Formatter::HTML - TAP Test Harness output delegate for html output
      • TAP::Harness - Prove supports TAP::Harness plugins and has a plugin system of its own
      • Notes for TAP::Harness!
        • Usage: prove -Ilib [-j 9] -r t where t is the dir name
        • See "FORMATTERS" in prove, "SOURCE HANDLERS" in prove and App::Prove for more details
      • Tapper::Cmd - Backend functions for CLI and Web
      • Notes for Tapper::Cmd!
        • Search MetaCPAN for Tapper:: to find other components of this set
      • Task::Perl::Critic - Install everything Perl::Critic
      • Testcontainers - Makes it simple to create and clean up container-based dependencies
      • Test::Against::Commit - Test CPAN modules against Perl dev releases
      • Test::Against::Dev - Test CPAN modules against Perl dev releases
      • Test::BDD::Cucumber - Feature-complete Cucumber-style testing in Perl
      • Notes for Test::BDD::Cucumber!
        • See also Gherkin above
      • Test::Builder - Back-end for building test libraries
      • Test::Changes::Strict::Simple - Strict semantic validation for CPAN Changes files
      • Test::CheckDeps - Check for presence of dependencies
      • Test::CheckGitStatus - Check git status after every test
      • Test::CircularDependencies - Make sure non of the modules depend on themselves
      • Test::Class - Easily create test classes in an xUnit/JUnit style
      • Test::Class::Moose - Serious testing for serious Perl
      • Test::CleanNamespaces - Check for uncleaned imports
      • Test::Compile - Assert that your Perl files compile OK
      • Test::CPAN::Changes - Validation of the Changes file in a CPAN distribution
      • Test::CPAN::Changes::ReallyStrict - Ensure a Changes file looks exactly like it would if it was machine generated
      • Test::CPAN::Health - Analyse a CPAN distribution and produce a comprehensive health report
      • Test::CPAN::Meta - Validate your CPAN META.yml files
      • Test::CPAN::Meta::JSON - Validate a META.json file within a CPAN distribution
      • Test::CPANfile - See if cpanfile lists every used modules
      • Test::CVE - Test against known CVE's
      • Test::Data - Provides utility functions to check properties and values of data and variables
      • Notes for Test::Data!
        • See also Mock::Data above
      • Test::Database::Temp - Create temporary test databases and run tests in all available ones
      • Notes for Test::Database::Temp!
        • Database::Temp is also in DatabaseAndSQL1
      • Test::Deep - Extremely flexible deep comparison
      • Notes for Test::Deep!
        • Has over 40 sub-modules
      • Test::Deep::Hashbag - A Test::Deep hash comparator ignoring hash keys
      • Test::Dependencies - Ensure that the dependency listing is complete
      • Test::DescribeMe - Tell test runners what kind of test you are
      • Test::Differences - Test strings and data structures and show differences if not ok
      • Test::DistManifest - Author test that validates a package MANIFEST
      • Test::Env - Test the environment
      • Test::Exception - Test exception-based code
      • Notes for Test::Exception!
        • See also Test::Fatal
      • Test::Expander - Expansion of test functionalities that appear to be frequently used while testing
      • Test::ExpectAndCheck - Expect/check-style unit testing with object methods
      • Test::Fatal - Incredibly simple helpers for testing code with exceptions
      • Notes for Test::Fatal!
        • See also Test::Exception
      • Test::File - Test file attributes
      • Test::File::Contents - Test routines for examining the contents of files
      • Test::File::Cmp - Test routine for file comparison independent of CRLF or LF line endings.
      • Test::File::ShareDir - Create a Fake ShareDir for your modules for testing
      • Test::File::ShareDir::Dist - Simplified dist oriented ShareDir tester
      • Test::Fixme - Check code for FIXMEs
      • Test::Future::IO::Impl - Acceptance tests for Future::IO implementations
      • Test::Harness - Run Perl standard test scripts with statistics
      • Test::HTML::Content - Perl extension for testing HTML output
      • Test::HTTP::LocalServer - Spawn a local HTTP server for testing
      • Test::HTTP::Scenario - Deterministic record/replay of HTTP interactions for test suites
      • Test::HTTPStatus - Check an HTTP status
      • Test::Inline - Embed your tests in your code, next to what is being tested
      • Test::Instruction - A test framework
      • Test::Inter - Framework for more readable interactive test scripts
      • Test::Is - Skip test in a declarative way, following the Lancaster Consensus
      • Test::ISBN - Check International Standard Book Numbers
      • Test::IsAny - Check if a value is any of the given values
      • Test::JSON::Schema - Test your data against a JSON Schema
      • Test::JSON::Schema::Acceptance - Acceptance testing for JSON-Schema based validators
      • Test::Kit - Build custom test packages with only the features you want
      • Notes for Test::Kit!
        • Similar modules: Import::Base, Toolset
        • Test::Kit largely differs from these in that it always makes your module behave like Test::More
      • Test::Kwalitee - Test the Kwalitee of a distribution before you release it
      • Test::Kwalitee::Extra - Run Kwalitee tests including optional indicators, especially, prereq_matches_use
      • Test::Load::Helper - Automatically load test helpers by walking the directory tree
      • Test::Manifest - Interact with a t/test_manifest file
      • Test::Memcached - Memcached Runner For Tests
      • Test::MinimumVersion - Does your code require newer perl than you think?
      • Test::MinimumVersion::Fast - Does your code require newer perl than you think?
      • Test::MixedScripts - Test text for mixed and potentially confusable Unicode scripts
      • Notes for Test::MixedScripts!
        • See also https://util.unicode.org/UnicodeJsps/confusables.jsp
      • Test::Mock::Guard - Simple mock test library using RAII
      • Test::MockFile - Allows tests to validate code that can interact with files without touching the file system
      • Test::Mockingbird - Advanced mocking library for Perl with support for dependency injection and spies
      • Test::MockModule - Override subroutines in a module for unit testing
      • Test::MockTime - Replaces actual time with simulated time
      • Test::MockTime::HiRes - Replaces actual time with simulated high resolution time
      • Test::Mojibake - Check your source for encoding misbehavior
      • Test::More - See Test::Simple or Test::Simpler
      • Test::Most::Explain - Enhanced diagnostics for Test::More with structured diffs and helpful hints
      • Test::mysqld - mysqld runner for tests
      • Test::Needs - Skip tests when modules not available
      • Test::Nginx - Data-driven test scaffold for Nginx C module and Nginx/OpenResty-based libraries and applications
      • Test::NoBreakpoints - Test that files do not contain soft breakpoints
      • Test::NoTabs - Check the presence of tabs in your project
      • Test::NoWarnings - Make sure you didn't emit any warnings while testing
      • Test::Output - Utilities to test STDOUT and STDERR messages
      • Test::Perl::Critic - Use Perl::Critic in test programs
      • Test::Perl::Critic::Progressive - Gradually enforce coding standards
      • Test::PerlTidy - Check that all your files are tidy
      • Test::Pod - Check for POD errors in files
      • Test::Pod::Coverage - Check for pod coverage in your distribution
      • Test::Pod::LinkCheck - Tests POD for invalid links
      • Test::Pod::LinkCheck::Lite - Test POD links
      • Test::Pod::Links - Test Pod for invalid HTTP/S links
      • Test::Pod::No404s - Using this test module will check your POD for any http 404 links
      • Test::Pod::Spelling - A Test library to spell-check POD filesTest::Pod::Spelling - A Test library to spell-check POD files
      • Notes for Test::Pod::Spelling!
        • See also Pod::Spelling, Test::Spelling
      • Test::Pod::Spelling::CommonMistakes - Checks POD for common spelling mistakes
      • Test::Portability::Files - Check file names portability
      • Test::PostgreSQL - PostgreSQL runner for tests
      • Test::PostgreSQL::v2 - A modern, isolated PostgreSQL executable runner for tests
      • Test::Prereq - Check if Makefile.PL has the right prerequisites
      • Notes for Test::Prereq!
        • Supersedes the next module
      • Test::Prereq::Meta - Test distribution prerequisites against CPAN meta data file
      • Notes for Test::Prereq::Meta!
        • But see Test::Prereq
      • Test::QuickGen - Utilities for generating random test data
      • Test::RedisServer - Redis-server runner for tests
      • Test::Regexp - Test your regular expressions
      • Test::Regexp::Pattern - Test Regexp::Pattern patterns
      • Test::Reporter - Sends test results to cpan-testers@perl.org
      • Test::Reporter::Transport - Base class for Test::Reporter transports
      • Test::Reporter::Transport::Metabase - Metabase transport for Test::Reporter
      • Test::Requires - Checks to see if the module can be loaded
      • Test::Returns - Verify that a method's output agrees with its specification
      • Test::Routine - Composable units of assertion
      • Test::Script - Basic cross-platform tests for scripts
      • Test::Settings - Ask or tell when certain types of tests should be run
      • Test::SFTP - An object to help test SFTPs
      • Test::Signature - Automated SIGNATURE testing
      • Notes for Test::Signature!
        • See also Module::Signature in ModuleBuilding
      • Test::Simple - Basic utilities for writing tests
      • Notes for Test::Simple!
        • Has a huge array of components
        • Contains Test::More and Test2
        • But see the next module
      • Test::Simpler - Simpler than Test::Simple; more powerful than Test::More
      • Test::Smoke - The Perl core test smoke suite
      • Test::SPDX::Coverage - Perl Test Harness to verify all matched files in Manifest have a SPDX-License-Identifier
      • Test::Spelling - Check for spelling errors in POD files
      • Notes for Test::Spelling!
        • See also Test::Pod::Spelling
      • Test::Spelling::Stopwords - POD spell-checking with project-specific stopwords
      • Test::SQLite - SQLite setup/teardown for tests
      • Test::Strict - Check syntax, presence of use strict; and test coverage
      • Test::Subunit::V2 - Subunit version 2 binary protocol support
      • Notes for Test::Subunit::V2!
        • Contains Test::Subunit, Test::Subunit::Diff
      • Test::TCP - Testing TCP program
      • Test::TempDir::Tiny - Temporary directories that stick around when tests fail
      • Notes for Test::TempDir::Tiny!
        • Strongly recommended over File::Temp which uses global "our" variables, which affects Capture::Tiny, since the latter also uses File::Temp. Don't do that
      • Test::TrailingSpace - Test for trailing space in source files
      • Test::Trap - Trap exit codes, exceptions, output, etc
      • Test::Unit - The PerlUnit testing framework
      • Test::URI - Check Uniform Resource Identifiers
      • Test::utf8 - Handy utf8 tests
      • Test::ValkeyServer - Valkey-server runner for tests
      • Test::Version - Check to see that version's in modules are sane
      • Test::Warn - Perl extension to test methods for warnings
      • Test::Warnings - Test for warnings and the lack of them
      • Test::Which - Skip tests if external programs are missing from PATH (with version checks)
      • Test::Without::Module - Test fallback behaviour in absence of modules
      • Notes for Test::Without::Module!
        • See also Devel::Hide
      • Test::WWW::Mechanize - Testing-specific WWW::Mechanize subclass
      • Notes for Test::WWW::Mechanize!
        • There are many similar modules on MetaCPAN
        • https://metacpan.org/search?size=20&q=Test::WWW::Mechanize
      • Test::WWW::Simple - Test Web applications using TAP
      • Test::XML - Compare XML in perl tests
      • Test::XML::Easy - Test XML with XML::Easy
      • XML::SemanticDiff - Perl extension for comparing XML documents.
      • Test::XML::Simple - Easy testing for XML
      • Test::YAFT - Yet another testing framework
      • T2 - Define the T2 namespace that can always be used to access functionality from a Test2 bundle such as Test2::V1
      • Test2::API - Primary interface for writing Test2 based testing tools
      • Test2::Bundle::Numerical - Numerical Quadmath/longdouble aware Test2 bundle
      • Test2::Harness - Contains yath, which replaces test and prove (see below for usage of prove)
      • Notes for Test2::Harness!
        • https://perladvent.org/2023/2023-12-23.html
      • Test2::Harness2::ChildSubReaper - Tiny XS wrapper around Linux's PR_SET_CHILD_SUBREAPER prctl.
      • Test2::Plugin::MemUsage - Collect and display memory usage information
      • Test2::Plugin::SubtestFilter - Filter subtests by name
      • Test2::Plugin::UUID - Use REAL UUIDs in Test2
      • Test2::Require::ProgramInPath - Skip test unless a program exists in the PATH
      • Test2::Suite - Distribution with a rich set of tools built upon the Test2 framework
      • Test2::Tools - Documentation for Tools
      • Notes for Test2::Tools!
      • Test2::Tools::ComboObject - Combine checks and diagnostics into a single test as an object
      • Test2::Tools::DOM - Tools to test HTML/XML-based DOM representations
      • Test2::Tools::LoadModule - Test whether a module can be successfully loaded
      • Test2::Tools::PerlTidy - Test2 check that all of your Perl files are tidy
      • Test2::Tools::Pod - Check that POD syntax is valid
      • Test2::Tools::Spec - RSPEC implementation on top of Test2::Workflow
      • Test2::Tools::Subtest - Tools for writing subtests
      • Test2::Tools::TypeTiny - Test2 tools for checking Type::Tiny types
      • Test2::Tools::URL - Compare a URL in your Test2 test
      • Test2::Transition - Transition notes when upgrading to Test2
      • Notes for Test2::Transition!
        • See also: https://metacpan.org/pod/Test2::Suite
      • Test2::Util::DistFiles - Gather files in a distribution
      • Test2::Util::Guard - Inline copy of Scope::Guard
      • Test2::V1 - V1 edition of the Test2 recommended bundle
      • Test2::Workflow - A test workflow is a way of structuring tests using composable units
      • Time::RTM - Run-time metrics stats
      • UNIVERSAL::dump - Add dump and other methods to all classes and objects
    • TextProcessing
      • See also!

        • [Topic]
        • https://fontawesome.com/- For a huge list of icons
        • For the homepage of fzf. See also fd
        • OpenRefine is a powerful free, open source tool for working with messy data
        • A universal document converter
        • For fd aka fd-find. See also fzf
        • Not Perl but can use reStructuredText
      • Aion::Format - Perl extension for formatting numbers, coloring output, etc
      • Algorithm::Diff - Compute differences between two files / lists
      • Algorithm::Diff::XS - Algorithm::Diff with XS core loop
      • Algorithm::LCS::XS - Fast (XS) implementation of the Longest Common Subsequence (LCS) Algorithm
      • Algorithm::MLCS - Fast heuristic algorithm for finding Longest Common Subsequence of multiple sequences
      • Antsy - Streaming ANSI escape sequences
      • App::AlgorithmDiffUtils - CLI utilities based on Algorithm::Diff
      • App::FzfUtils - fzf is an interactive filter program for any kind of list
      • App::optex::textconv - Optex module to replace document file by its text contents
      • App::PPI::Dumper - Use the PPI to dump the structure of a Perl file
      • App::s2p - A stream editor
      • App::TextTemplatePermuteUtils - CLI utilities related to Text::Template::Permute
      • Ascii::Text - Module for generating ASCII text in various fonts and styles
      • Bible::OBML - Open Bible Markup Language parser and renderer
      • Bible::OBML::Gateway - Bible Gateway content conversion to Open Bible Markup Language
      • Bible::Reference - Simple Bible reference parser, tester, and canonicalizer
      • Data::Text - Class to handle text in an OO way
      • Data::Text::Simple - Data object for text in language
      • File::MultipleDiff - Compare multiple files
      • Hash::Diff - Return difference between two hashes as a hash
      • HTML::Differences - Reasonable sane HTML diffing
      • HTML::Quoted - Extract structure of quoted HTML mail message
      • Notes for HTML::Quoted!
        • Various MUAs use quite different approaches for quoting in emails
        • Purpose and returned structures are very similar to those in Text::Quoted
        • Uses HTML::Parser
      • Indent - Class for indent handling
      • Notes for Indent!
        • See also Tags::Output::Indent
      • Indent::Block - Class for block indenting
      • Indent::Data - Class for data indenting
      • Indent::String - Class for text indenting
      • Indent::Word - Class for word indenting
      • Intellexer - API client for Intellexer
      • Notes for Intellexer!
        • Perl API client for the Intellexer, a webservice that, "enables developers to embed Intellexer semantics products using XML or JSON."
      • LCS - Longest Common Subsequence
      • Notes for LCS!
        • Contains LCS::Tiny
      • LCS::Similar - Allow differences in the compared elements of Longest Common Subsequence (LCS) Algorithm
      • LCS::XS - Fast (XS) implementation of the Longest Common Subsequence (LCS) Algorithm
      • Lingua::TermWeight - Language-independent TermWeight calculator
      • Lingua::Thesaurus - Thesaurus management
      • Pandoc - Wrapper for the mighty Pandoc document converter
      • Parse::RecDescent - Generate Recursive-Descent Parsers
      • Perl::MinimumVersion - Find a minimum required version of perl for Perl code
      • PPI - Parse, Analyze and Manipulate Perl (without perl)
      • PPI::Statement::Variable - Variable declaration statements
      • PPIx::Regexp - Represent a regular expression of some sort
      • PPIx::Utilities - Extensions to PPI.
      • PPIx::Utils - Utility functions for PPI
      • PPR - Pattern-based Perl Recognizer
      • Notes for PPR!
        • The docs compare it with PPI
      • Quote::Code - Quoted strings with arbitrary code interpolation
      • Regexp::Grammars - Add grammatical parsing features to Perl 5.10 regexes
      • String::Diff - Simple diff to String
      • Notes for String::Diff!
        • Various ways of listing the difference between 2 strings
      • String::Escape - Backslash escapes, quoted phrase, word elision, etc
      • String::Glob::Permute - Expand {foo,bar,baz}[2-4] style string globs
      • Tags - Structure oriented SGML/XML/HTML/etc. elements manipulation
      • Tags::Output::Indent - Indent class for Tags
      • Notes for Tags::Output::Indent!
        • See also Indent
      • Tags::Output::LibXML - Printing 'Tags' structure by LibXML library
      • Term::ANSIScreen - Terminal control using ANSI escape sequences
      • Text::Amuse - Generate HTML and LaTeX documents from Emacs Muse markup
      • Text::Amuse::Compile - Compiler for Text::Amuse
      • Text::ANSI::Fold - Text folding library supporting ANSI terminal sequence and Asian wide characters with prohibition character handling
      • Text::ANSI::Printf - Printf function to print string including ANSI sequence
      • Text::ANSI::Util - Routines for text containing ANSI color codes
      • Text::Autoformat - Automatic text wrapping and reformatting
      • Text::Balanced - Extract delimited text sequences from strings
      • Text::Conceal - Conceal and recover interface for text processing
      • Text::Diff - Perform diffs on files and record sets
      • Text::Diff::FormattedHTML - Generate a colorful HTML diff of strings/files
      • Text::Diff::HTML - XHTML format for Text::Diff::Unified
      • Text::Diff::Parser - Parse patch files containing unified and standard diffs
      • Text::Diff::Unified::XS - The fast Text::Diff module
      • Text::DiffU - Diff two sequences and print unified-style output
      • Text::Diff3 - Three way text comparison and merging
      • Text::Diff3::Text - Line number scheme free text buffer
      • Text::FindIndent - Heuristically determine the indent style
      • Text::Fragment - Manipulate fragments in text
      • Text::Glob - Match globbing patterns against text
      • Text::Lorem - Generate random Latin looking text
      • Text::Glob::Expand - Permute and expand glob-like text patterns
      • Text::Minify::XS - Remove indentation and trailing whitespace from multi-line text
      • Text::OutputFilter - Enable post processing of output without fork
      • Text::ParseWords - Parse text into an array of tokens or array of arrays
      • Text::PrettyTable - Allow for auto-fixed-width formatting of raw data
      • Text::Quoted - Extract the structure of a quoted mail message
      • Notes for Text::Quoted!
        • Uses Text::Autoformat
        • Uses Text::Tabs
      • Text::Stencil - Fast XS list/table renderer with escaping, formatting, and transform chaining
      • Text::Tabs - Expand and unexpand tabs like unix expand(1) and unexpand(1)
      • Text::Template::Permute - Template for generating permutation of text
      • Text::Unpack::Auto - Automatically generate unpack strings from fixed-width text
      • Text::vFile::asData - Parse vFile formatted files into data structures
      • Notes for Text::vFile::asData!
        • Reads vFile format files, such as vCard (RFC 2426) and vCalendar (RFC 2445)
        • An app using it! See https://metacpan.org/pod/App::MergeCal
        • Command line program that merges iCal files into a single calendar
        • See TheRFCGuide for rfc2426, rfc2445
      • Text::VisualPrintf - Printf family functions to handle Non-ASCII characters
      • Tree::Lexicon - Object class for storing and retrieving a lexicon in a tree of affixes
      • Treex::PML - Perl implementation for the Prague Markup Language (PML).
      • Treex::Unilang - Collection of blocks parametrized by language and language independent
      • Notes for Treex::Unilang!
        • Includes Czech
      • Web::Microformats2 - Read Microformats2 metadata from HTML or JSON
      • XML::Diff - XML DOM-Tree based Diff & Patch Module
      • YAML::Diff - Diff 2 YAML Documents Semantically

      • Notes for App::genpw!
        • Contains script genpw similar to Linux's genpw
      • Auth::GoogleAuth - Google Authenticator TBOT Abstraction
      • Authen::TacacsPlus - Perl extension for authentication using tacacs+ server
      • Authen::TOTP - Interface to RFC6238 two factor authentication (2FA)
      • Notes for Authen::TOTP!
        • See also Trog::TOTP
      • Concierge - Service layer orchestrator for authentication, sessions, and user data
      • Concierge::Auth - Password authentication and token generation using Crypt::Passphrase
      • Concierge::Sessions - Session manager with factory pattern and multiple backend support
      • Concierge::Users - User data management with multiple storage backends
      • Config::Identity - Load (and optionally decrypt via GnuPG) user/pass identity information
      • Notes for Config::Identity!
        • Uses GnuPG as gpg
        • Use with gpg-agent
        • For GitHub API access, an identity is a login/token pair
        • For PAUSE access, an identity is a user/password pair
      • Data::Password::Common - Check a password against a list of common passwords
      • Data::Password::Manager - Generate, check, manage crypt - des passwords
      • DBIx::Array::Connect - Database Connections from an INI Configuration File
      • DBIx::MyPassword - Store database authentication info in a CSV file
      • DBIx::Password - Allows you to create a global password file for DB password
      • DBIx::PasswordIniFile - Manages DBI connections with password and other params stored in a .ini style file
      • File::KDBX - Encrypted database to store secret text and files
      • Notes for File::KDBX!
        • Works like KeePassXC (https://github.com/keepassxreboot/keepassxc)
      • GitHub::Config::SSH::UserData - Read user data from comments in ssh config file
      • KeePass::Reader - Interface to KeePass V4 database files
      • Notes for KeePass::Reader!
        • See also File::KDBX above
      • Module::Features::PasswordManager - Password manager is a tool that lets you input, store, and retrieve individual passwords securely
      • MooX::Role::CryptedPassword - Password attribute from a encrypted file
      • Net::SAML2 - SAML2 bindings and protocol implementation
      • Pass::OTP - Perl implementation of HOTP / TOTP algorithms
      • Password::Policy - Make managing multiple password strength profiles easy
      • String::Mask - Mask sensitive data
      • String::Mask::XS - Mask sensitive data faster
      • String::Secret - Secret string wrapper to mask secret from logger
      • Tags::HTML::ChangePassword - Tags helper for change password
      • Tags::HTML::Login::Access - Tags helper for login access
      • Tags::HTML::Login::Register - Tags helper for login register
      • Notes for Tags::HTML::Login::Register!
        • Helps generate code for CSS and HTML Login form

      • Mo::utils::UDC - Mo utilities for Universal Decimal Classification

      • perlport - Writing portable Perl

      • Notes for perlwhich!
        • But see App::PMUtils

      • Search::Query - Polyglot query parsing
      • Search::Query::Parser - Convert query strings into query objects

    • UUID
      • See also!
        • [Topic]
        • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apophis
        • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Horus
        • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sekhmet
        • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Universally_unique_identifier
        • http://e2fsprogs.sourceforge.net/
        • https://github.com/ulid/spec
      • Apophis - Content-addressable storage with deterministic UUID v5 identifiers
      • Data::GUID - Globally unique identifiers
      • Data::GUID::Any - Generic interface for GUID/UUID creation
      • Data::KSUID - K-Sortable Unique IDentifiers
      • Notes for Data::KSUID!
        • https://github.com/segmentio/ksuid
      • Data::ULID - Universally Unique Lexicographically Sortable Identifier
      • Notes for Data::ULID!
        • See also ULID::Tiny below
      • Data::UUID - Globally/Universally Unique Identifiers (GUIDs/UUIDs)
      • Data::UUID::LibUUID - uuid.h based UUID generation (versions 2 and 4 depending on platform)
      • Data::UUID::MT - Fast random UUID generator using the Mersenne Twister algorithm
      • Horus - Ultra-fast XS UUID/GUID generator supporting all RFC 9562 versions
      • Sekhmet - Ultra-fast XS ULID generator built on Horus
      • ULID::Tiny - A lightweight ULID (Universally Unique Lexicographically Sortable Identifier) generator
      • Notes for ULID::Tiny!
        • See also Data::ULID above
      • UUID - DCE compatible Universally Unique Identifier library for Perl
      • UUID::Random - Generate random uuid strings
      • UUID::Random::Patch::UseMRS - Make UUID::Random use Math::Random::Secure's rand()
      • UUID::Random::Secure - Like UUID::Random, but uses Math::Random::Secure for random numbers
      • UUID::Tiny - Pure Perl UUID Support With Functional Interface
      • Notes for UUID::Tiny!
        • Warning: Low quality. But see UUID::Tiny::Patch::UseMRS below
      • UUID::Tiny::Patch::UseMRS - Make UUID::Tiny use Math::Random::Secure's rand()
      • UUID::URandom - UUIDs based on /dev/urandom or the Windows Crypto API
      • UUID4::Tiny - Cryptographically secure v4 UUIDs for Linux x64
      • Notes for UUID4::Tiny!
        • https://github.com/ulid/spec

      • XML::Grove::Builder - PerlSAX handler for building an XML::Grove
      • XML::Handler::Trees - PerlSAX handlers for building tree structures
      • XML::Hash::XS - Simple and fast hash to XML and XML to hash conversion written in C
      • XML::IETF - An interface to the IETF XML Registry.
      • XML::LibXML - Perl Binding for libxml2
      • Notes for XML::LibXML!
        • http://grantm.github.io/perl-libxml-by-example/
        • Supports parsing huge documents
        • Supports parsing (possibly faulty) HTML
      • XML::LibXSLT - Interface to the GNOME libxslt library
      • XML::Loy - Extensible XML Reader and Writer
      • XML::MyXML - A simple-to-use XML module, for parsing and creating XML documents
      • XML::Parser - A perl module for parsing XML documents
      • Notes for XML::Parser!
        • Includes XML::Parser::Expat
      • XML::Parser::EasyTree - Easier tree style for XML::Parser
      • XML::PP - A simple XML parser
      • Notes for XML::PP!
        • Prefer XML::Simple or XML::LibXML
      • XML::PugiXML - Perl binding for pugixml C++ XML parser
      • XML::Sablotron - A Perl interface to the Sablotron XSLT processor
      • XML::SAX::Simple - XML::SAX::Simple - SAX version of XML::Simple
      • XML::Saxon::XSLT2 - Process XSLT 2.0 using Saxon 9.x
      • Notes for XML::Saxon::XSLT2!
        • http://saxon.sourceforge.net/
      • XML::Sig - A toolkit to help sign and verify XML Digital Signatures
      • XML::Sig::OO - Modern XML Signatured validation
      • XML::Similarity - Calculate the structural similarity between two XML documents
      • XML::Simple - An API for simple XML files - Deprecated
      • XML::Smart - A smart, easy and powerful way to access or create XML from fiels, data and URLs
      • XML::TokeParser - Simplified interface to XML::Parser
      • XML::TreeBuilder - Parser that builds a tree of XML::Element objects
      • Notes for XML::TreeBuilder!
        • Contains XML::Element
      • XML::TreePP - Pure Perl implementation for parsing/writing XML documents
      • XML::Trivial - The trivial tool representing parsed XML as tree of read only objects
      • XML::Twig - A Perl module for processing huge XML documents in tree mode
      • XML::XPath::XMLParser - The default XML parsing class that produces a node tree
